title: H1: Parallel bench+fuzz+microbench harness (full CPU ~80%)
description: Build standalone multi-threaded harness linking libwirehair: (a) gf_microbench for gf256_add/mul/muladd_mem GBPS per-core and aggregate; (b) wh_bench end-to-end encoder_create/encode/decode/recover MBPS + overhead across N grid; (c) wh_fuzz correctness fuzzer (random N, blockBytes, loss patterns, seeds) verifying exact round-trip recovery. Threads target ~80% of 128 HW threads. This is the measurement substrate for all ablations.
close_reason: Closed

title: Reconcile handoff exact V2 packet solver with certified decoder
description: Selectively integrate the exact joint packet/precode solver from handoff commit 90fed75 while preserving the current production-row contract, transactional OOM retry, size-overflow guards, K=64000 schedules, and facade lifecycle coverage. Exclude the mixed table-regeneration/JWG files and passive Beads export.
acceptance_criteria: Use one unambiguous packet contract version (bump after reconciliation); import exact solver/profile binding and solve/roundtrip/seed tests; preserve duplicate retry after transient OOM, allocation/no-write/overflow tests, production degree cap, and K=64000 recovery-only test; add compare --precode, precodefail, and precodecheck coverage; pass fresh strict build/full CTest, ASan+UBSan focused/full feasible gates, and CI Python tests.
notes: 2026-07-10 integration/validation: selectively imported exact handoff solver/profile files and excluded all JWG/table/top-level CI paths. Reconciled one packet contract v4, exact mix3 production rows, complete profile binding, deterministic seed attempts, GF256 residual/heavy solve, conflicting duplicate/corruption handling, transient OOM duplicate retry, 4096 residual memory cap, 32-bit size guards, direct GF initialization, independent packet and tiny dense-Gaussian oracles, and failure timing. Fresh strict GCC build passed 26/26 full CTests; scheduled 5/5 including both K64000 modes; full ASan+UBSan passed 25/25; CI Python 17/17 and table Python 5/5. Real compare --precode grids passed with zero errors across N up to 64000 and block bytes 17/1280/102400/1048576. precodefail 100-trial grids at K1000/10000/64000, bb1/1280, OH0/OH1 had zero errors and exercised real rank-failure reporting.
close_reason: Selective handoff reconciliation complete: exact joint solver/profile binding imported without JWG paths, packet contract unified at v4, current OOM/overflow/K64 regressions preserved, and compare/precodefail/precodecheck added. Fresh strict static/shared matrices, full/scheduled CTest, ASan+UBSan, TSan, Python/native/package E2E, 100-trial real failure grids, and repeated code audits all pass.

title: Prevent Python wrapper construction and conversion double-free
description: The ctypes wrapper owns a native codec before Python object construction is complete. If Encoder/Decoder initialization raises after _Codec.__init__, or closes/detaches/replaces its finalizer before raising, the exception cleanup can double-free or leak. Decoder.become_encoder has the same bug after native conversion: a failed Encoder wrapper may free the handle while Decoder still appears to own it.
acceptance_criteria: Use one explicit idempotent native-owner guard shared across construction and conversion; guarantee exactly one free for failures before/after base initialization, close-then-raise, detached/replaced-finalizer cases, native creation failures, normal close/GC, and successful conversion. Add focused unit coverage and run the real installed native Python E2E. Complete an independent clean bug-review pass.
notes:
Implemented one _NativeCodecOwner guard whose release operation clears its pointer before freeing, so fresh create wrappers and decoder-to-encoder transfers share a single idempotent native owner. Wrapper finalizers are installed only after subclass construction succeeds; failure cleanup no longer trusts mutable wrapper _finalizer state. Successful conversion adopts the shared owner before the decoder relinquishes it. Focused FakeLibrary coverage proves exactly one free for Encoder and Decoder __new__ failures; __init__ failures before and after base initialization; close-then-raise; detached or replaced _finalizer; native create/conversion failures; normal explicit/double close and GC finalization; successful conversion and closed-source behavior. The wrapper suite passes 14/14 and the complete Python aggregate passes 60/60. A clean Clang 18 shared install to /tmp/wirehair-owner-env-review-prefix followed by ci/python_native_test.py passed the real 4099-byte, 37-block installed-library loss/reorder/full recovery/per-block recovery/conversion and negative E2E. Independent final source review and git diff --check found no remaining ownership defect.
Post-closure native export CI additions raise the final repository Python aggregate to 66/66; the ownership-focused wrapper suite remains 14/14 and its closure evidence is unchanged.
close_reason: Single shared native-owner guard now guarantees exactly-once cleanup across construction and conversion failure/success paths; 14/14 focused tests, 60/60 aggregate Python tests, fresh installed native E2E, strict builds, and independent clean source review pass.

title: Fix heavy-row full-rank elimination after pivot swaps
description: tables/HeavyRowGenerator.cpp IsFullRank() swaps a discovered pivot at j into pivot slot i, but eliminates only slots k=j+1..end. When j>i this skips unused rows in slots i+1..j, so the rank oracle can misclassify matrices and invalidate the documented deterministic singular-rate evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Eliminate every remaining unused pivot row after each swap using a correct, reviewable invariant; add a deterministic regression fixture that fails under the old j+1 bound and an independent rank oracle comparison across generated matrices; recompute the shipped-seed 4096-trial empirical count and update documentation/tests if it changes; run normal plus ASan/UBSan generator tests and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Final review disproved the suspected defect. Pivot search selects the first nonzero slot j, so slots i..j-1 are zero in the pivot column; after swapping, the old slot i moved to j is likewise zero. Therefore eliminating only j+1..end is sufficient. An independent physical-row oracle and brute-force comparison across 200,000 binary 6x6 matrices agreed with the existing algorithm; the shipped-seed deterministic rate remains 14/4096. No production change retained.
close_reason: Invalid finding disproved by invariant analysis and independent brute-force oracle

title: Reject overflowing row-operation trace replay dimensions
description: experiments/tiling/rowop_tiling.cpp accepts inferred trace block counts and --block-bytes, then multiplies RecoveryBlocks/InputBlocks by BlockBytes in size_t without checked arithmetic. Malformed or hostile traces can wrap allocation sizes and replay out of bounds; oversized non-wrapping allocations can throw uncaught.
acceptance_criteria: Validate trace-derived block domains and every allocation-size product before allocation or output, report a stable nonzero diagnostic for overflow/unsupported memory, preserve valid trace behavior and metrics, add malformed/overflow E2E fixtures that prove no partial result is emitted, and run normal plus ASan/UBSan replay tests with a repeated bug-review pass.
notes:
Implemented checked trace recovery/input size products, aggregate replay-size arithmetic, vector max_size checks, and bad_alloc/length_error containment before replay output. The default --max-memory-mib policy is now a conservative 256 MiB aggregate budget: it includes actual operation-vector capacity, timing samples, input, and base/untiled/tiled recovery copies, plus a 1 MiB allowance for the bounded parser, ifstream buffer, and allocator metadata. Operation-vector growth is checked including old+new transient capacity. Added a one-million operation ceiling with lower --max-operations support, a 4096-byte line ceiling, exact CSV-header validation, and checked ByteLedger accumulation that also keeps total traffic representable. E2E fixtures cover size_t wrap, non-wrapping aggregate over-budget storage, operation-vector budget, missing header, overlong line, and operation-cap rejection; every rejection asserts nonzero exit, stable stderr, and empty stdout. Verification: git diff --check; GCC warning-clean build; bash experiments/test_byte_metrics.sh passed with persisted real-trace checksum 0x8c08d158d46a7cbe and historical timing-ratio oracle; final ASan+UBSan build passed the persisted 4,125-op trace and all rejection categories with no sanitizer findings. Repeated review confirmed ref.Block*BlockBytes replay offsets are bounded by validated allocations and the budget covers the true verification peak of schedule capacity + 3*recovery + input.
Independent final review found and fixed three additional correctness gaps: bounds-valid overlapping operands could violate memcpy/GF restrict contracts; median copied an unbudgeted timing vector; and timing buffers could throw after the CSV header. The loader now rejects unsupported destination/source and two-source overlap before allocation/output while preserving disjoint/adjacent ranges and exact in-place division, checks strtol ERANGE, and preallocates/reuses recovery and timing buffers before output. Final evidence: git diff --check; ShellCheck clean; strict GCC warning-clean builds; bash experiments/test_byte_metrics.sh passed (7 success paths, 11 exact negative paths, persisted 4,125-op checksum 0x8c08d158d46a7cbe, timing ratio 0.593 vs historical 0.597); ASan+UBSan focused suite 11/11 (2 success, 9 exact rejection) with zero sanitizer findings; forced allocation-failure E2E returned 1 with stable diagnostic and zero stdout bytes. Final repeated source review found no remaining aoz defects; acceptance is satisfied and the bead can close.
close_reason: Trace replay dimensions, parsing, aliasing, aggregate memory, and pre-output allocations are bounded and independently verified by normal plus ASan/UBSan E2E

title: Fail random-loss unit tests on terminal decoder exhaustion
description:
## Problem
The primary random-loss correctness test treats Wirehair_ExtraInsufficient as a passing outcome and skips byte recovery, allowing a terminal decoder failure or regression to produce a green CTest run.

## Evidence
test/UnitTest.cpp:588-600 logs decoder exhaustion, cleans up, and returns true. The caller therefore never executes DoesCodecProduceOriginals for that case.

## Steps to Reproduce
Inject or force Wirehair_ExtraInsufficient in Test_DecodeRandomLosses and observe that unit_test continues as if the round passed.

## Scope
Make correctness tests fail on every terminal decode result. If overhead/exhaustion measurement is intentionally nonfatal, isolate it in an explicitly statistical benchmark mode whose outcome cannot satisfy the round-trip correctness gate.
acceptance_criteria: Add a focused decoder/test seam proving NeedMore continues, Success requires full byte comparison, and InvalidInput/Error/OOM/ExtraInsufficient all fail the correctness test. Add deterministic seeds/cases that exercise the extra-row boundary without flaky probabilistic expectations. End to end, run a representative small/medium/maximum-N random-loss matrix and assert every passing case reconstructs and byte-compares the message; demonstrate an injected terminal failure makes CTest nonzero. Run ASan/UBSan and repeated bug-review passes.
notes:
Random-loss correctness now treats ExtraInsufficient as terminal failure. Added shared terminal-result policy coverage for InvalidInput, seed/input errors, ExtraInsufficient, Error, OOM, and UnsupportedPlatform plus a WILL_FAIL CTest injection proving terminal exhaustion makes the test nonzero. Deterministic Release runs pass at N=2..256, N=1000, and N=64000, with ASan/UBSan.
Independent exact-oracle review replaced WILL_FAIL with stable injected rc=4, required terminal diagnostic, and sanitizer rejection. The stricter gate exposed a 692-byte encoder leak on Test_DecodeRandomLosses early returns; scoped codec ownership now covers every exit. Full Clang ASan/UBSan/LSan CTest passed 20/20, including the exact terminal case with no leak.
close_reason: Exact terminal oracle now rejects wrong exits and sanitizer failures; leaked codecs on all early returns were fixed and full ASan/UBSan/LSan suite is green.

title: Strictly validate dense-count shard schemas, domains, and provenance
description:
## Problem
Dense-count aggregation/compare/derive tools discard the first line without proving it is a header and accept impossible numeric domains. Malformed shards can silently lose real data and publish invalid replacement candidates.

## Evidence
tables/aggregate_dense_count_shards.sh:45-55 and compare_dense_count_shards.sh:133-143 skip line 1 unconditionally and accept arbitrary numeric N/dense/failure; derive lacks production domain checks. A headerless file containing rows '2 2 .1' and '3 99 1.5' drops N=2 and publishes the impossible N=3 row.

## Steps to Reproduce
Feed that two-row headerless fixture to aggregate, derive, and compare and inspect the accepted output.

## Scope
Require an exact/versioned header and field count; enforce production N, dense-count, failure-rate, uniqueness/order domains; reject duplicate/conflicting N; and verify shard provenance/trial/failure-threshold compatibility before combining evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Add fixture tests for valid input, headerless input, wrong/reordered columns, missing/extra fields, N outside 2..64000, invalid dense counts, failure outside [0,1], NaN/infinity, and duplicate/conflicting N. Compatible distinct-seed repeats must combine while preserving provenance; disjoint shards from one seed/campaign must compose once, and overlapping reused-seed evidence must not be double-counted. Incompatible trial/threshold/methodology manifests must fail or be explicitly grouped separately. Invalid inputs must produce zero candidate stdout. End to end, validate and run aggregate -> derive -> compare over every current dense-count shard set, repeat deterministically, and independently spot-check candidate rows against GetDenseCount/model limits. Run bash -n/shellcheck and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Added shared strict dense_count_validate.py and routed aggregate/compare/derive through it. Exact shard/aggregate headers and field counts are required; N/DenseCount/rates/observation/min-max domains, finiteness, duplicates, and nonempty data are checked. Manifest/filename provenance carries seed, trials, max_failures and low_count_run; incompatible methodology and overlapping same-seed N ranges reject, while distinct seeds and disjoint same-seed shards are allowed. Unit suite 5/5 covers schemas/domains/NaN/duplicates/provenance. End-to-end compatible two-seed aggregate+derive passes; current compatible t500 shards produce 546 lines and comparison total=2180 changed=2120; malformed/headerless/NaN inputs fail with zero stdout. bash -n, py_compile, diff check, and repeated bug passes pass.
close_reason: Dense-count pipelines now enforce schemas, domains, methodology provenance, and seed overlap rules with unit and end-to-end coverage.

title: Export the C API correctly from Windows shared-library builds
description:
## Problem
Windows shared builds do not define the macros required by the public header to export Wirehair symbols, so a DLL can build without a linkable public C API.

## Evidence
include/wirehair/wirehair.h:49-64 emits __declspec(dllexport/dllimport) only for WIREHAIR_BUILDING plus WIREHAIR_DLL. The targets at CMakeLists.txt:129-142 define neither; CMake's automatic wirehair_EXPORTS macro is not used.

## Steps to Reproduce
Configure BUILD_SHARED_LIBS=ON with MSVC, build the DLL, inspect its export table, then link an external C consumer against the generated import library/API.

## Scope
Add target-scoped build/import/static definitions or a generated export header, preserving source incorporation/static usage and avoiding macro leakage to unrelated targets.
acceptance_criteria: Add MSVC and MinGW shared/static build tests. Inspect DLL exports for every public C function and compile/link an out-of-tree installed pure-C consumer without manual defines. The consumer must initialize, encode packets, drop/reorder data, decode/recover byte-exactly, convert a decoder to encoder, and call recover_block. Confirm static consumers do not use dllimport, run Windows CTest, and complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes:
CMake now sets WIREHAIR_BUILDING+WIREHAIR_DLL privately for DLLs, WIREHAIR_DLL on consumers, and WIREHAIR_STATIC for static targets; the header static branch prevents dllimport. Strict x86_64 MinGW 13 static/shared installs plus external pure-C/C++/plugin/host all compile and link. PE DLL export evidence contains exactly all 15 WIREHAIR_EXPORT C functions. The shared C consumer has __imp_wirehair imports and a libwirehair.dll dependency; static gets WIREHAIR_STATIC and no Wirehair DLL dependency. Full MinGW strict core/tests/V2/all explicit tools compile after using excluded static internal support without widening DLL ABI.

The native gate pins both MSVC jobs to windows-2022 because windows-latest no longer guarantees the Visual Studio 17 environment. The strict Visual Studio shared lane locates the installed VS2022 dumpbin.exe through PATH, VCToolsInstallDir, VSINSTALLDIR, VCINSTALLDIR, VS170COMNTOOLS, vswhere, or standard VS2022 roots; runs /nologo /exports against the installed DLL; retains complete output under ci-logs; and requires the entire parsed DLL export table to equal WIREHAIR_C_API_EXPORTS exactly before package-consumer and native Python E2E.

Local evidence: ci.test_run_ci passes 17/17. Parser, discovery, lane selection, and integration-style validator coverage includes exact success, missing exports, unexpected wirehair exports, arbitrary unexpected exports, forwarded names, PATH preference, and newest toolset selection. python py_compile, actionlint 1.7.12, AST parse, and git diff --check pass. Independent review tightened the assertion from wirehair-name filtering to whole-table equality and caught/fixed unreachable test setup. Keep this bead open until the pushed native MSVC static/shared jobs and dumpbin assertion are green.
Hosted run 29066341405 at exact SHA 337cc0d73e79f6b1ad64e0e4dfa7449250e33c90 passed native MSVC static/shared. Shared job 86278561264 ran the real VS2022 dumpbin.exe against the installed bin/wirehair.dll and the whole export table equaled exactly the 15 public C API symbols; Windows CTest passed 19/19, installed C/C++/plugin consumers passed 3/3, and installed native Python loss/reorder/recover_block/conversion E2E passed. Static job 86278561261 also passed install/package 3/3. Recorded strict MinGW import/export evidence remains green. Run: https://github.com/catid/wirehair/actions/runs/29066341405.
close_reason: Implemented, independently reviewed, locally verified, and accepted by the all-green 9-job hosted run 29066341405 at exact SHA 337cc0d73e79f6b1ad64e0e4dfa7449250e33c90, including native MSVC static/shared end-to-end coverage.

title: Make legacy and GF256 initialization thread-safe with cached results
description:
## Problem
Concurrent first use races on the process-global initialization flags and GF256 tables. Callers can read partially initialized state, and the C++ data races are undefined behavior.

## Evidence
wirehair.cpp:34 and 69-83 reads/writes m_init without synchronization. gf256.cpp:488-489 and 794-823 similarly guards writes to GF256Ctx with an unsynchronized bool. The API documents startup initialization but does not prevent concurrent calls.

## Steps to Reproduce
Release many threads from a barrier so they call wirehair_init/gf256_init and create codecs simultaneously on a cold process; inspect with ThreadSanitizer or a race detector.

## Scope
Use one-time initialization with atomic publication and a cached success/permanent-failure result. Keep version mismatch per caller from poisoning a later correct-version call and define/document retry semantics after platform/self-test failure.
acceptance_criteria: Add a cold-start subprocess test that releases many threads into repeated init plus parallel codec round trips; run it under TSan on a supported CI host and require no race. Add injectable unit cases for correct/wrong versions, concurrent mixed versions, cached success, and permanent initialization failure. End to end, initialize and encode/decode concurrently across many independent codecs, run ASan/UBSan as well, document thread guarantees, and complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Implemented std::call_once initialization and cached results for Wirehair/GF256, with version mismatch non-poisoning and cached permanent failure. Independent review fixed unconditional Threads::Threads linkage for all library variants including BUILD_TESTS=OFF. Release/ASan/UBSan, installed static/shared consumers, 24-thread cold-start codec E2E, mixed versions, and failure subprocesses pass. After installing Clang 18, a direct -fsanitize=thread build ran preinit, init-failure, cold-start, and full LegacyCoreTest modes with TSAN_OPTIONS=halt_on_error=1 and no race report. Multiple independent bug-review passes found no remaining defect.
close_reason: Thread-safe cached initialization is fully covered, including a clean Clang 18 ThreadSanitizer cold-start/concurrent codec run.

title: Enforce codec mode and lifecycle on every legacy C API operation
description:
## Problem
The opaque codec does not track/check whether it is an encoder, decoder, completed decoder, or converted encoder. Wrong-state calls can return success, emit zero data, or read past the caller's message.

## Evidence
wirehair_decoder_becomes_encoder only null-checks at wirehair.cpp:248-259; InitializeEncoderFromDecoder relies on a release-disabled assertion around WirehairCodec.cpp:5143-5176; Encode has no mode check. Converting an encoder with a partial final block returned success then caused an ASan heap-buffer-overflow in systematic Encode. A fresh decoder also accepted wirehair_encode and returned zero data with success.

## Steps to Reproduce
Call decoder_becomes_encoder on an encoder, then encode its final partial block under ASan. Separately call wirehair_encode on a fresh decoder and inspect the successful zero-filled result.

## Scope
Introduce explicit Uninitialized/Encoder/Decoder/DecodeComplete/Converted/Failed lifecycle state, validate it at every public entry point, and reset it correctly on object reuse and all failures.
acceptance_criteria: Add a table-driven unit matrix covering every public operation in every state, including premature/double conversion, encode/recover before completion, decode on encoder, operations after terminal failure, free/null, and encoder/decoder reuse. Wrong-state calls must return stable errors, zero output lengths where applicable, and never mutate state/output. Run the partial-final ASan repro plus end-to-end V1 encode/loss/decode/recover/convert/re-encode flows and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Implemented explicit Uninitialized/Encoder/Decoder/DecodeComplete/Converted/Failed Codec modes and guards on all legacy C API operations. Duplicate originals and terminal decode errors enter Failed; reuse resets mode. Tests cover encoder, fresh/completed/converted/failed decoder states, premature/double conversion, partial-final wrong conversion repro, zero lengths/unchanged buffers, null/free, reuse, recovery, and converted re-encode under ASan/UBSan.
close_reason: Implemented, covered by focused unit/E2E tests and strict installed consumers, and verified in Release plus ASan/UBSan after repeated bug-review passes.

title: Make legacy encoder input ownership explicit and provide an owning path
description:
## Problem
wirehair_encoder_create silently borrows the caller's message pointer, but the public contract does not require the buffer to remain alive and immutable. Freeing it causes use-after-free; mutating it can make systematic and repair symbols encode different messages.

## Evidence
WirehairCodec.cpp:4384-4390 stores the input pointer without copying and lines 4930-4937 read it later for systematic encoding. include/wirehair/wirehair.h:151-182 omits lifetime/immutability requirements. Free-after-create produced an ASan heap-use-after-free in Codec::Encode.

## Steps to Reproduce
Create an encoder, free or mutate the source buffer, then request a systematic block. Under ASan the free case reports UAF; mutation changes emitted source bytes while precomputed repair state still reflects the old data.

## Scope
Document the borrowed zero-copy contract immediately and add an ABI-compatible owning/copying creation option or explicit detach operation. Ensure reuse and failure ownership rules cover both modes.
acceptance_criteria: Add unit tests for borrowed and owned lifetimes, mutation behavior, partial final blocks, reuse, allocation failure, and destruction. The owning path must remain correct after the caller overwrites/frees its source and must expose no leaks/UAF under ASan. End to end, destroy the original message, generate a mix of systematic and repair packets under loss/reordering, decode, and byte-compare the full message. Benchmark/document copy-memory and initialization costs and complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Implemented additive borrowed-result and owned encoder APIs. Owned mode copies and pads input inside Codec; borrowed lifetime/immutability and O(messageBytes) cost are documented. LegacyCoreTest covers mutation, caller free/overwrite, partial final block, reuse, injected OOM, destruction, lossy/reordered systematic+repair E2E, and creation timing. ASan/UBSan and installed C/C++ static/shared consumers pass.
close_reason: Implemented, covered by focused unit/E2E tests and strict installed consumers, and verified in Release plus ASan/UBSan after repeated bug-review passes.

title: Verify recovered bytes in whx performance benchmarks
description:
## Problem
The whx performance path counts a round as successful when wirehair_recover returns success but never compares decoded bytes with the original message. A data-corrupting optimization can therefore publish valid-looking throughput and exit zero.

## Evidence
bench/whx.cpp:816-829 times/accepts recovery without comparing w.decoded to w.message even though both buffers exist. Prior API-return/failure accounting did not add content verification.

## Steps to Reproduce
Inject a one-byte corruption after successful recovery or link a fault stub that returns Wirehair_Success with bad output; run the performance benchmark and observe that current code reports success/MBPS.

## Scope
Compare the full message outside the timed interval, record mismatch counts and reproducible seed/N/block-byte context, return nonzero on any corruption, and keep validation overhead out of reported codec timing.
acceptance_criteria: Add a focused unit/test seam whose fault-injected successful recover is rejected, including first/middle/final-byte corruptions and partial-final-block messages. End to end, run a deterministic small N x block-bytes benchmark grid and an ASan valid run with exact output verification, then prove a deliberate one-byte corruption build/hook fails and emits reproducible context. Confirm throughput timing excludes comparison and complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes: bench_one_round now compares the complete recovered message after the recover timer stops, counts mismatches as failures, and emits seed/N/bb/bytes/offset context. WHX_BENCH_CORRUPT provides validated first/middle/last/absolute fault injection. Unit seam rejects first/middle/final corruption in a 10-byte partial-final-block shape; CLI faults at offsets 0/112/223 all exit 1 with MISMATCHES=1; small grids, fuzz, real generated-table codec pipeline, and ASan/UBSan pass.
close_reason: Benchmark recovery is byte-exact outside timing with proven fault rejection

title: Prevent seedsearch output from mislabeling non-10-percent loss data
description:
## Problem
seedsearch accepts arbitrary loss rates but always emits a positional field labeled best_mean@0.10. Downstream fixup generators consume that field as 10% evidence, so a run at another loss can silently generate semantically invalid tables.

## Evidence
bench/whx.cpp:1193-1202 parses arbitrary --loss, validation near 1281 uses that configured loss, but output near 1266 always labels best_mean@0.10. Running with --loss 0.20 still emits the 0.10 label; bench/gen_fixups*.sh consume positional columns.

## Steps to Reproduce
Run a bounded seedsearch with --loss 0.20, inspect the output label/value, then feed it to the fixup generator parser.

## Scope
Either enforce loss exactly 0.10 for the existing format or introduce a versioned self-describing schema and update all producers/consumers. Reject mixed/mislabeled artifacts before table generation.
acceptance_criteria: Choose and test one explicit design. If the legacy positional format is retained, unit/CLI tests must accept exactly loss=0.10 and reject 0.20, malformed, and nonfinite loss before output. If a versioned self-describing format is introduced, add producer/parser tests for 0.10/0.20, missing/mismatched metadata, supported/unsupported versions, column mapping, and mixed inputs. In either design, run seedsearch -> gen_fixups/gen_fixups2 -> compile -> independent verification at the configured primary loss and 0.30 with deterministic seeds, proving recorded metadata/contract matches executed trials and incompatible input fails before emitting includes. Run script syntax/static checks, sanitizers for whx, and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Retained the positional legacy contract and now require --loss to parse exactly as 0.10 before any output. Seedsearch emits validated v1 schema/pairing metadata; validate_seedsearch.sh accepts consistent legacy 5-column artifacts and current 6-column artifacts, rejects wrong loss/version/mixed widths/nonfinite or out-of-range data, and generators write atomically. E2E seedsearch -> both generators -> include compile -> real WirehairTools link -> fuzz/ohead at 0.10 and 0.30 passes; 0.20/nan/malformed CLI and mislabeled artifacts fail without changing outputs. Final acceptance reran RUN_SANITIZERS=1 bash bench/test_whx.sh successfully: normal/knob selftests (33/36 assertions), seedsearch-to-generators-to-compiled real-codec fuzz/ohead, exact negative cases, verify_fix seven-scenario coverage, and ASan+UBSan+LSan selftest/ohead/bench all passed.
close_reason: Legacy 10-percent contract and artifact validation enforced end to end

title: Make bench verify_fix.sh fail on residual and collateral regressions
description:
## Problem
The fixup verification script describes a zero-residual and collateral-safety gate but only prints scan counts and suppresses failures, so incomplete or harmful fixup tables can pass with exit status zero.

## Evidence
bench/verify_fix.sh:2-6 states the contract, but lines 20-21 pipe/print weak counts with || true and the file ends at line 26 without the documented collateral step. This gap remains after the prior pipefail hardening.

## Steps to Reproduce
Run the script against a deliberately incomplete/bad fixup include or a fake whx that reports WEAK residual rows; it reaches the end and exits successfully unless the separate fuzz command happens to fail.

## Scope
Turn every stated condition into an asserted gate: exact requested weak-set scan, zero residuals, fuzz status preservation, and deterministic non-fixed collateral comparison. Add any exact --nfile scan support needed and make threads/ranges reproducibly configurable.
acceptance_criteria: Add shell/integration tests with a fake harness covering build failure, residual weak rows, fuzz failure, collateral regression, malformed output, and the all-pass path; each failure must return nonzero with a precise diagnostic. End to end, prove a deliberately bad fixup fails and the current shipped fixups pass exact scan, fuzz, and collateral comparison with recorded deterministic commands/seeds. Run bash -n, shellcheck when available, and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Independent review strengthened the distinct-baseline gate: separate paths/inodes are no longer enough; byte-identical baseline/fixed harnesses are rejected. Fake integration coverage now has seven scenarios including same-content binaries, plus build failure, residual, fuzz failure, collateral change, malformed output, and pass. Script syntax and real whx E2E remain clean; shellcheck is unavailable.
close_reason: Exact residual/fuzz/collateral gate implemented and real/fake tested

title: Compute whx histogram quantiles with valid nearest ranks
description:
## Problem
whx quantiles use a zero target rank for small samples, selecting an empty bin and reporting percentiles inconsistent with the observed mean/max.

## Evidence
bench/whx.cpp:721-724 and the call around line 1055 calculate target=(uint64_t)(p*trials) and select cum>=target. With one sample, target is zero. The deterministic ohead repro reports mean/max=1 but p50/p99/p999=0.

## Steps to Reproduce
Run whx ohead --threads 1 --nlo 100 --nhi 100 --trials 1 --bb 1 --startmode 1 --loss 0 --seed 12 and compare the printed mean/max and percentiles.

## Scope
Introduce one documented histogram quantile helper with correct nearest-rank/ceil semantics, explicit empty-input behavior, overflow-safe accumulation, and use it for every reported percentile.
acceptance_criteria: Unit-test exact histograms for sample counts 0, 1, 2, repeated bins, percentile boundaries, and capped overflow/tail bins against a simple sorted-sample oracle. The stated repro must report all quantiles as one. End to end, run deterministic ohead and fuzz distributions, retain/export raw samples or an independent reference calculation, and assert every reported p50/p99/p999 matches. Run sanitizer builds and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Implemented one nearest-rank histogram helper with explicit empty behavior, uint64 bins/saturating sums, coherent overflow fallback, and reuse by fuzz/ohead/WH_COUNT summaries. Added raw ohead sample export and fixed degenerate/log sweep endpoints. Verification: selftest covers 0/1/2/repeated/boundary/capped/overflow histograms against a sorted oracle; exact one-trial repro reports mean/max/p50/p99/p999=1; randomized 41-sample CLI output matches independent sorted raw samples; normal and ASan/UBSan suites pass.
close_reason: Nearest-rank statistics implemented and independently verified

title: Reject overflowing precode_sim scheme dimensions before construction
description:
## Problem
precode_sim accepts arbitrary uint32 scheme dimensions, combines them with unchecked arithmetic, and uses wrapped/out-of-domain column indexes. A malformed CLI scheme can segfault worker construction instead of rejecting input.

## Evidence
experiments/precode/precode_sim.cpp:1526-1534 accepts H tokens; lines 435-437 compute D/L in unsigned arithmetic; only K is range-checked around 2652-2662; PeelSolver trusts column indexes at 827-832. The repro none_h4294967295 exits 139, with ASan at ColRows[c]. This remains after the July 3 token-truncation parser fix.

## Steps to Reproduce
Run precode_sim --K 2 --oh 0 --schemes none_h4294967295 --trials 1 --threads 1 under ASan or a normal build and observe the out-of-bounds crash.

## Scope
Validate every explicit _d/_h and related dimension token using checked widened K+D+H/D+Dense2 arithmetic, representability and model-resource caps before printing CSV, allocating, or spawning workers.
acceptance_criteria: Add table-driven parser/model unit tests for zero, boundary, maximum, maximum+1, wraparound, and resource-cap values across every scheme grammar. A malformed CLI fuzz corpus under ASan/UBSan must always exit cleanly with no CSV data rows. Run precode_sim --self-test plus real valid sweeps at small K and K=64000 for representative dense, LDPC, codecport, and explicit-dimension schemes, verifying schema and row integrity. Complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Added widened validation for D+Dense2, K+D+Dense2+H, a 4096 precode-column resource cap, H<=128, dense-sparse weight bounds, and K+overhead<=UINT16_MAX. Validation now preflights the complete K/scheme/overhead grid before any CSV header or worker allocation. Self-test has table-driven coverage across all scheme grammars for zero, exact cap, cap+1, 16-bit total boundary, uint32 max/wraparound, sparse weights, heavy suffixes, N1 overrides, and shuffle2. Verified normal self-test (11520 rank-invariance instances); 20-case malformed CLI corpus under ASan/UBSan exits nonzero with zero CSV output and no sanitizer reports; real valid dense/LDPC/codecport/explicit-dimension sweeps at K=32 and K=64000 produce and validate all 8 expected rows; tracked results use max D=692 below the cap. Repeated manual review passes found no further defect.
close_reason: Overflowing and resource-excessive simulator dimensions are rejected before output/allocation and verified under sanitizers.

title: Gate x86 SIMD dispatch on OSXSAVE and XCR0 state
description:
## Problem
Runtime dispatch enables AVX2, GFNI, and AVX-512 kernels from CPU feature bits alone. A CPU/VM may advertise those instructions while the OS has not enabled saving the required vector state, causing SIGILL or state corruption when a dispatched kernel executes.

## Evidence
gf256.cpp:448-471 reads CPUID leaves 1 and 7 and sets CpuHasAVX2/CpuHasGFNI/CpuHasAVX512 directly. It never requires CPUID OSXSAVE/AVX or reads XCR0 for XMM/YMM and AVX-512 opmask/ZMM state.

## Steps to Reproduce
Build with the optional AVX2/GFNI/AVX-512 kernels enabled and run under a VM/emulator or injected feature snapshot that advertises leaf-7 ISA bits while clearing OSXSAVE or required XCR0 bits. Observe that current selection still marks the kernel active.

## Scope
Centralize x86 capability detection, check maximum leaves, OSXSAVE/AVX prerequisites, and XGETBV state masks before enabling each kernel. Never execute XGETBV when unsupported and retain scalar/SSSE3 fallbacks.
acceptance_criteria: Extract or wrap detection so unit tests can inject CPUID/XCR0 snapshots. Cover every combination of CPU bit present/absent, OSXSAVE absent, XMM/YMM incomplete, and AVX-512 opmask/ZMM bits incomplete; assert only legal kernels activate. Add a dispatch-observation test and run specialized binaries under at least one lower-feature VM/emulator without SIGILL, followed by gf256 self-tests and full codec encode/drop/decode/recover. Test GCC/Clang/MSVC implementations, run sanitizers where applicable, and complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes:
Implemented injectable CPUID/XCR0 feature selection with leaf checks, AVX+OSXSAVE prerequisites, and legal XMM/YMM/opmask/ZMM masks. Independent review expanded coverage to each absent ISA bit and each missing XCR0 bit, built native AVX2/GFNI/AVX-512 kernels, and passed selector, self-test, fuzz, and full codec benchmark E2E under Release and ASan/UBSan. Clang/MSVC and an actually lower-feature VM/emulator remain outstanding.
Follow-up portability verification: Clang 18 selector/full codec builds pass; Clang TSan init/cold-start modes pass. Automated exact QEMU lane runs portable binary on Conroe with SSSE3/AVX2/GFNI/AVX512=0/0/0/0 and AVX2-specialized binary on Haswell with 1/1/0/0, each completing GF256 selftest, N=64000 indexed recovery, lifecycle, loss/decode/recover E2E without SIGILL. Awaiting pushed native MSVC matrix only.
Hosted run 29066341405 for exact SHA 337cc0d73e79f6b1ad64e0e4dfa7449250e33c90 passed native MSVC static/shared strict full builds and Windows CTest 19/19, including the injectable CPUID/XCR0 selector, dispatch observation, legacy lifecycle/cold-start modes, and high-N recovery coverage. Both jobs were warning-clean and completed all exact success/failure E2Es without illegal-instruction failure. Combined with the recorded Clang, sanitizer, and QEMU Conroe/Haswell evidence, acceptance is complete. Run: https://github.com/catid/wirehair/actions/runs/29066341405; static job 86278561261; shared job 86278561264.
close_reason: Implemented, independently reviewed, locally verified, and accepted by the all-green 9-job hosted run 29066341405 at exact SHA 337cc0d73e79f6b1ad64e0e4dfa7449250e33c90, including native MSVC static/shared end-to-end coverage.

title: Make V2 recovery-row lookup independent of prior IDs and support full block IDs
description:
## Problem
The production V2 precode encoder regenerates every prior matrix row for each repair packet. Sequential output is quadratic, random high IDs are increasingly slow, and recovery indexes at or above 65,536 are rejected despite the uint32 block-ID API.

## Evidence
codec/WirehairV2Peel.cpp:877-955 loops from row 0 through row_index in both single-row helpers. codec/WirehairV2PrecodeEncode.cpp:593-600 calls GenerateRecoveryMatrixRow for every encoded repair and imposes kMaxPeelMatrixRows. Release measurements at K=1000/mix=5 showed row 65,535 taking 30.44 ms; rows 0..4095 took 3.442 s through the single helper versus 1.888 ms in batch (about 1,823x).

## Steps to Reproduce
Benchmark GenerateRecoveryMatrixRow at indexes 0, 1024, 16384, and 65535, then encode rows 0..4095 one by one and compare with GenerateRecoveryMatrixRows. Attempt a recovery block whose index is 65536.

## Scope
Adopt row-index-addressable deterministic PRFs before wire-format freeze, or an equally efficient checkpoint/stream design. Seek/index overhead must not replay earlier rows; generating the requested row remains O(degree + mix). Remove evaluator-only row limits from packet IDs and explicitly preserve, or intentionally version and revise, the existing contract that each recovery row's source prefix equals the corresponding peel row.
acceptance_criteria: Add golden row vectors plus unit tests proving batch/single identity, deterministic generation, distinct source/mix streams, the recovery-source-prefix contract (or an explicitly versioned replacement), and arbitrary shuffled uint32 recovery IDs. Add deterministic work/RNG-draw counters showing seek overhead does not grow with row ID, plus a statistically robust scaling benchmark for at least 4096 sequential and shuffled repairs that demonstrates near-linear total work. End to end, encode source and repair packets from MessagePrecodeEncoder in reordered/lossy delivery and compare every symbol with a batch/manual-XOR oracle; leave the full V2 decode round trip to wirehair-axd. Run ASan/UBSan and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Implemented row-contract v2 with independently keyed per-row source/mix streams and constant-time uint32 lookup; removed packet-ID row cap. Added golden vectors, 4096 batch/single identity, shuffled full-ID work counters/scaling, maximum block-ID tests, and reordered MessagePrecodeEncoder source/repair symbols checked against batch/manual-XOR oracles. Release scaling: 4096 batch 0.279 ms, sequential singles 0.353 ms, shuffled full-range 0.348 ms. ASan/UBSan passed. Full V2 decode round trip remains explicitly in active wirehair-axd; encoder-symbol E2E required here is complete.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with focused unit/property/oracle tests, Release E2E/subprocess CTests, constrained-memory repros, strict warning build, and ASan/UBSan.

title: Make host-native ISA compilation opt-in and ship portable defaults
description:
## Problem
Default builds are specialized for the build host and can crash with illegal instructions when the library is copied to an older CPU, packaged for a distribution, or cross-compiled.

## Evidence
CMakeLists.txt:13 defaults MARCH_NATIVE to ON and lines 87-91 append -march=native to every normal configuration. This can let the compiler emit host-only instructions even outside the runtime-dispatched gf256 kernels, defeating portability and reproducibility.

## Steps to Reproduce
Configure without setting MARCH_NATIVE, build verbosely on an AVX-capable x86 host, and observe -march=native on all core translation units. Move that binary to a runner/VM exposing an older ISA, or inspect emitted instructions relative to the intended baseline.

## Scope
Default to a documented portable target, retain an explicit native-tuning opt-in, use compiler/architecture capability checks, and keep specialized kernels behind correct runtime dispatch or separate target-specific compilation.
acceptance_criteria: Add configure/compile tests proving the default has no -march=native and the opt-in does. Run full CTest plus an installed C API encode/drop/decode/recover round trip on at least one lower-ISA x86 VM/emulator and one non-x86 or cross-compile target where available. Test GCC and Clang portable builds and verify the explicit native build still passes SIMD self-tests and records a benchmark comparison. Complete repeated code-review passes.
notes: MARCH_NATIVE now defaults OFF, is target-scoped, capability-checked, documented host-only, and covered by build_policy_e2e (portable compile DB rejects -march=native; opt-in requires it). GCC/Clang portable suites pass. Portable Clang E2E passed qemu-x86_64 Conroe with runtime SIMD inactive; explicit AVX2 passed Haswell and correctly SIGILLed on Conroe. Strict AArch64 cross CMake/install plus qemu consumers/plugin passed 3/3 and N=64000 lifecycle passed. Native build passed GF256/legacy E2E; sample 20-trial Unit benchmark wall time was 0.10s native vs 0.14s portable (observational).
close_reason: Implemented, independently reviewed, and verified by strict GCC/Clang/MinGW/AArch64 builds, full CTest/sanitizer suites, installed consumer/plugin E2Es, build-policy regressions, and repeated clean bug-review pass.

title: Stop overriding caller CMake configurations and compiler flags
description:
## Problem
The top-level build rewrites standard CMake configuration state instead of composing with the caller/toolchain, silently disabling sanitizer, hardening, cross-toolchain, distro-packaging, and user optimization flags.

## Evidence
CMakeLists.txt:7 FORCE-replaces CMAKE_CONFIGURATION_TYPES with Debug/Release, and lines 82-84 replace CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS_{DEBUG,RELEASE,RELWITHDEBINFO}. A verified configure with -DCMAKE_CXX_FLAGS_RELEASE='-O1 -DUSER_RELEASE_SENTINEL' compiled with -O3 and dropped the sentinel.

## Steps to Reproduce
Configure Release with a unique value in CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS_RELEASE, build verbosely, and observe that the compile commands contain the project's -O3 but not the caller's sentinel. Configure a multi-config generator and observe the forced two-configuration list.

## Scope
Move project warnings/optimization policy to target-scoped options and configuration generator expressions; preserve user/toolchain cache values and standard/custom multi-config configurations.
acceptance_criteria: Add a CMake regression test that configures unique sentinel flags in Debug, Release, RelWithDebInfo, and a custom/multi-config setup and proves they reach compilation/linking. Clean GCC and Clang builds must pass in Debug and Release; ASan/UBSan flags supplied by the caller must remain active and the full registered CTest suite must pass. Run an out-of-tree installed-library C consumer round trip using a nondefault toolchain/configuration. Document intentionally added project flags and complete repeated code-review passes until one finds no new defects.
notes: Implemented target-scoped warnings/native/LTO/PGO and removed forced CMAKE_CONFIGURATION_TYPES/build-type/flag rewrites; C++11 is now a caller-overridable default. Automated build_policy_e2e proves compile+shared-link sentinels for Debug, Release, RelWithDebInfo, AuditConfig, and Ninja Multi-Config AuditConfig. GCC 13 and Clang 18 strict Debug/Release target graphs build; caller ASan+UBSan flags are visible in compile_commands and instrumented CTests pass. Relocated custom-libdir package C consumer and LTO/PGO-generate E2Es pass. README documents project-added flags.
close_reason: Implemented, independently reviewed, and verified by strict GCC/Clang/MinGW/AArch64 builds, full CTest/sanitizer suites, installed consumer/plugin E2Es, build-policy regressions, and repeated clean bug-review pass.

title: HANDOFF: analyze running big-K precode queue, fit final (S,D2,H) rule, write up + close session
description:
A detached measurement queue (started 2026-06-10 ~17:00, pid family of 'bash /tmp/run_queue.sh', script preserved at experiments/precode/run_bigk_queue_2026-06-10.sh) is producing the remaining big-K evidence for the dense-matrix replacement. Pick up from here; all small-K analysis is already recorded in wirehair-edj/wuw/47m notes and committed CSVs.

== Monitoring ==
Completion markers: /tmp/e5small_done (done), /tmp/e3abig_done, /tmp/e1bbig_done, /tmp/e5big_done, /tmp/queue_done; failures append to /tmp/queue_fail; per-run stderr /tmp/q_*.err. Live check: ps aux | grep precode_sim. If the queue died, comment out already-completed cells in the preserved script (a cell is complete when its CSV under experiments/precode/results/ has all oh rows for every scheme) and relaunch detached: setsid nohup bash <script> </dev/null >/tmp/queue_run.log 2>&1 &

== Expected outputs ==
e3a_K{16000,20000,32000,48000,64000}.csv (S-fraction x D2 grid + dense/ldpc controls + 2 h12 points, --paired seed 24414209), e1_hgrid_K{32000,64000}.csv (--ge-replay H-grid), e5_K{32000,64000}.csv (shuffle2/n1 at S=Dprod d12). Dprod per K: 16000:114, 20000:134, 32000:190, 48000:370, 64000:346.

== Analysis (decision rules from experiments/SESSION_PLAN_2026-06-10.md) ==
1. E3a rule fit: per K find (S,D2) with fail <= dense+0.3pp at oh0 (trials: 8000 -> CI ~±0.29pp; 3000 -> ±0.46pp) maximizing sparse-XOR reduction. Small-K answer so far: S=1.0*Dprod, D2=12 flat, -23..-28%. KEY QUESTIONS: (a) does d12 still pin the tail at K=32000/48000/64000 at full S, or does the def tail (def_max >> H, cliff) reappear -> need d26+/d0.3*Dprod? (b) K=48000: production GetDenseCount=370 is non-monotone (vs 346 at 64000) — flag if the smooth rule beats the table point. (c) pure ldpc passed at K<=10000 — check if it still passes at 32000+ (old low-trial data said no).
2. E1b: confirm ge_real_bitops ratios (real ~4x below estimate at small K; ldpc advantage -12% real vs -17% est) and the heavy-band census at scale (small-K: W99=13/19/23 for H=6/12/16; def_outside_w18_rate ~1.5% at H=12). Record band spec in wirehair-wuw notes.
3. E5 big-K: shuffle2 (_s2) vs iid and n12 vs n13 at scale. Small-K verdict: _s2 == iid; n12 == n13; but n12+_s2 COMBINED leaked at K=10000 (0.0209 vs 0.0166 dense, 20k trials) — verify whether the combination is also degraded at 32000/64000; if so the production spec is: n1=2 XOR shuffle2-D2, not both (or d2 17+ to compensate — untested, note it).
4. Run python3 experiments/precode/rank_total.py --xor-csv experiments/peeling/results/cold_xor_calibration.csv --muladd-csv experiments/peeling/results/muladd_calibration.csv on the e1_hgrid + e3a CSVs for the calibrated total-cost ranking and the h12-vs-dense crossover.

== Write-up + close ==
Append a 'June 2026 session' section to experiments/precode/results/SUMMARY.md (mirror the E2/E8 section added to experiments/peeling/results/SUMMARY.md): headline rule, heavy-band spec, real-GE correction (existing GE-bitops columns/claims in the old SUMMARY are ~4x pessimistic and the ldpc relative advantage shrinks under real elimination — correct the -32..-68% headline), shuffle2/n1 implementability verdict with the n12+s2 caveat, E5/E3a CSV references. Update + close-or-defer wirehair-edj (rule delivered), wirehair-sux (close: alternatives adjudicated — LDPC staircase won; deck/fixed-weight/heavyonly all dead), wirehair-wuw (band spec). Check the final unit_test gate at /tmp/unit_test_final.log (grep -i fail; 'Too much overhead' warnings are the known non-fatal class; if the process died early rerun ./build/unit_test). git add/commit/push all result CSVs and SUMMARY; verify 'git status' clean and up to date with origin per CLAUDE.md.
acceptance_criteria: All queue CSVs committed; final S(K)/D2(K)/H rule with per-K numbers recorded in wirehair-edj; precode SUMMARY session section written; wirehair-sux closed with reasoning; unit_test gate checked; git push clean.

title: Add v2 invalid-initialization regression coverage
description: The v2 wrapper fixes in commit 1549c9e validate huge message sizes before narrowing and preserve the last good SeedProfile after failed validation/core initialization. Add focused policy-test coverage so those regressions are caught directly.
