title: Adopt codec hardening fixes from dirty worktree
description: Dirty worktree contains focused codec hardening changes that need validation and commit: reject blockBytes above gf256 signed-int byte-count range, bound AllocateMatrix GE clearing to allocated rows under WH_ALIGN64, and keep duplicate all-original decode precondition failures from later resuming without GE state. Validate the changes, add focused policy coverage where practical, then commit and push.
close_reason: Validated and adopted codec hardening fixes: blockBytes >= 2^31 is rejected before gf256 signed-int byte counts can truncate, AllocateMatrix clears only allocated GE rows under WH_ALIGN64, duplicate all-original decode failures no longer resume without GE state, and policy coverage now checks duplicate-original failure/reuse plus oversized blockBytes on both encoder and decoder creation. Validation: cmake --build build --target wirehair wirehair_v2_policy_test unit_test; bash bench/build.sh; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test; ./bench/whx fuzz --threads 1 --secs 5 --nmax 1024 --seed 0xC0DEFACE; ./bench/whx ohead --threads 1 --trials 20 --bb 64 --startmode 1 --loss 0.10 --nlo 64 --nhi 256 --nstep 64; temporary WH_ALIGN64 ASan-only policy binary; git diff --check.

title: Precode replacement: tune hybrid ldpcdense rule, model heavy cost, prototype in codec
description: Continues wirehair-sux using experiments/precode/precode_sim (see experiments/precode/README.md and results/SUMMARY.md for the model, correctness proof, and findings). ESTABLISHED: LDPC-staircase + small dense hybrid (ldpcdense_s<S>_d<D2>) dominates the production dense matrix — at K=3200, s43_d12 with production H=6 gives equal failure, -12% GE size, -24% sparse XOR, -32% GE bitops; with H=12 (explicit Cauchy, see wirehair-wuw) failure drops 10-100x below production. Gains grow with K (-63% GE bitops at K=32000 for pure ldpc). NEXT STEPS: (1) K=64000 sweep was running at session end (experiments/precode/results/sweep_K64000.csv, appends as it goes; driver script /tmp/precode_sweep.sh, binary already built) — commit results when complete and check the ldpc trend holds at max N. (2) Hybrid grid at K=32000/64000 (ldpcdense_s{0.5,0.6,0.7}xD_d{0.15,0.25,0.35}xD x H{6,12}) to fit a sizing rule S(K), D2(K). (3) Sweep --mix 2,3,4 (received-row precode-column degree; production uses 3) — mix=2 saves 1 XOR/packet if reliability holds. (4) Add heavy-row cost columns: GE heavy elimination ~ H*R byte-ops per binary pivot + H muladd block-ops per pivot; confirm net win at 1280B blocks (use experiments/peeling/xor_bench calibration: 1280B=0.0226us, 100KiB=4.31us, 1MiB=45.9us per block XOR). (5) Prototype in the real codec: generate staircase+dense precode rows in a v2 path (codec/ dir wraps production via OverrideSeeds; a real prototype needs a new matrix construction next to MultiplyDenseRows/SetHeavyRows in WirehairCodec.cpp — see bd notes in wirehair-wuw for the heavy part). CAVEATS in SUMMARY.md interpretation section: simulator lets constraint rows peel (production peels only received rows — that capability is part of the win and must be implemented); heavy muladd cost not yet in cost columns; LDPC parity value generation is sum of ~3K/S+2 blocks per check row (precode_gen_xors column tracks it).
acceptance_criteria: sizing rule fitted for K in [1000,64000]; heavy-cost-adjusted frontier table; go/no-go recommendation for codec prototype
notes:
2026-06-10 session end state: rule through K=10000 settled (S=GetDenseCount(K), D2=12, H=12 -> fail 0.0001 vs dense 0.0171 at -27.8% sparse XOR at K=10000; pure ldpc also passes at K<=10000). Big-K grids (16000..64000) running in a detached queue; continuation handed off via wirehair-evq -> wirehair-brd (certification) -> wirehair-axd (codec prototype). E5 implementability: _s2==iid, n12==n13 individually; n12+_s2 combined leaked at K=10000 (+0.4pp) — production spec choice deferred to certification.
2026-06-10 E3a K16000 checkpoint (e3a_K16000.csv, 8000 paired trials): dense OH0 fail=0.0155. Full-size s114_d12 matches fail (0.0150) with -17.0% inact, -28.3% sparse XOR, and -42.5% GE bitops; s103_d23 also matches (0.015625) with -16.6% inact, -26.1% sparse, -41.8% GE bits. More aggressive s91_d23 saves -50.9% GE bits but fail rises to 0.0214; s68_d12 is clearly undersized (0.1455 fail) despite -69.7% GE bits. H=12 variants reduce fail to <=0.00075 with smaller GE savings. Awaiting K20000+.
2026-06-10 E3a K20000 checkpoint (e3a_K20000.csv, 8000 paired trials): dense OH0 fail=0.015875. s134_d12 matches fail (0.0165) with -19.7% inact, -28.8% sparse XOR, -47.9% GE bitops; s121_d27 is slightly better fail (0.013625) with -18.5% inact, -26.3% sparse, -45.6% GE bits. More aggressive s107_d27 saves -53.9% GE bits but fail rises to 0.017875; s80_d27 is too small (0.0605 fail). H=12 variants cut fail to <=0.001375 while keeping 45-51% GE-bit savings. Awaiting K32000+.
2026-06-10 E3a K32000 checkpoint (experiments/precode/results/e3a_K32000.csv, 5000 paired trials, Dprod=190): dense OH0 fail=0.0200. Pure ldpc still passes at OH0 fail=0.0182 with -28.2% inact, -30.8% sparse XOR, and -62.7% GE bitops, but has no dense rows. Full-size ldpcdense_s190_d12 matches/improves fail (0.0166) with -24.9% inact, -29.6% sparse XOR, and -57.5% GE bitops; H=12 ldpcdense_s190_d12_h12 drops OH0 fail to 0.0004 while keeping -29.6% sparse XOR and -55.6% GE bitops. ldpcdense_s152_d38_h12 is also strong (fail=0.0008, -24.7% sparse, -56.9% GE bits). Undersized d12 rows show tails (s114_d12 bad; s133_d12 high), so at K32000 d12 only looks safe when S is near Dprod unless H=12 is used. Next queued result: E3a K48000.
2026-06-11 E3a K48000 checkpoint (experiments/precode/results/e3a_K48000.csv, 3000 paired trials, Dprod=370): dense OH0 fail=0.017667. Pure ldpc still passes at OH0 fail=0.016000 with -26.2% inact, -35.4% sparse XOR, and -59.7% GE bitops. Full-size ldpcdense_s370_d12 also passes (0.015000) with -24.1% inact, -35.0% sparse, -56.2% GE bits. The best H=6 balance is around s296_d12: OH0 fail=0.012667 with -35.6% inact, -32.8% sparse, and -73.2% GE bits; s259_d12 is close to dense reliability (0.016667) with stronger GE savings (-79.3%) but nonzero fail at OH1/OH2/OH5. Undersized s222_d12 is bad (0.091-0.121 fail even with OH), while larger D2 often adds cost without matching s296_d12. H=12 checks: s296_d74_h12 OH0 fail=0.000333 with -31.4% sparse and -57.7% GE bits; s370_d12_h12 had 0 failures in 3000 trials with -35.0% sparse and -55.2% GE bits. K64000 E3a is now running.
2026-06-11 E3a K64000 checkpoint (experiments/precode/results/e3a_K64000.csv, 3000 paired trials, Dprod=346, committed c969d02): dense OH0 fail=0.019000. Pure ldpc still passes at OH0 fail=0.016000 with -32.1% inact, -32.3% sparse XOR, and -68.6% GE bitops, but remains dense-row-free. Full-size ldpcdense_s346_d12 is the best H=6 low-cost balance: OH0 fail=0.011333, OH1=0.001000, OH2=0.001333, OH5=0.000333 with -30.1% inact, -31.4% sparse, and -65.8% GE bitops. Higher D2 at full S improves the high-overhead tail to zero observed failures for d69/d104 at OH>=1, but costs more GE (-51.5% / -41.3% vs dense). H=12 checks are strongest: s346_d12_h12 has OH0=0.000667, OH1=0.000667, OH2/OH5=0 with -31.4% sparse and -64.9% GE bits; it beats s277_d69_h12 on both cost and tail stability. Takeaway through max K: S=Dprod, D2=12 remains the conservative rule; H=12 gives the intended tail suppression with modest cost over H=6. Awaiting E1 H-grid and E5 big-K results before final SUMMARY/rule close.
2026-06-11 E1 H-grid K32000 checkpoint (experiments/precode/results/e1_hgrid_K32000.csv, 3000 paired trials, ge-replay, committed 9858479): dense H6 OH0 fail=0.022000 with GE estimate 1.52M bitops vs real replay 297.8k bitops (about 5.1x lower absolute). The relative LDPC/hybrid savings survive the real replay: pure ldpc OH0 fail=0.017000, sparse -30.8%, est GE -62.7%, real GE -61.2%; ldpcdense_s152_d38 OH0 fail=0.014333, sparse -24.7%, est GE -58.8%, real GE -56.9%. H=12 tail suppression costs little relative GE: ldpcdense_s190_d12_h12 OH0 fail=0.000333, OH1=0.000333, OH2=0.000000, sparse -29.6%, est GE -55.6%, real GE -54.4%. Band stats at K32000: H6 W99 about 13-14 with def_outside_w18 <=0.001; H12 W99 about 17-20 with outside_w18 about 0.0037-0.021 depending scheme/OH. Awaiting E1 K64000 to confirm band/cost scaling at max K.
2026-06-11 E1 H-grid K64000 checkpoint (experiments/precode/results/e1_hgrid_K64000.csv, 1500 paired trials, ge-replay, committed 62668a7): dense H6 OH0 fail=0.019333 with GE estimate 9.42M bitops vs real replay 1.71M bitops (about 5.5x lower absolute). Relative savings again survive replay: pure ldpc OH0 fail=0.014000, sparse -32.3%, est GE -68.6%, real GE -67.7%; ldpc_h12 OH0 fail=0.001333, real GE -67.1%. Among hybrid H12 points, s277_d69_h12 gives OH0 fail=0.000667 with sparse -26.9%, est GE -61.8%, real GE -61.1%, W99=19; s346_d12_h12 is stronger cost/reliability at max K: OH0 fail=0.000667, OH1/OH2=0, sparse -31.4%, est GE -64.9%, real GE -64.2%, W99=19. Band stats at K64000 match K32000: H6 W99 about 13-14 with outside_w18 <=0.0013; H12 W99 about 17-19 with outside_w18 roughly 0.0047-0.0207. This reinforces the sizing-rule takeaway: use S=Dprod, D2=12, H=12 for the conservative prototype/certification path.
2026-06-11 E5 K32000 checkpoint (experiments/precode/results/e5_K32000.csv, 5000 paired trials, committed d00eac5): baseline ldpcdense_s190_d12 OH0 fail=0.016600 vs dense 0.020000 with gen=176.7k, sparse=323.8k, GE=647.5k. shuffle2 alone is neutral and cheaper to generate: s190_d12_s2 OH0 fail=0.016400, gen=112.3k, sparse=318.9k, GE=652.0k. n12 alone saves generation vs baseline but is less reliable than the baseline hybrid: n12_s190_d12 OH0 fail=0.019600, OH1=0.001800, OH2=0.001800. The combined n12+shuffle2 variant is definitively bad at scale: fail=0.106200/OH0, 0.052200/OH1, 0.041600/OH2 with a visibly heavier deficit tail. D2=57 does not justify its GE cost: OH0 fail=0.016800 like baseline but GE grows to ~1.01M; OH1/OH2 zero failures in 5000 trials, but at much higher solve cost. Interim implementability verdict: shuffle2 alone is acceptable; do not combine n12 and shuffle2; if reducing n1 to 2 is desired, keep the received-row precode selection iid unless certification proves otherwise.
2026-06-11 final rule delivery: big-K grids, GE replay, rank_total scoring, and E5 implementability checks are complete and summarized in experiments/precode/results/SUMMARY.md. Conservative prototype/certification rule is S=GetDenseCount(K), D2=12, H=12: ldpcdense_s<GetDenseCount(K)>_d12_h12. At K64000 this gives OH0/OH1/OH2 fail 0.000667/0/0 in the H-grid, with -31.4% sparse XORs and -64.2% real GE bitops vs dense. rank_total at 1280-byte blocks puts the full H12 hybrid at about +2.6% total block ops vs dense at K64000 because of parity generation, while buying much lower failure tails; pure ldpc_h12 is cheaper but has no dense safety rows. E5 verdict: keep iid row selection for the primary candidate, optionally certify shuffle2 only with H=12, and reject n12 plus n12+shuffle2.
close_reason: Final dense-replacement rule and recommendation delivered. Summary appended in experiments/precode/results/SUMMARY.md; queue CSVs through E5 K64000 committed/pushed; conservative prototype/certification candidate is ldpcdense_s<GetDenseCount(K)>_d12_h12 with iid D2 row selection, optional shuffle2 only for H12 certification, and n12/n12+shuffle2 rejected. Phase-B certification continues in wirehair-brd after handoff/unit gate closure.

title: Finish adversarial bug-hunt: codec-ge region unreviewed + ~20 unverified candidates
description: The multi-agent bug hunt (June 2026) reviewed 12 code regions with 2-skeptic adversarial verification; 21+14 findings confirmed and fixed (see commits 'Fix confirmed API and gf256 bugs', 'Fix v2 bench methodology', 'Fix whx and tiling harness'). INCOMPLETE due to session/spend limits: (1) The codec-ge region was NEVER reviewed: WirehairCodec.cpp lines 1700-3100 (matrix compression, dense binary GE, heavy GF(256) elimination, Triangle, InitializeColumnValues) plus WirehairCodec.h lines 700-1387 — hunt for pivot bookkeeping, heavy-row index math, bitfield off-by-one, allocation sizing. (2) Candidates found but never adjudicated (titles truncated at 40 chars from the failure log): 'OverrideSeeds is sticky across re-initia...', 'DecodeFeed after all-original fast-path...', '32-bit overflow in size validation and b...' (ReconstructOutput ~3716, partially covered by the widening issue), 'ShuffleDeck16 with count == 0 underflows', 'Dense-seed fixup override ignores dense_...', 'TuneSeedProfile ranks peel seeds with a ...', 'RqccLowref solver applies boundary-max s...' (codec/WirehairV2Peel.cpp — possible mis-port of solver semantics vs peel_sweep methods), 'KsBmaxTop16 solver is a mis-port: wrong ...', 'ResidualRows accounting diverges from de...', 'AvalancheEstimate.Peeled and .SingletonR...', 'minrow_rand (method 88) draws its random...', 'RaptorQ LDPC struct-vs-randomized-contro...', 'Unbounded --overhead-pct triggers undefi...', 'startMode=1 (repair-only) samples at mos...', 'Small-block calibration runs cache-resid...' (xor_bench), 'Encode() truncates block_id to uint16 wh...' (the final-block-size aspect IS fixed; check for any other truncated use), 'WH_SEED_KNOBS WH_PEELCAP cache in Genera...'. Each needs fresh verification: read the code, check premises, write a repro if feasible, fix if real. Full prior results: the fixed findings are described in the three commit messages; treat the truncated titles as leads, not facts.
acceptance_criteria: codec-ge region reviewed with adversarial verification; each listed candidate confirmed+fixed or refuted with reasoning
notes:
2026-06-10 checkpoint: confirmed/fixed two seed/dense override leads while reviewing the codec GE path. (1) ShuffleDeck16 itself is now a no-op for count=0, and ChooseMatrix rejects invalid dense-count overrides before dense-row generation can enter non-advancing loops or overrun fixed dense arrays. Direct OverrideSeeds(0/501) now returns Wirehair_InvalidInput during InitializeEncoder. WH_SEED_KNOBS validation also rejects WH_DENSE=0, WH_DENSE beyond CAT_MAX_DENSE_ROWS, and WH_DENSE values that need an automatic dense seed but do not fit kDenseSeeds. (2) GetDenseSeed now applies per-N dense-seed fixups only when dense_count == GetDenseCount(N); non-default dense-count sweeps (e.g. v2 --dense-delta) now use the dense-count table seed instead of a fixup tuned for the default dense count. Added fast coverage in codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test. Validation: cmake --build build --target wirehair wirehair_v2_policy_test unit_test; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test; WH_SEED_KNOBS temporary harness for invalid overrides; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_bench compare --nlo 5550 --nhi 5550 --trials 1 --bb-list 64 --loss 0.1 --dense-delta -4 --seed 1 --max-message-mib 1; git diff --check. Broader codec-ge review and the remaining candidate list are still open.

2026-06-10 checkpoint: confirmed/fixed DecodeFeed after all-original fast-path. Repro: after feeding all original blocks, DecodeFeed returned Wirehair_Success without allocating GE state; a later valid repair block called ResumeSolveMatrix and crashed writing through missing GE maps. Fix: added an explicit _decode_complete guard, set it on every decode success path, reset it during encoder/decoder initialization for reuse, and return Wirehair_Success for later valid packets without mutating state. Added codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test coverage for all-original completion, post-success repair decode, recovery correctness, and decoder reuse resetting the completion flag. Validation: cmake --build build --target wirehair_v2_policy_test unit_test; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test; git diff --check; standalone ASan/UBSan repro no longer crashes and returns Wirehair_Success after the extra repair block, though it still reports existing unaligned word-access UB warnings in older xor paths.
2026-06-10 checkpoint: confirmed/fixed OverrideSeeds stickiness. Direct wirehair::Codec overrides were previously persistent because _seed_override stayed set after ChooseMatrix, so an invalid dense-count override poisoned later InitializeEncoder/InitializeDecoder attempts on the same object even when no override was requested. OverrideSeeds now applies to the next initialization attempt only; v2 and table-generator callers already set overrides immediately before each initialization they need. Added policy-test coverage that failed dense-count overrides do not persist. Validation: cmake --build build --target wirehair_v2_policy_test unit_test; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test; git diff --check. Bug-fix pass over v2 and generator callers found no further override-lifetime issue.
2026-06-10 checkpoint: refuted remaining block-id truncation lead. Encode() now accepts a uint32_t block_id and passes it through to PeelRowParameters::Initialize, which stores the row seed as uint32_t; the fixed final-block-size check compares the full 32-bit id. wirehair_decode passes the unsigned id through to DecodeFeed, and PeelRow::RecoveryId is uint32_t. wirehair_recover_block has the only public uint16_t narrowing, but it first rejects blockId > 0xffff and ReconstructBlock validates block_id < _block_count, so invalid large block ids cannot wrap to a valid original block. No code change needed beyond existing coverage.
2026-06-10 checkpoint: adjudicated the 32-bit size-validation/block-offset lead as covered by the large-message fix in 3f6b88f. Codec::_block_bytes is size_t, so ReconstructOutput's message-size check and all _block_bytes * index pointer expressions use pointer-sized arithmetic on 64-bit hosts; AllocateInput and AllocateWorkspace compute allocation bytes in uint64_t; ChooseMatrix now keeps only a 32-bit-size_t addressability guard for the largest intermediate block offset. No additional qn0 code change needed; wirehair-hph remains open only for the separate exhaustive unit_test acceptance caveat.
2026-06-10 checkpoint: refuted TuneSeedProfile ranking lead. The implementation matches codec/README.md: it evaluates H=0 rows=N plans, uses the same trial salts across candidate peel seeds, treats residual columns as an outlier filter, then ranks non-outliers by estimated XOR cost. BuildPeelSolvePlan includes block count, block bytes, row count, peel seed, dense seed/count, and policy fields in MatrixSeed, and EvaluatePeelingRows supplies the residual and TotalXorCost values being scored. This is a heuristic choice, not a correctness bug; compare auto mode separately validates tuned-vs-base before using tuned profiles in benchmarks.
2026-06-10 checkpoint: confirmed/fixed the RqccLowref and KsBmaxTop16 solver-port leads in codec/WirehairV2Peel.cpp. The production v2 evaluator was selecting all live degree-2 endpoints, sorting by low reference count, and applying boundary-max even for rqcc_lowref. The experiment harness semantics are different: rqcc_lowref uses the largest degree-2 component with low-reference scoring, while ks_bmax_top16 takes the largest degree-2 component, keeps the top 16 by default score (cumulative degree-2 weight, row refs, column), then boundary-scores that subset. Fix: added cumulative Weight2Refs tracking, largest degree-2 component collection, low-ref/default/boundary scoring helpers, KS min-row fallback, and regression matrices that fail the old behavior. Validation: cmake --build build --target wirehair_v2_policy_test unit_test; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test; cmake --build build --target wirehair_v2_bench; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_bench compare --nlo 64 --nhi 96 --trials 2 --bb-list 1280 --max-message-mib 16; git diff --check. Repeated bug-fix pass found and fixed one component-size narrowing, then found no further issues. ResidualRows/deferred-row accounting remains a separate qn0 lead.
2026-06-10 checkpoint: confirmed/fixed the ResidualRows accounting lead in the v2 peel evaluator. The evaluator was marking every live row incident to an inactivated column as residual before the peel cascade ran, but the reference peel_sweep simulator counts only rows that cannot be solved after deferred/peeled column avalanche. Fix: RowState now tracks Solved and Deferred, inactivation no longer pre-marks incident rows, singleton processing marks the solving row, and rows become residual only when they lose all live todo columns while unsolved. Candidate boundary scoring now separates static row refs used by low-ref/default scoring from live non-deferred refs used by boundary scoring, matching peel_sweep metric semantics. Added regression matrices for largest-component solver selection and for rows solved by the peel cascade not counting as residual. Validation: cmake --build build --target wirehair_v2_policy_test unit_test wirehair_v2_bench; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_bench compare --nlo 64 --nhi 96 --trials 2 --bb-list 1280 --max-message-mib 16; git diff --check. Repeated bug-fix passes fixed deferred-row candidate skipping and live-vs-static ref scoring, then found no further issues.
2026-06-10 checkpoint: refuted the AvalancheEstimate.Peeled/SingletonRows lead and confirmed/fixed the minrow_rand method-88 random tie lead. AvalancheEstimate is experimental-only and validates Removed against exact one-step copied simulation; Peeled and SingletonRows are redundant by construction because every additional removed column is introduced by the singleton row that exposed it, so both equal Removed - 1 and only affect redundant heuristic tie-break fields. For minrow_rand, the all-minimum-rows random tie previously hashed row_i as well as column_i, so a column appearing in multiple minimum rows received multiple random lottery tickets. Method 88 now deduplicates candidate columns and hashes only seed, column, and ChoiceCount for the primary random key, preserving the existing ExactD2/fill/column secondary tie-breaks. Added a PeelSim self-test with rows {0,1}/{0,2} and seed 6 that catches the old duplicate-column bias. Validation: ./experiments/peeling/build.sh; ./experiments/peeling/peel_sweep --self-test; ./experiments/peeling/peel_sweep --N 64 --methods 88 --structures lt_m2_c64 --trials 2 --threads 1 --seed 123 --overhead 0; git diff --check. Repeated bug-fix passes found and fixed only the initial test API placement issue, then found no further issues.
2026-06-10 checkpoint: refuted the RaptorQ LDPC structured-vs-randomized-control lead and confirmed/fixed the unbounded --overhead-pct lead. LDPC refutation: raptorq_ldpc_struct and raptorq_ldpc_rand both prepend S check rows before paired packet rows; the random-control path uses a separate seed stream for check rows only, preserves the packet-row RNG stream, emits unique-column random rows, and existing self-tests check row count, structured-vs-random difference, same check-row degrees, and identical paired packet rows. No code change needed for LDPC. Overhead fix: parse_double_list now rejects non-finite doubles such as nan/inf, and make_trial_config computes percentage and total row count in double with finite/range checks before converting, using the existing >65535 rejection path for oversized finite percentages. This avoids undefined casts/overflow from huge --overhead-pct values. Added self-test coverage for finite parsing, nan/inf rejection, valid 10pct row counts, and oversized finite pct rejection. Validation: ./experiments/peeling/build.sh; ./experiments/peeling/peel_sweep --self-test; invalid CLI checks for --overhead-pct inf and 1000000000; finite --overhead-pct 10 smoke; git diff --check. Repeated bug-fix passes found no further issues.
2026-06-10 checkpoint: confirmed/fixed the startMode=1 repair-only sampling lead in bench/whx.cpp. The shared roundtrip path and repro mode started repair-only trials at N + (rng.u32() % 16), so seed-search, ohead, scan, and fuzz repair-only coverage exercised only the first 16 repair-row offsets before walking forward. Added repair_start_block_id(), which samples a broad uint32 repair-id start in [N, UINT32_MAX - maxNeeded] so the subsequent delivered-block loop cannot wrap, and used it in roundtrip and repro. Left the count/memory-attribution path deterministic at N. Validation: bash bench/build.sh; ./bench/whx repro 0x1234 64 64 1 0.10 showed first repair id 2662708829 and decoded successfully; ./bench/whx ohead --threads 1 --trials 4 --bb 64 --startmode 1 --loss 0.10 --nlo 64 --nhi 64 --nstep 1; ./bench/whx fuzz --threads 1 --secs 1 --nmax 256 --seed 0x5150; git diff --check. Repeated bug-fix passes found no further issues.
2026-06-10 checkpoint: confirmed/fixed the small-block xor_bench cache-residency lead. The old default used only 8192 working blocks for <=4096-byte blocks, so the 1280-byte calibration touched about 10 MiB and could run from cache while being used as a memory-cost conversion. xor_bench now sizes the default working set by bytes: 256 MiB for every block size, with --working-mib for quick smoke runs. The parser now rejects non-finite --target-gib values, and working-set size arithmetic checks overflow before allocation. README documents the default; results/SUMMARY.md marks the existing calibration table as historical/stale for 1280-byte conversion. Validation: ./experiments/peeling/build.sh; xor_bench --sizes 1280 --target-gib 0.001 --repeats 1 --working-mib 8; xor_bench --sizes 1280 --target-gib 0.001 --repeats 1 (reported 209716 working blocks); invalid --target-gib nan and --working-mib 0 checks; git diff --check. Repeated bug-fix passes found the stale-summary documentation issue, fixed it, then found no further issues.
2026-06-10 checkpoint: refuted the WH_SEED_KNOBS/WH_PEELCAP generator-cache lead. GeneratePeelRowWeight caches WH_PEELCAP in a function-local static, but no generator or whx seed-search path mutates WH_PEELCAP inside a running process; searches that vary seeds use explicit Codec::OverrideSeeds or thread-local wh_set_override, and table generators construct fresh Codec objects with one-shot OverrideSeeds before each initialization. WH_PEELCAP is therefore a process-wide experimental row-weight cap selected at process start, not a per-trial knob expected to change dynamically. The earlier OverrideSeeds stickiness fix already ensures explicit seed overrides do not persist across codec reinitialization. No code change needed.
2026-06-10 checkpoint: confirmed/fixed a scalar BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal heavy-coefficient lead in WirehairCodec.cpp. Extra rows can be pivoted before _first_heavy_column, then InsertHeavyRows copies their later heavy-column coefficients into _heavy_matrix. The scalar tail was checking ge_up_i >= first_heavy_column before reading that coefficient, so those early-pivoted extra rows fell back to stale binary GE bits for heavy columns. The guard now keys off pivot_i >= first_heavy_column, matching the windowed back-sub path and the heavy_col_i index. Validation: cmake --build build --target wirehair wirehair_v2_policy_test unit_test; bash bench/build.sh; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test; ./bench/whx ohead --threads 1 --trials 20 --bb 64 --startmode 1 --loss 0.10 --nlo 64 --nhi 256 --nstep 64; ./bench/whx fuzz --threads 1 --secs 10 --nmax 2000 --seed 0xBADC0DE; git diff --check. A full ./build/unit_test executable run was started and stopped after about 6 minutes because it is an exhaustive N=2..64000 by blockBytes=1..65 sweep; it had only emitted existing overhead warnings before termination.
close_reason: Completed adversarial bug hunt. All listed leads were either fixed or refuted in notes; final codec-GE review confirmed and fixed the scalar heavy back-substitution guard, and found no further pivot-map, heavy-row, windowing, or dense-value bugs.

title: Widen 32-bit per-block offset arithmetic for >=4GiB messages (full fix)
description: CONFIRMED HIGH severity by two independent verifiers with live 8GiB repros. All per-block pointer arithmetic in WirehairCodec.cpp multiplies 32-bit 'unsigned _block_bytes' by a uint16 block index; the 32-bit product wraps for messages >= 4GiB (e.g. N=8192 x 1MiB: index 4096 wraps to offset 0), silently corrupting encode/decode while returning success. Sites include WirehairCodec.cpp lines ~652, 657, 714, 723, 1581, 1603, 1632, 1691, 1783, 1851, 1928, 2050, 2266, 2940, 3741, 4327, 4563 (grep for '_block_bytes \*'), plus the size validation expression near line 3716 in ReconstructOutput which also wraps. MITIGATION ALREADY SHIPPED (commit 'Fix confirmed API and gf256 bugs'): ChooseMatrix now rejects messages whose intermediate span (N + CAT_MAX_DENSE_ROWS + kHeavyRows + 1) * block_bytes >= 2^32, and the header documents the precondition. FULL FIX: widen every product to size_t/uint64_t (pattern: (size_t)_block_bytes * index), fix line ~3716 validation, then relax/remove the ChooseMatrix guard and the header precondition. Verify with: 8GiB round-trip (N=8192, 1MiB blocks, drop a block, decode from repair) on a big-RAM host, plus full ./build/unit_test.
acceptance_criteria: 8GiB encode/decode round-trip recovers bit-exact; unit_test passes; guard removed or relaxed
notes:
2026-06-10 test harness checkpoint: added opt-in environment controls to ./build/unit_test so acceptance warnings can be reproduced without an exhaustive run: WIREHAIR_UNIT_SEED, WIREHAIR_UNIT_MIN_N/MAX_N, WIREHAIR_UNIT_BLOCK_BYTES_MIN/MAX, WIREHAIR_UNIT_BENCH_TRIALS, and WIREHAIR_UNIT_SKIP_BENCH. Defaults preserve the historical full behavior. Validation: cmake --build build --target unit_test; focused repro env OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 WIREHAIR_UNIT_SKIP_BENCH=1 WIREHAIR_UNIT_SEED=1781109278852559 WIREHAIR_UNIT_MIN_N=1096 WIREHAIR_UNIT_MAX_N=1096 WIREHAIR_UNIT_BLOCK_BYTES_MIN=60 WIREHAIR_UNIT_BLOCK_BYTES_MAX=60 ./build/unit_test reproduced the exact '9 extra' warning and exited 0; small N=2..4/blockBytes=1..3 skip-bench smoke passed; WIREHAIR_UNIT_MIN_N=0 rejected as invalid; WIREHAIR_UNIT_BENCH_TRIALS=1 smoke with benchmarks enabled passed. This confirms the observed overhead lines are warning-only output, not the reason full acceptance is pending.
2026-06-10 8GiB acceptance PASS: added explicit non-default CMake target large_message_test (commit 7ac62cd) and ran ./build/large_message_test with defaults: N=8192, block_bytes=1048576, message_bytes=8589934592, drop_block=4096. Decode succeeded after 1 repair block and verified all 8192 recovered blocks bit-exact. Log: /tmp/large_message_test.log. Remaining gate before close: running full ./build/unit_test must exit 0.
2026-06-26 closeout: full unit_test gate satisfied for seed 1782150870029327, N=2..64000, blockBytes=1..65, skip-bench. Authoritative status files include /tmp/wirehair_unit_shards2 for 2..48000 and 56001..64000; 48001..56000 covered by /tmp/wirehair_unit_shards2 log through N=53878 plus /tmp/wirehair_unit_tail_48001_supp4 status=0 shards for 52238..56000. 8GiB acceptance had already passed.
close_reason: 8GiB acceptance and unit gate passed

title: V2 research: peeling matrix structure/algorithm improvements (prove out in peel_sweep)
description: Design and experimentally validate improvements to the v2 peeling matrix structure and inactivation scheduling. Candidates: CRN-paired structure comparisons, incremental avalanche scoring, LDPC-precode-style rows, better dense-residual cost model.
notes: 2026-06-10 E8 VERDICT: KILLED decisively. Deck-dealt column-degree regularization (exact balance, max-min<=1) at equal edges is 1.7x worse at N=320, 3.7x at N=3200, 10.5x at N=32000 (deck_lt_m2_c256_fold 1269 vs iid 121 mean residual cols, OH0, ks_bmax_top16, 400 paired trials). deckloss10/30 identical to pure deck (the regularization itself harms, not a fragility artifact); p2c soft variant also ~9x worse. Mechanism: irregular (Poisson) column degrees are necessary for peel avalanches. E8 hypothesis (high-cap advantage = removing low-coverage tail) REFUTED. Results: e8_deck_seed1.csv. E2 VERDICT: PROMOTED — pct-overhead frontier was stale; lt_m2_c2048_fold60 beats it 49% at N=32000 pct1 (21.83 vs 42.55, 4/4 seeds); no interior fold optimum (monotone in fold and cap at pct>=1); per-edge frontier still held by plain _fold family (c1024_fold); d2_003/d3_003 mass catastrophic at N=32000. ks_bmax_top16 best method in every cell (validates v2 policy solver). e2_foldcap_seed1-4.csv.
close_reason: Completed the peeling-structure research pass: E8 deck/regularization was killed decisively, E2 fold/cap was promoted and recorded, ks_bmax_top16 was validated as the v2 policy solver, and the LDPC-precode/dense-residual follow-up is now carried by wirehair-edj/wirehair-evq and downstream prototype/certification tasks. Result CSVs and summaries are already committed.

title: V2 research: dense matrix replacement alternatives
description: Evaluate replacing the v2/production dense binary rows (+heavy rows) with faster or more effective alternatives: structured LDPC precode, M4RI dense GE, smaller dense count with better peel residual handling, circulant dense rows.
notes: Substantially advanced June 2026 — see experiments/precode/ (simulator + README + results/SUMMARY.md). Headline: LDPC-staircase (+ small dense hybrid) replaces the dense matrix at equal reliability with 12-63% cost cuts growing with K; heavy 6->12 (explicit Cauchy, wirehair-wuw) cuts failure 10-100x. Continuation filed as wirehair-edj.

title: V2 review: bug hunt across codec, V2 scaffold, and experiment harnesses
description: User-requested comprehensive bug hunt over WirehairCodec, wirehair.cpp API, gf256, V2 codec files, peel_sweep harness, and benches. Findings verified adversarially before reporting.
close_reason: Hunt executed via two multi-agent workflow runs (92+94 agents): 12 regions, 40+ candidates, 2-skeptic adversarial verification. 30+ confirmed findings fixed across three commits (production API/codec, gf256, v2 bench, whx/tiling harnesses); high-severity: 4GiB offset wrap (mitigated, full fix wirehair-hph), uint16 block-count truncation, OOM null deref. Remaining: codec-ge region + ~20 unverified candidates -> wirehair-qn0.

title: Loop dense seed count co-tuning experiments
description: Continue v2 optimization loop by co-tuning dense row count and dense seed candidates in isolated codec benchmark tooling. Validate with paired seeds at multiple loss rates and block sizes, run repeated code-reading bug checks after edits, and record whether any candidate provides a robust overhead and throughput win.
close_reason: Added densegrid paired dense-count/dense-seed sweep mode and compare-mode dense overrides. Repeated code-reading and smoke validation found no robust dense-count/seed candidate suitable for default policy: densegrid produced local candidates, but direct compare validation under the baseline-v2 random-loss protocol regressed or only traded overhead for slower decode. Production seed policy left unchanged.

title: Loop on v2 peel dense optimization wins
description: Iterate on v2 optimization experiments around peel structure, dense matrix composition/size, seed selection, and benchmark protocol. Identify likely wins, implement isolated codec/ tooling or policy changes, run focused comparisons, bug-check edited code after each change, and record whether each iteration improves overhead or processing time.
close_reason: Implemented and tested two optimization-loop tools in codec/: compare --v2-profile auto with a conservative auto-min-delta threshold to avoid rare-event overfitting, and densecount to sweep dense row count deltas with paired seeds. Experiments found no universal peel/tuned-seed win; tuned seed calibration overfits unless thresholded. Dense count is a real tradeoff knob: reducing dense rows can improve create/decode throughput (e.g. N=320/100KiB dense -16 and N=12000/1280 dense -8), but candidates were loss-regime dependent and did not produce a universally safe overhead win. N=3200 dense -16 failed, establishing a safety bound.

title: Find baseline vs v2 codec differentiator
description: Run additional baseline-vs-v2 experiments across loss rates, N/block-byte bands, and loss patterns until there is a clear differentiator or evidence that the current v2 wrapper is functionally equivalent for end-to-end codec behavior. Record extra symbols, failures, and processing time.
close_reason: Added a tuned-profile compare option and ran fixed-N baseline vs v2_tuned sweeps. Found the differentiator: base v2 is seed-equivalent to baseline, while tuned v2 changes overhead distributions. Tuned v2 improved some bands (e.g. N=12000/bb=1280: 0.030 -> 0.010 mean OH; capped 1MiB: 0.050 -> 0.000) but worsened others (e.g. N=12000/capped 100KiB: 0.020 -> 0.060; N=320/bb=1280: 0.014 -> 0.026). Throughput remained mostly within noise or slightly worse for tuned v2 on large payloads.

title: Run 1000-trial baseline v2 comparison
description: Run 1000 paired random-loss experiments with different seeds for each baseline/v2 parameter set and report extra symbols plus processing time.
close_reason: Ran 1000 paired baseline/v2 random-loss experiments for each block-byte parameter set: 1280, 102400, and 1048576 bytes, each with independent seed streams. All cohorts completed with no decode failures; results reported extra-symbol distribution and approximate per-message processing time.

title: Run v2 tuning and comparison experiments

Ran selected 25 structures x 15 methods at N=1000,6400,32000 with N-jitter=10, fixed overheads 0/1/2, paired matrix seeds, and 400 trials per aggregate row. title: Add XOR-cost metrics to peeling sweep comparisons

notes: Implemented XOR-cost accounting in experiments/peeling/peel_sweep.cpp. Added row-generation metrics (matrix_refs_mu, row_refs_mu, row_xors_mu, matrix_xors_mu), sparse solve block-XOR counting, dense residual block-XOR estimate, and combined solve estimate. Bug-fix pass found and fixed a sparse-solve accounting miss for rows that consume columns already peeled before the row is added; added self-test coverage for that case. Ran N=3200 frontier rerun: 27 structures x 13 methods x OH 0/1/2/4/8 x 4 seed families x 100 trials. CSV integrity passed: 20 files, 351 rows each, expected metadata, no stderr logs. Residual means matched old full sweep to floating-point roundoff. Summary updated with XOR-cost Pareto readout: residual-only high-cap winners remain best for dense residual size, but lt_m2_c512/lt_m2_c512_fold and lt_m2_c1024 variants are better tradeoffs when block XOR budget matters, often saving roughly 10-18% combined XOR proxy for less than one extra residual column. Validation passed: optimized build, self-test, ASan/UBSan self-test, ASan/UBSan smoke sweep, and git diff --check.

title: Run N=3200 peeling recipe ablation sweep
description: Repeat the N=320 recipe ablation protocol for the N=3200 cohort: add any N=3200-specific structure aliases needed for a fair cohort-scale comparison, run all 115 methods over fixed N=3200 with overhead 0/1/2/4/8, 4 seed families x 100 trials, paired matrix seeds, validate with self-tests/sanitizer smoke/CSV integrity checks, document top exact structure+method results and caveats, and close the issue.
notes: Completed the N=3200 recipe ablation sweep. Normal sparse bucket: 76 structures x 115 methods x OH 0/1/2/4/8 x 4 seed families x 100 trials = 174800 result rows. High-row smoke: 3520 rows. Dense binary smoke: 140 rows. CSV integrity passed with fixed N=3200, N-jitter=0, random-paired source, PDFs present, and expected method/structure coverage. Final validation passed: optimized build, self-test, ASan/UBSan self-test, ASan/UBSan representative smoke sweep, and git diff --check. Results appended to experiments/peeling/results/SUMMARY.md. Strict zero-overhead winner is lt_m2_c1920 + rqcc_dup at 35.023 mean residual columns, with raptorq_d2cc/rqd2_default effectively tied at 35.030 and about 2x faster. Other overhead winners: +1 lt_m2_c3200 + rqd2_default, +2 lt_m2_c1024_fold25 + rqd2_default, +4 lt_m2_c1024 + rqd2_default, +8 lt_m2_c1280 + rqd2_default. Fixed high-row base-distribution bug: ordinary rows in kStructureLTHighRows now use LT weights instead of default 1/d; normal sparse results were unaffected because high-row structures were smoke-only.
close_reason: N=3200 recipe ablation sweep completed, validated, summarized, and recorded.
