Validate new structures and report top results/caveats.
close_reason: Completed N=320 recipe ablation sweep: added N=320-focused structures, ran all-method zero-overhead and +1/+2/+4/+8 overhead sweeps over 4x100 trials, validated with harness self-tests, ASan/UBSan self-test/smoke, CSV integrity checks, and documented results in experiments/peeling/results/SUMMARY.md.

title: Audit high-cap LT peeling results for bugs or missed confounders
description: Thoroughly audit the high-cap LT peeling experiment for implementation bugs, protocol confounders, and statistical artifacts. Check folded-tail math, seed pairing, structure comparison fairness, row degree/edge budgets, output parsing, and focused-method coverage. Run targeted validation experiments where needed and update notes/results if any caveat or bug is found.
notes: Audit findings: no implementation bug found in folded-tail math, seed parsing, trial aggregation, or residual reporting. Important caveats found and recorded in experiments/peeling/results/SUMMARY.md: high-cap quality comparison is equal-row, not equal sparse-edge/XOR; high caps have higher expected row degree; naive equal-edge rerun is invalid when it reduces rows below N; structure comparisons use different structure-name-derived matrix streams, though four seed-family reruns at N=32000 preserved ranking; c512/c1024 clamp to the same distribution at N<=cap, so small-N differences are Monte Carlo noise; all-method audit completed for c256_fold/c512_fold and was stopped in known-slow methods during c1024_fold, with no completed-method surprise winner.
close_reason: Audit completed; caveats documented and no implementation bug found

title: S2: AVX-512 ZMM widening of gf256_add_mem/add2_mem/addset_mem/memswap
description: Add 512-bit XOR loops with 4-8 independent accumulators; keep AVX2 for small/cold buffers. Memory-bound but halves loop overhead, better store coalescing. Expect 5-25% on large blocks. (codex P3)

comments.author: catid
comments.text: S2 IMPLEMENTED & VERIFIED (0 fuzz fails, 287K+181K trials incl add2_mem exhaustive). Kernel speedup (clean 64thr): add_mem/addset/add2 1.3-1.76x at 4-16KB, ~1.0x at >=64KB (bandwidth-bound). END-TO-END: ~0 (perf stat bb=8192 N=4096: instructions 5.90B->3.35B (-43%) but cycles only -1.8%, IPC 0.94->0.54). CODEC IS MEMORY-BOUND (22% L1 miss, scattered recovery-block working set). Confirmed at 1 thread AND 128 threads. KEEP (correct, -43% insns/power) but not a speed lever. Real levers: reduce memory traffic / work.

title: S1: GFNI gf2p8affineqb constant-multiply for gf256_mul_mem & gf256_muladd_mem (poly 0x14D)
description: Replace split-nibble pshufb with vgf2p8affineqb on ZMM (64B/instr). Precompute 256 8x8 affine matrices for poly 0x14D at init. Matrix: m|=((gf256_mul(c,1<<i)>>j)&1)<<(8*j+i). Sanity: matrix[0]==0, matrix[1]==0x8040201008040201, exhaustive c,x vs gf256_mul. Runtime GFNI dispatch + AVX2 fallback. Expect mul/muladd 1.5-3x, macro decode +10-30%. (codex P1)

comments.author: catid
comments.text: S1 IMPLEMENTED & VERIFIED. Bug found+fixed during bug-hunt: gf2p8affineqb uses M.byte[7-i] (Intel SDM), so row j -> matrix byte (7-j); true identity is 0x0102040810204080 (codex's 0x8040201008040201 was wrong). Exhaustive selfcheck (256 consts x 256 vals x 7 offsets) PASS; end-to-end fuzz 89632 trials 0 fails. Kernel speedup (clean, 64thr L1/L2): mul_mem 2.0-2.8x, muladd_mem 1.5-2.2x. End-to-end (low-contention 4thr): ~neutral, within noise -- GF ops are small fraction of codec time. KEEP (no downside, helps GF-heavy regimes). Next: profile to find real bottleneck.

title: H2: Instrumentation counters (defer/dense/pivot counts, GF muladd bytes, overhead p99/p999)
description: Add macro-gated counters in WirehairCodec for _defer_count,_dense_count,_pivot_count, failed pivot index, binary row XOR count, GF256 muladd byte count; harness reports mean extra blocks, p99/p999 overhead, NeedMore rate at exactly N. Makes every ablation falsifiable. (codex P0)
close_reason: Closed

title: Use MSVC-safe environment access in strict targets
description: The strict MSVC matrix enables /W4 /WX, but core and registered test sources call getenv directly. MSVC diagnoses getenv as C4996 at /W3, so the first native Windows build would fail before CTest. Active uses include WirehairTools.cpp, wirehair.cpp/gf256.cpp testing paths, UnitTest.cpp, and LargeMessageTest.cpp.
acceptance_criteria: Introduce one portable environment-read helper that uses a warning-clean secure API on MSVC and standard behavior elsewhere; convert all strict-target getenv uses without blanket warning suppression; unit-test unset/empty/value/numeric parsing behavior and compile every affected target under strict GCC/Clang plus pushed native MSVC /W4 /WX; run CTest and relevant environment-driven E2Es, then complete a clean bug-review pass.
notes:
Paused mid-implementation at user request for server restart. Partial shared-worktree changes add WirehairEnvironment.h, CMake source entries, production call-site conversions, and LegacyCoreTest environment coverage. On resume, verify _dupenv_s uses a valid size_t output pointer, test unset/empty/value behavior, convert every strict-target getenv call, scan other C4996 APIs, run strict local gates, and leave native /W4 /WX closure to the first pushed Actions matrix.
Resume implementation complete; awaiting hosted MSVC only. WirehairEnvironment.h now uses a real std::size_t output (_dupenv_s(&Owned, &length, name)) and frees the returned CRT allocation, preserving nullptr for unset and the exact string for empty/value. All strict-target callers use EnvironmentValue; focused LegacyCore coverage checks mutation success plus unset/value and an actual empty value where the process environment represents one, while UnitTest covers unset/empty/default retention, decimal, hexadecimal, UINT64_MAX, negative/overflow/trailing-garbage rejection, and unchanged outputs on rejection. Static C4996 audit: no _CRT_SECURE_NO_WARNINGS or C4996 pragma; the only remaining getenv token is the helper's non-MSVC branch; WH_OPLOG uses fopen_s on MSVC; remaining fopen is the non-MSVC branch and open/read/close are LINUX_ARM-only.

Exact local verification on the resumed worktree: strict GCC 13 static and Clang 18 shared ci/run_ci.py matrix lanes each passed 21/21 CTests and 3/3 installed C/C++/plugin package E2Es; both quick large-message suites passed success/repeat plus exact terminal, repair-cap, timeout, and resource-cap failures; Clang shared installed native Python E2E passed. Separate GCC/Clang -Werror builds with WH_SEED_KNOBS=1 and WH_OPLOG=1 compiled legacy_core_test, unit_test, large_message_test, and legacy_c_consumer, then executed the consumer with WH_PEELCAP=63 and WH_OPLOG_PATH and produced the exact operation-log header. MinGW strict static/shared ABI/package lane passed, and a BUILD_TESTS=ON cross build compiled legacy_core_test.exe, unit_test.exe, and large_message_test.exe. An x86_64-w64-mingw32 strict syntax probe with _MSC_VER=1930 compiled the actual helper _dupenv_s branch, confirming the signature and valid size pointer. git diff --check passes. Repeated source review found no remaining environment/C4996 exposure in strict MSVC targets. Do not close until the pushed hosted MSVC /W4 /WX matrix and Windows CTest are green.
Independent resumed review found and fixed a standards-level lifetime gap: the non-MSVC branch retained std::getenv's raw pointer even though a later environment lookup may overwrite it, and WirehairCodec intentionally holds multiple values concurrently. EnvironmentValue now owns a malloc-backed, non-throwing immutable snapshot on every platform while preserving nullptr for unset and a one-byte owned string for empty; MSVC continues to use _dupenv_s with a real size_t output and frees the allocation through the same CRT-facing helper. LegacyCoreTest now proves the first of two differently named snapshots remains unchanged after the second lookup and a source-environment mutation, in addition to unset/empty/value coverage. Independent verification: fresh strict GCC 13 static and Clang 18 shared full builds were warning-clean; each passed focused unit_test, all four legacy core modes, and large_message_fast 6/6; strict MinGW rebuilt legacy_core_test.exe, unit_test.exe, and large_message_test.exe; and an x86_64-w64-mingw32 -D_MSC_VER=1930 -Werror syntax probe compiled the actual _dupenv_s branch. Static deprecated-CRT audit found no suppression and no remaining unsafe API in an MSVC matrix target: getenv/fopen/open/read/close tokens are confined to protected non-MSVC/Linux branches; bench/whx.cpp's legacy sprintf/fopen calls are outside the CMake/MSVC matrix. git diff --check and a clean source pass succeeded. Hosted native MSVC /W4 /WX plus Windows CTest remains the only closure gate.
Hosted run 29066341405 for exact SHA 337cc0d73e79f6b1ad64e0e4dfa7449250e33c90 passed both native MSVC target-scoped /W4 /WX full graphs and Windows CTest 19/19. This natively compiled and ran secure _dupenv_s access plus the unset/empty/value/snapshot/numeric environment coverage without a deprecated-CRT warning. Static job 86278561261 and shared job 86278561264 both succeeded. Run: https://github.com/catid/wirehair/actions/runs/29066341405.
close_reason: Implemented, independently reviewed, locally verified, and accepted by the all-green 9-job hosted run 29066341405 at exact SHA 337cc0d73e79f6b1ad64e0e4dfa7449250e33c90, including native MSVC static/shared end-to-end coverage.

title: Make active audit shell workflows relocation-safe and ShellCheck-clean
description: Focused ShellCheck on the active, audit-modified scripts reports a hard-coded repository path in the precode queue, ambiguous variable expansion in verify_fix, and a redundant numeric-validation pattern. The queue cleanup trap intentionally captures a local value but lacks an explanatory targeted suppression.
acceptance_criteria: Derive the queue repository root from the script location, make verify_fix expansion and positive-integer validation unambiguous, document the intentional trap-time capture locally, pass ShellCheck warning/error severity on all active modified scripts, and rerun the queue and benchmark self-tests from outside the repository working directory.
notes: Queue derives ROOT from its script path and succeeds from /tmp. Clarified intentional local-value capture for EXIT cleanup. Removed the redundant zero-field pattern and braced the regex expansion in verify_fix. Focused ShellCheck warning/error gate is clean; queue promotion self-test, verify_fix 7 scenarios, and whx 33/36-assertion self-tests all pass from outside the repository. Re-review found no semantic change to field validation or cleanup.
close_reason: Closed

title: Bound the dense-table reference interpolation loop
description: Cppcheck reports that tables/TableGenerator.cpp FindDenseCount_Ref iterates i < kDensePointCount while reading kDensePoints[i + 1]. Current valid inputs return before the final iteration because of the endpoint invariant, but the loop itself permits an out-of-bounds read if that invariant changes or invalid input reaches a release build.
acceptance_criteria: Iterate only over valid adjacent point pairs, retain exact interpolation results at both endpoints and every supported N, run the table generator comparison/smoke path under ASan/UBSan, and rerun focused cppcheck with no out-of-bounds diagnostic.
notes: Changed the reference interpolation loop to require a valid adjacent pair (i + 1 < count) and extended exhaustive comparison through the upper endpoint N=64000. Full gen_tables --no-bench --heavy-trials 0 passed under ASan/UBSan; focused cppcheck is clean. A repeated boundary review found no further defect.
close_reason: Closed

title: Make Python unit tests importable from repository-root discovery
description: Root-level unittest module execution fails because precode and table tests import sibling implementation modules only as top-level names. This makes the suites dependent on the current working directory and prevents reliable aggregate discovery.
acceptance_criteria: Both tests support package-relative and direct-script execution. Run repository-root unittest discovery for all Python suites and the existing directory-local invocations; all must pass without PYTHONPATH manipulation.
notes:
Added package-relative imports with direct-execution fallback. Verified 41 root-imported tests, 27 directory-local precode tests, 5 directory-local table tests, and 3 byte-validator tests without PYTHONPATH changes.
Follow-up root aggregate found and fixed the byte-metric validator's sibling-only import with the same package/direct fallback. Final repository-root aggregate passes 55/55 tests, and directory-local byte validation passes 3/3 without PYTHONPATH changes.
close_reason: All Python suites now support repository-root package imports and directory-local execution; final aggregate is 55/55.

title: Keep core, tests, and explicit tools warning-clean under strict builds
description:
## Problem
Caller-supplied strict warning builds fail in production/core and explicitly enabled tooling, preventing -Werror quality gates and masking future compiler diagnostics.

## Evidence
GCC reports unannotated intentional fallthroughs at WirehairTools.cpp:444-450 and the duplicated shuffle code at tables/TableGenerator.cpp:1073-1079. test/UnitTest.cpp:101 also exposes an MSVC-only pragma to non-MSVC compilers, and optimized V2 currently has the separately tracked uninitialized-state warning. A verified -Werror core/gen_tables build fails.

## Steps to Reproduce
Configure with strict GCC or Clang warnings promoted to errors, build the core/tests, then explicitly build gen_tables and other tool targets.

## Scope
Make intentional fallthrough and compiler-specific pragmas portable in C++11, resolve warnings at their source, and avoid broad suppressions. Keep copied shuffle implementations consistent or consolidate only if that reduces real duplication safely. Coordinate the V2 uninitialized diagnostic with wirehair-4kn.
acceptance_criteria: Add strict GCC and Clang configurations using -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Werror (plus appropriate MSVC warning-as-error coverage) for the library, all registered tests, V2, and every explicitly enabled generator/benchmark target. Add a compile gate that proves no warning is hidden by blanket project-wide suppression. Run the full CTest suite, bounded smoke commands for explicitly built tools, and an installed C consumer encode/drop/decode/recover round trip under the strict build. Complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes:
Reopened during final acceptance audit because the original Windows matrix omitted explicit generators/benchmark. ci/run_ci.py now detects strict Visual Studio jobs, enables WIREHAIR_BUILD_TOOLS and WIREHAIR_BUILD_BENCHMARKS for both static/shared configurations, builds the full graph under /W4 /WX, and smoke-runs gen_small_dseeds, gen_peel_seeds, gen_most_dseeds, gen_dcounts, gen_tables, and wirehair_v2_bench with the established bounded arguments. Runner tests cover MSVC enablement and non-MSVC default omission (11/11); strict GCC/Clang explicit-tool gates and MinGW affected builds pass. Close only after pushed native MSVC static/shared build/smoke/CTest succeeds.
Final pre-push runner suite is 17/17 after native export-gate additions; the strict VS static/shared explicit-tool behavior remains directly covered. The hosted logs must still prove all six bounded smokes under /W4 /WX.
First hosted native MSVC evidence (run 29064885216, SHA cab00638dc1e9a487846aac55ef94225cadf236b) failed at the initial full build and proved this bead is not yet complete. /WX exposed 65 unique WirehairCodec.cpp warning sites: C4267 size_t narrowing into gf256 int lengths and unsigned fields, C4244 uint64_t remainder narrowing, plus C4127 for the compile-time size_t-width branch. Separately, the runner replaced CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS and dropped /EHsc, causing C4530 in gen_tables. Resolve with target-scoped strict options, a checked GF256 int-byte helper tied to ChooseMatrix's <= INT_MAX invariant, explicit safe unsigned conversions, and a preprocessor-selected 32-bit overflow guard; no blanket warning suppression.
Resolved the first native /W4 /WX failure without suppressions. Added target-scoped WIREHAIR_STRICT_WARNINGS so MSVC retains /EHsc; propagated strictness through registered and direct package consumers. All 57 reported size_t-to-int GF256 calls now use a centralized conversion guarded by ChooseMatrix's <=INT_MAX input policy; all ten other bounded conversions are explicit; the size-width check is a compile-time 32-bit guard. Independent diagnostic inventory and source review found every C4267/C4244/C4127/C4530 site addressed. Final local evidence: strict GCC/Clang main matrices CTest 21/21 each, full tools+benchmark graphs CTest 22/22 each, package consumers 3/3, Python 66/66, ASan/UBSan/LSan 20/20, and MinGW static/shared exact ABI all pass. Awaiting pushed native MSVC static/shared full build, six tool smokes, Windows CTest/install/package.
Second native MSVC run 29065564699 proves target-scoped strict flags and all 69 first-run fixes work. Full compilation progressed to newly exposed test/V2 issues only: 11 LegacyCoreTest vector.size() to uint32_t API capacities, an int-valued std::fill into vector<uint8_t>, and missing direct <iterator> for std::back_inserter in WirehairV2PrecodeEncode.cpp. No first-run diagnostic recurred. Fix all affected typed calls/includes and rerun full local/hosted gates; the jobs still did not reach tool smokes, profiles, CTest, install, dumpbin, package, or Python native phases.
Second-run warning fixes and proactive dependent-target audit complete. All reported LegacyCore C4267/STL C4244 sites, both missing back_inserter includes, every V2 byte-fill specialization, both RNG integer-to-double conversions, and package constant-size/API-width sites are explicit and warning-clean. Fresh GCC/Clang strict full tools builds each pass 22/22 CTests plus all six tool smokes; cl-compatible /W4 /WX cross syntax probes pass the changed x64 Windows surfaces; MinGW package/ABI and focused ASan Legacy/V2 8/8 pass. Independent repeated bug-review passes found no remaining likely native MSVC blocker.
Hosted run 29066341405 for exact SHA 337cc0d73e79f6b1ad64e0e4dfa7449250e33c90 passed all strict compiler/linkage jobs. Native MSVC static/shared built the complete /W4 /WX graph without warnings, ran all six bounded generator/benchmark smokes successfully, passed exact quick large-message success/failure profiles, Windows CTest 19/19, install, and strict package consumers 3/3. Four strict Linux compiler/linkage jobs, MinGW, and sanitizer jobs also passed. Run: https://github.com/catid/wirehair/actions/runs/29066341405; raw static/shared evidence: https://nightly.link/catid/wirehair/runs/86278561261.txt and https://nightly.link/catid/wirehair/runs/86278561264.txt.
close_reason: Implemented, independently reviewed, locally verified, and accepted by the all-green 9-job hosted run 29066341405 at exact SHA 337cc0d73e79f6b1ad64e0e4dfa7449250e33c90, including native MSVC static/shared end-to-end coverage.

title: Register scalable high-N and large-block end-to-end CTests
description:
## Motivation
The repository has a useful large-message round-trip executable, but it is excluded from the default build and not registered with CTest. Routine coverage is capped at N=256 and block bytes=32, leaving maximum-N and memory-scaling paths manual.

## Evidence
CMakeLists.txt:154-159 registers a small unit_test profile but declares large_message_test EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL with no add_test. The audit had to build/run it explicitly; N=1000, block_bytes=257 passed, and separate N=64000 coverage remains manual.

## Scope
Create resource-bounded routine and scheduled integration profiles that vary both N and block_bytes, with deterministic loss/reordering, repair limits, timeouts, labels, and clear skip/resource policy.
acceptance_criteria: Register a fast default end-to-end profile that builds automatically and performs encode/drop/repair/decode/recover with full byte verification. Add scheduled profiles covering N=64000, partial-final messages, representative 1-byte/packet-size/large-block sizes, and memory pressure based on N*block_bytes without requiring an 8 GiB default allocation. Assert terminal decode errors and timeouts fail. Run all profiles in normal and applicable sanitizer builds, record peak memory/time bounds, and complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes:
Reworked test/LargeMessageTest.cpp into a resource-bounded public-API E2E harness. Safe standalone/default CTest uses N=256, block=257, a 113-byte partial final block, five deterministic seeded losses, reordered delivery, bounded repairs, encode/decode/recover, exact packet-length checks, and full regenerated-byte verification. N*block_bytes is checked against an explicit payload cap before allocation; internal deadlines and CTest timeouts bound runtime. Terminal decode errors, repair-cap exhaustion, injected timeout, actual wall-deadline expiration, malformed configuration, and resource rejection all return nonzero. The harness logs phase time and peak RSS. Added executable test/run_large_message_profiles.sh with deterministic replay comparison, exact expected-failure diagnostics, GNU-time portability, and quick/scheduled modes; added test/LARGE_MESSAGE_TEST.md with policy, commands, and measured bounds. Coordinated CMake integration: large_message_test now builds by default; 4 default CTests carry e2e/large-message/fast-or-failure labels and 15-45s timeouts; WIREHAIR_ENABLE_SCHEDULED_TESTS=ON adds serial N=64000 one-byte, N=4096 packet/partial, and N=32 1MiB-block/partial 32MiB-payload profiles with 210-330s timeouts. CTest JSON property assertions verified exactly 4 default and 3 scheduled tests. CI scheduled lane enables the option and runs the scheduled label. Final normal validation: default CTests 4/4 in 0.01s; all runner profiles PASS. Release measurements: fast 0.00s/3MiB, N64000 0.06s/26-27MiB, packet 0.01s/29MiB, large block 0.10s/204MiB. Actual 1ms deadline on the large profile exited 3 after 22ms with the expected timeout. GCC ASan+UBSan all 7 CTests passed in 0.67s: fast ~10MiB, high-N/packet ~40MiB, large block ~239MiB, no sanitizer findings. Repeated review passes found and fixed repair-ID overflow/unbounded loss logging and runner/env/RSS portability details; the final full code/config/script/doc pass found no remaining bug.
Independent follow-up replaced permissive WILL_FAIL cases with exact rc+diagnostic wrappers, rejects sanitizer/signal failures, and added a runner-contract selftest for wrong rc, sanitizer output, and missing deterministic digests. The stricter gates pass in strict GCC/Clang matrices and full post-fix Clang ASan/UBSan/LSan 20/20; scheduled high-N/packet/large-block profiles remain green.
close_reason: All profiles and exact failure oracles are implemented; deterministic, scheduled, strict, and full sanitizer matrices pass after independent review.

title: Bound whx benchmark workers by rounds and memory budget
description:
## Problem
whx bench spawns every resolved thread and each worker allocates N*block_bytes buffers and performs a full warmup before consulting the shared work queue, even when there is only one measured round. Small jobs can consume huge memory/work with no throughput benefit.

## Evidence
bench/whx.cpp:897-915 creates all requested workers and performs per-thread setup/warmup before atomic round assignment. --rounds 1 with many threads still pays every worker's allocation and warmup.

## Scope
Plan active_workers=min(requested_threads,total_rounds), then enforce a documented configurable memory budget derived with checked per-worker allocation arithmetic. Only workers that can receive measured work should allocate/warm; reject configurations that cannot support one worker.
acceptance_criteria: Unit-test worker planning for zero/one/many rounds, requested thread boundaries, per-worker size overflow, exact/insufficient memory budgets, and hardware-thread fallback. End to end, compare --rounds 1 at one versus many requested threads: results must match while active workers, peak RSS, and warmup work remain near the one-worker case. Run representative multi-round scaling to show throughput is not regressed, plus ASan/UBSan and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Independent review found the original 4*N*bb+bb estimate undercounted codec metadata and matrices by orders of magnitude for small blocks. Replaced it with overflow-checked worst-case encoder/decoder workspace, GE matrix, pivot, staging, and explicit-buffer accounting, including WH_ALIGN64 pitch. Self-tests cover exact/insufficient budgets, arithmetic overflow, and N=64000 bb=1 metadata/matrix accounting; Release, ASan/UBSan, aligned, one/many-worker, and scaling E2E runs pass.
close_reason: Workers and allocations are bounded by rounds and checked memory budget

title: Reject nonfinite and malformed rank_total inputs
description:
## Problem
rank_total.py accepts NaN, negative calibration values, invalid rates, and malformed/duplicate PDF data. These values propagate into rankings that exit zero and can label every cost as nan.

## Evidence
experiments/precode/rank_total.py:125-145 accepts float calibration input; 467-473 accepts --assume-ratio nan; score/PDF parsing around 67-75 and 169-223 lacks finite/domain/duplicate-bin checks. Running a tracked CSV with --assume-ratio nan exits zero with nan totals/heavy costs.

## Steps to Reproduce
Run rank_total.py on a tracked result with --assume-ratio nan and inspect exit status/ranking.

## Scope
Validate all numeric inputs at parse boundaries: finite positive ratios/timings/costs, rates in [0,1], nonnegative unique deficit bins, finite nonnegative masses with documented normalization, and consistent row fields. Emit concise source/line diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria: Expand self-tests with NaN/infinity, negative/zero calibration, bad rates, duplicate/negative bins, missing/extra fields, bad PDF mass, and parser overflow; use property fixtures for valid normalized PDFs and cost calculations. Every invalid case must return nonzero without a ranking. End to end, parse/rank every tracked simulator result CSV that matches a supported positional input schema and each calibration CSV through its documented flag, comparing deterministic report checksums. Generated report TXT files are not rank inputs and must be excluded or validated by a separate report schema. Run a malformed corpus and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Implemented atomic strict parsing in experiments/precode/rank_total.py for all six supported simulator schemas and both documented calibration schemas. Every numeric field is finite/domain checked; result rows additionally validate field counts, duplicates, empirical six-decimal PDF counts, unique/nonnegative bins, PDF mass, fail/noheavy/runaway rates, def mean/max, rank/inactivation constraints, and recorded heavy-divisor semantics. Errors identify source:line, return nonzero, emit no ranking, and leave an existing JSON target unchanged. Generated TXT reports are rejected as inputs. Added experiments/precode/test_rank_total.py malformed-corpus, all-numeric-field, property, cost-oracle, calibration, parser-overflow, and CLI atomicity coverage. Final validation: rank_total.py --self-test ran 20 tests; all 47 tracked supported result CSVs (1604 rows) parsed and ranked; cold_xor, preamble xor, and combined muladd calibration CSVs passed their documented flags; reversed input order produced byte-identical TXT/JSON with SHA256 590012b716b2652f5da4d282abcf65028a35b590d8b4c4c296626358b2ba42ac / bb33118cdcdffd38fcec28f9f9b2eacb8982c1ae421da0ca3b0389c8d13d41d8. Three manual bug-review passes completed; final pass found no remaining issue.
close_reason: Acceptance criteria implemented and verified by unit, property, oracle, tracked-corpus, and fresh simulator end-to-end tests.

title: Make the installed Python example fail on corruption and bounded NeedMore
description:
## Problem
The installed Python program can loop forever if a decoder keeps returning NeedMore and returns success even when recovered bytes differ. Resource cleanup is duplicated across branches and is not exception-safe.

## Evidence
python/whirehair.py:182-227 has no delivered-block cap. Lines 243-249 print only on equality but mismatch continues, and line 252 returns zero. Codec ownership is freed manually on individual paths.

## Steps to Reproduce
Inject a fake CDLL decoder that always returns NeedMore or a recover function that returns success with corrupt bytes, then run _run_example.

## Scope
Bound delivery/repair attempts with a terminal diagnostic, require byte equality, avoid ASCII-only output assumptions, and centralize cleanup in try/finally or ownership helpers.
acceptance_criteria: Add fake-CDLL unit tests for perpetual NeedMore, terminal errors, corrupt recovery, non-ASCII messages, encode/recover exceptions, and allocation/creation failure; assert nonzero exit and exactly-once free of every acquired codec. End to end, install to a custom prefix/libdir, import against the installed shared library, perform systematic+repair loss recovery with byte equality, and exercise invalid parameters without hangs. Run Python syntax/tests on supported versions and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Rebuilt the example on context-managed Encoder/Decoder wrappers with lazy injectable loading, structured WirehairError results, and centralized exactly-once finalization. Example delivery is bounded, terminal decode results fail immediately, recovery mismatch is fatal, and output is length-only/UTF-8-safe. Fake-CDLL suite (9 tests) covers perpetual NeedMore, terminal failure, corrupt recovery, non-ASCII messages, injected encode/recover exceptions, encoder/decoder creation OOM, validation, and exact cleanup counts. Python 3.12 py_compile/unittest pass. Installed to a custom prefix/nonstandard libdir and real shared-library example plus loss/reorder recovery completed byte-exactly without hangs. Repeated review tightened documented block-size bounds; final pass found no remaining example defect.
close_reason: The installed Python example is bounded, corruption-failing, exception-safe, and verified with fake and native end-to-end tests.

title: Reject zero-work GenerateSmallDenseSeeds ranges
description:
## Problem
GenerateSmallDenseSeeds accepts any valid model N range but searches only a compile-time candidate list. A request containing no configured candidate exits zero and prints full tables, misleading automation into treating it as completed work.

## Evidence
tables/GenerateSmallDenseSeeds.cpp:19-28 currently defines N_List={17}; CLI validation around 488-492 accepts other N; filtering at 527-533 can process none. gen_small_dseeds --seed 1 --trials 1 --nlo 18 --nhi 18 returns 0 with 146 output lines and no N=18 selection row.

## Steps to Reproduce
Run that command and compare requested range, processed rows, exit status, and emitted candidate table.

## Scope
Separate selection planning from execution, report processed candidate count, and reject zero work before table output. Optionally add explicit --nlist/runtime --full-search behavior rather than implying all valid N are searchable.
acceptance_criteria: Unit-test the selection planner for exact configured N, partial overlap, empty valid range, invalid range, explicit lists, duplicates, and full-search mode if added. End to end, N=18 must either genuinely run/search/revalidate the entry or fail clearly with no candidate table; the default N=17 bounded slice must still run twice byte-identically and pass its quality check. Integrate the failure into run_regeneration_slice, run sanitizers, and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Added a pre-execution selection planner, explicit --nlist override, duplicate/domain/range validation, --selection-self-test, selected-source metadata, and processed candidate count. Empty curated selections now fail before GF256 initialization or any stdout/table output. run_regeneration_slice records and forwards optional SMALL_DENSE_NLIST and propagates empty-selection failure. Verified planner unit cases for exact/partial/empty/invalid ranges, explicit lists, duplicates, empty items and trailing separators; default N=18 fails rc=1 with 0 stdout; explicit N=18 genuinely searches one candidate and finds zero-failure dense/peel winners; fixed-seed N=17 ran twice byte-identically with processed_candidates=1 and zero-failure winners; run_regeneration_slice N=18 fails with zero generator stdout; ASan/UBSan planner and N=17 run pass. Repeated review caught/fixed trailing-comma acceptance; final pass and diff/shell syntax checks pass.
close_reason: Zero-work selections are rejected before output; explicit selection and deterministic/sanitized end-to-end coverage are verified.

title: Validate precode result contents and expected experiment grids
description:
## Problem
The precode result integrity script checks only nonempty .err and stale temporary files, so truncated, malformed, nonfinite, or incomplete CSV results are reported as valid and may be promoted as certification evidence.

## Evidence
experiments/precode/check_results_integrity.sh:14-24 does not parse CSV content. A temp directory containing truncated.csv with 'K,scheme', '1000,dense', and 'BROKEN' returns 'precode result integrity OK'.

## Steps to Reproduce
Create that fixture and run check_results_integrity.sh against its directory.

## Scope
Validate exact supported CSV schemas, field counts/types/domains, finite values, def_pdf mass/bins, H/failure consistency or re-score, and the expected K/scheme/OH/trial grid from a manifest/command. Make the queue validate before atomic promotion/markers.
acceptance_criteria: Add valid, truncated, wrong-column, nonnumeric, nonfinite, invalid-rate, bad/duplicate PDF-bin, wrong-trial-count, missing-grid-row, duplicate-row, and incompatible-manifest fixtures. Invalid input must fail without promotion. End to end, run a small deterministic precode_sim matrix, validate it, then prove truncation and row removal fail; batch-validate every committed precode result artifact with explicit handling for legacy schemas. Exercise queue temp-to-final behavior, run syntax/static checks, and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Implemented strict CSV/schema/domain/PDF/rate/runaway validation with optional exact JSON experiment manifests and command-grid expectations. Queue validates temporary output before atomic promotion, quarantines malformed output, preserves real command exit status, and exposes a bounded --self-test. check_results_integrity rejects empty result sets. Verified: Python unit suite 7/7 (valid, truncated, wrong header/count, numeric/nonfinite/rate domains, duplicate/bad PDF bins, rescore/runaway mismatch, duplicate rows, trial/grid/manifest mismatch); all 47 tracked result CSVs validated (1604 rows); deterministic precode_sim grid validated and missing-row corruption rejected; queue --self-test proves valid promotion, malformed quarantine, and rc=37 preservation; bash -n and py_compile pass. shellcheck is not installed in the environment. Multiple manual bug-review passes found and fixed cross-campaign dedup semantics and shell exit-status handling; final pass found no further issue.
close_reason: Strict content/grid validation and atomic queue promotion are implemented and verified end to end.

title: Add a capacity-checked recover_block API
description:
## Motivation
wirehair_recover_block has no output-capacity parameter, so the library cannot reject undersized buffers and callers cannot use a checked C API for single-block reconstruction.

## Evidence
include/wirehair/wirehair.h:267-288 accepts blockData and bytesOut only; the implementation assumes blockData has blockBytes capacity.

## Scope
Add an ABI-compatible recover_block_ex (or equivalent) with explicit output capacity and no-write/length semantics on failure, while retaining the legacy entry point as a documented unchecked wrapper.
acceptance_criteria: Add canary and ASan unit tests for capacities 0, finalBytes-1/finalBytes, blockBytes-1/blockBytes, null outputs, invalid IDs, direct originals, regenerated originals, and partial final blocks. Assert bytesOut and output remain deterministic/unchanged on failure. End to end, use the installed C API to recover every block from mixed systematic/repair delivery and reassemble/byte-compare the message. Test legacy compatibility, static/shared builds, and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Added ABI-compatible wirehair_recover_block_ex with explicit capacity, zero bytesOut and no block writes on failure; legacy API remains unchecked wrapper. Canary tests cover zero/undersized/exact capacities, nulls, invalid IDs, direct/regenerated originals, final partial block, and every-block lossy E2E. Installed C/C++ static/shared consumers and ASan/UBSan pass.
close_reason: Implemented, covered by focused unit/E2E tests and strict installed consumers, and verified in Release plus ASan/UBSan after repeated bug-review passes.

title: Add result-preserving encoder and decoder creation APIs
description:
## Motivation
Legacy encoder/decoder creation collapses all validation, seed, OOM, initialization, and platform failures to nullptr, preventing callers from diagnosing or responding correctly despite the existing WirehairResult taxonomy.

## Evidence
wirehair.cpp:87-129 and 156-191 discard InitializeEncoder/InitializeDecoder result codes and return only a pointer. Callers cannot distinguish bad N, invalid input, seed failure, OOM, unsupported platform, or wrong initialization state.

## Scope
Add ABI-compatible _ex creation functions that return WirehairResult and write the codec through an out parameter while retaining legacy wrappers. Define output and reuse ownership on every success/failure path.
acceptance_criteria: Unit-test every validation boundary/result class, calls before initialization, bad seed injection, deterministic allocation failure, unsupported-platform injection, null output pointers, and reuse ownership; the out codec must be null/unchanged exactly as documented. Verify legacy wrappers preserve current compatibility. Build pure C and C++ consumers against static/shared installed libraries and complete byte-exact encode/loss/decode/recover round trips through both old and new creation APIs. Run sanitizers and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Added ABI-compatible wirehair_encoder_create_ex, wirehair_encoder_create_owned_ex, and wirehair_decoder_create_ex with documented output/reuse ownership. Results preserve pre-init Error, cached UnsupportedPlatform, validation, SmallN/LargeN, injected BadDenseSeed/BadPeelSeed, and deterministic OOM. Old wrappers remain. Installed strict C and C++ consumers pass static/shared lossy byte-exact round trips.
close_reason: Implemented, covered by focused unit/E2E tests and strict installed consumers, and verified in Release plus ASan/UBSan after repeated bug-review passes.

title: Use overflow-safe block-count ceiling arithmetic in the legacy codec
description:
## Problem
Legacy message-to-block count calculation adds blockBytes-1 to uint64 messageBytes and can wrap, misclassifying huge messages and potentially bypassing size validation.

## Evidence
WirehairCodec.cpp:3503-3508 computes (message_bytes + _block_bytes - 1) / _block_bytes. InitializeDecoder(UINT64_MAX, 2) returns Wirehair_BadInput_SmallN instead of Wirehair_BadInput_LargeN. codec/WirehairV2Codec.cpp already uses safe quotient/remainder arithmetic.

## Steps to Reproduce
Call the legacy internal initializer or a result-preserving create path with messageBytes=UINT64_MAX and blockBytes=2 and inspect the returned classification.

## Scope
Share one overflow-safe ceiling-division/block-count helper across encoder, decoder, and V2 where practical, with explicit supported N/domain checks before allocations.
acceptance_criteria: Add boundary-table unit tests for message sizes 0,1, exact multiples, partial multiples, UINT64_MAX-k through UINT64_MAX and block sizes 0,1,2,maximum; assert exact N or SmallN/LargeN/InvalidInput classifications. Run property tests against a widened reference formula. End to end, exercise valid minimum/maximum N and partial-final messages through encode/loss/decode/recover under ASan/UBSan, and complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Overflow-safe quotient/remainder ceiling arithmetic is covered by an explicit boundary table for zero/one, exact/partial multiples, UINT64_MAX-k through UINT64_MAX, and block sizes 0/1/2/UINT32_MAX, plus randomized reference checks and N=64000 E2E. Independent Release and ASan/UBSan reviews pass.
close_reason: Implemented, covered by focused unit/E2E tests and strict installed consumers, and verified in Release plus ASan/UBSan after repeated bug-review passes.

title: Pair peel-only and joint seedsearch finalist validation trials
description:
## Problem
seedsearch compares peel-only and joint finalists on different random validation trials, so candidate selection includes independent sample noise despite the paired-search methodology and can change across otherwise equivalent runs.

## Evidence
bench/whx.cpp:1280-1282 evaluates peel-only with D00D/BEE5/F00D bases, while lines 1305-1308 evaluates joint with AB12/CD34 before directly comparing their scores.

## Steps to Reproduce
Instrument or log validation trial IDs for both finalists in one campaign and observe disjoint seeds at each loss. Repeat near-tied searches to expose selection sensitivity.

## Scope
Use identical validation trial IDs/loss realizations for both finalists, preserve deterministic tie breaking, and record pairing metadata in output so independent certification can reproduce the decision.
acceptance_criteria: Add an injectable scorer/unit test that records trial IDs and asserts exact peel/joint pairing at every loss, including near ties and deterministic tie-break behavior. End to end, run the same bounded campaign with thread counts 1 and N and require byte-identical finalist choice/output, then independently certify the selected seed on a fresh deterministic trial set at the configured primary loss and 0.30 (primary=0.10 if the legacy seedsearch format is retained). Run sanitizer and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Peel-only and joint finalists now share identical primary/secondary validation bases and trial IDs; deterministic ties retain peel-only and seed ties use lowest IDs. Output records validation trials, stride 2654435761, and base formulas. Injectable scorer selftest records identical trial vectors. Bounded bb=0 campaign stdout is byte-identical at threads=1 and threads=4; selected finalist independently certifies with fresh seeds 9001/9002 at loss 0.10/0.30; ASan/UBSan passes.
close_reason: Finalist trials are paired, reproducible, and independently certified

title: Support linking the installed static archive into shared consumers
description:
## Problem
The default Unix static library is built without position-independent code, so downstream Python extensions, plugins, and shared libraries cannot embed it.

## Evidence
A clean BUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF install linked into a downstream shared object failed with an R_X86_64_PC32 relocation error requesting recompilation with -fPIC. The wirehair target has no POSITION_INDEPENDENT_CODE policy in CMakeLists.txt.

## Steps to Reproduce
Configure/install with BUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF, then link libwirehair.a into a minimal downstream shared library that calls wirehair_init; the linker rejects non-PIC relocations.

## Scope
Define and document static PIC behavior using target properties/options that respect supported platforms and caller policy. Coordinate with the library-variant/package targets without forcing inappropriate flags on platforms that do not need them.
acceptance_criteria: Add an out-of-tree shared-library/plugin consumer that links the installed static Wirehair target, loads dynamically, and completes an encode/drop/decode/recover round trip through the plugin. Test default and explicit PIC policy on GCC and Clang plus relevant Windows behavior; inspect relocations to ensure no text relocations. Record any performance/size effect, run static and shared project CTests, and complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Static targets default POSITION_INDEPENDENT_CODE ON with explicit WIREHAIR_STATIC_PIC=OFF policy and docs; build_policy_e2e asserts default -fPIC and explicit absence. Relocated external MODULE links installed static wirehair, dlopens, and performs full byte-exact codec round trip under GCC and Clang; strict AArch64 QEMU plugin passes. readelf dynamic section has no TEXTREL. Static archive sample was 108,976 bytes PIC vs 113,504 non-PIC (-3.99%, observational). Shared/static project and package suites pass; Windows ignores PIC appropriately.
close_reason: Implemented, independently reviewed, and verified by strict GCC/Clang/MinGW/AArch64 builds, full CTest/sanitizer suites, installed consumer/plugin E2Es, build-policy regressions, and repeated clean bug-review pass.

title: Honor V2 benchmark memory caps and report OOM without aborting
description:
## Problem
V2 benchmark modes allocate large message buffers before validating codec limits, and compare can force N upward even when that violates the configured message cap. Untrusted or mistaken CLI values can abort with uncaught bad_alloc instead of a diagnostic.

## Evidence
codec/WirehairV2Bench.cpp allocates trial buffers before validation around lines 463-466 and 554-557; compare forces max_n to at least two around 1446-1458. Under a 64 MiB process limit, densecheck --N 2 --bb 100000000 and the equivalent capped compare both terminated on uncaught std::bad_alloc.

## Steps to Reproduce
Run those commands in a subprocess with a 64 MiB address-space limit and record the signal/uncaught exception. Also choose a message cap below 2*block_bytes and observe compare exceeds it.

## Scope
Validate widened N*block_bytes and codec bounds before allocating, honor the cap strictly (or reject an impossible configuration), catch/report allocation failure, and keep partial result files from appearing valid.
acceptance_criteria: Add constrained-memory subprocess CTests for every allocating benchmark mode, asserting non-signal nonzero exits, stable diagnostics, and no valid-looking partial stdout/file. Add boundary tests for multiplication overflow, cap below minimum message, exact cap, huge block bytes, and huge N. Smoke all modes with a valid one-trial run, then execute and parse a real bounded compare/densecheck workflow. Run ASan/UBSan and repeated bug-review passes.
notes:
Added widened message/working-set validation, strict compare cap handling, allocation preflight before result headers, a supported working-set ceiling, and top-level bad_alloc/length_error diagnostics. CLI tests cover impossible/exact caps, huge dimensions, all allocating modes, valid smokes, and 64 MiB RLIMIT subprocesses with non-signal failures, stable diagnostics, and empty stdout; precode facade has a separate 64 MiB OOM subprocess. Release and ASan/UBSan passed.
Independent final review extended pre-output integrity to peelcost: complete grid validation and trial-result reservation now occur before stdout, result storage is reused, and CTests assert invalid grids and 64 MiB allocation failure produce nonzero exits, stable diagnostics, and empty stdout. Sanitizer builds skip only real RLIMIT subprocesses that cannot start under ASan; deterministic OOM coverage remains active. Release V2 CTest 6/6 and ASan/UBSan V2 CTest 5/5 pass.
Exact constrained-memory testing exposed Clang eliding the temporary reserve feasibility probe. Added explicit Unix RLIMIT_AS preflight before output; all allocating modes under a 64MiB limit now return exact rc=1, stable diagnostic, empty stdout, and no sanitizer signature. Strict Clang CLI contract passes.
close_reason: Reverified with exact exit/diagnostic/sanitizer oracles; constrained-memory and loss-boundary paths are stable under strict Clang.

title: Prevent V2 seedtable trial-count narrowing and false one-trial runs
description:
## Problem
The V2 seedtable CLI accepts and prints a uint32 trial count but narrows it internally, so valid-looking large runs silently execute a different number of trials and can produce misleading tuning evidence.

## Evidence
codec/WirehairV2Bench.cpp parses --trials as uint32 and reports it, then narrows near line 1561. The audit repro --trials 65536 printed 65536 but completed immediately because the value became zero and was normalized to one.

## Steps to Reproduce
Run wirehair_v2_bench seedtable with --trials 65536 and compare runtime/output/counters with a genuine 65536-trial expectation and with --trials 1.

## Scope
Use one adequately wide validated trial-count type end to end, reject values that cannot be represented by downstream code, and report actual completed trials in machine-readable and human output.
acceptance_criteria: Add CLI unit/CTest coverage at 0, 1, 65535, 65536, the supported maximum, maximum+1, and parser overflow. Use a deterministic instrumented/small workload to assert requested equals executed trials without relying only on elapsed time. Smoke every benchmark mode after the type change and run a bounded real seedtable job whose output records the exact count. Complete sanitizer and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Changed SeedTuningOptions trial count and loop to uint32, added completed TuningTrials, CLI supported range [1,1000000], and requested/completed machine-readable columns. Subprocess tests execute 1, 65535, 65536, and 1000000 trials and reject 0, 1000001, and parser overflow; all benchmark modes smoke-tested. Release and ASan/UBSan passed.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with focused unit/property/oracle tests, Release E2E/subprocess CTests, constrained-memory repros, strict warning build, and ASan/UBSan.

title: Reject unsupported V2 benchmark loss values instead of silently clamping
description:
## Problem
V2 benchmark commands accept and print loss values above the supported range, then silently clamp them inside trial execution. Recorded parameters therefore do not describe the experiment that ran.

## Evidence
WirehairV2Bench accepts values above 0.99 while both trial runners clamp internally around lines 457-460 and 548-551. This can contaminate comparisons and persisted research results.

## Steps to Reproduce
Run applicable benchmark modes with a loss above 0.99 and observe that the CLI accepts/reports the requested value while execution uses 0.99.

## Scope
Define one documented legal loss interval, validate it centrally before output/allocation, and ensure reported configuration exactly equals the executed probability.
acceptance_criteria: Add parser/CLI tests for negative, zero, representative valid, 0.99, just-above-boundary, 1.0, NaN, infinity, and malformed values across every loss-accepting mode. Add a deterministic trial oracle confirming boundary probabilities produce the requested delivery/drop behavior. Run one valid end-to-end compare/densecheck smoke with output parsed back and matched to inputs, plus sanitizer and repeated bug-review passes.
notes:
Centralized loss validation at finite [0,0.99], removed internal clamping, and print round-trip exact executed values. CLI subprocess matrix covers negative, zero, representative valid, 0.99, above-boundary, 1.0, NaN, infinity, and malformed input across every loss mode. Deterministic 10k-draw boundary oracle added; valid compare/dense workflows parsed. Release and ASan/UBSan passed.
Independent follow-up replaced permissive CLI negative oracles with exact rc=1, required diagnostics, and sanitizer/signal rejection for every loss/parser boundary; strict Clang wirehair_v2_bench_cli_test passes all invalid and boundary-valid modes in 0.35s.
close_reason: Reverified with exact exit/diagnostic/sanitizer oracles; constrained-memory and loss-boundary paths are stable under strict Clang.

title: Return precise V2 precode initialization errors and contain allocation failure
description:
## Problem
Codec::InitializePrecodeEncoder maps every failure to Wirehair_BadDenseSeed, and dynamic allocation during initialization or packet Encode can throw across the result-code API. Invalid input and OOM are therefore misreported or terminate the process.

## Evidence
codec/WirehairV2Codec.cpp:130 collapses every MessagePrecodeEncoder::Initialize failure. A 4 GiB message with a 2 GiB block returned Wirehair_BadDenseSeed rather than Wirehair_InvalidInput. Source/parity vectors allocate during initialization, and Encode allocates row vectors through GenerateRecoveryMatrixRow; neither path consistently converts bad_alloc to Wirehair_OOM despite the public result enum.

## Steps to Reproduce
Call InitializePrecodeEncoder with the oversized block case, with a known singular dense corner, and under deterministic/fault-injected allocation failure. Also inject failure during recovery-row allocation in Encode. Compare results and observe misclassification or uncaught bad_alloc.

## Scope
Propagate typed failure results through precode/message/facade layers, validate sizes before allocation, translate allocation/exception failure during initialization and Encode to Wirehair_OOM, and preserve documented state/output on failure.
acceptance_criteria: Unit-test invalid size/domain -> Wirehair_InvalidInput, singular dense corner -> Wirehair_BadDenseSeed, injected initialization allocation failure -> Wirehair_OOM, injected recovery-row/Encode allocation failure -> Wirehair_OOM, and successful operations -> Wirehair_Success. Verify every initialization failure preserves the prior working mode/profile and every Encode failure leaves output length/data as documented; no exception may cross the facade boundary. Run constrained-memory subprocess tests, ASan/UBSan, and successful precode source/repair encoding plus V1/precode mode-transition sequences. Complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes:
Added typed InitializeResult/EncodeResult propagation for InvalidInput, BadDenseSeed, and OOM through precode/message/facade layers. Initialization commits via no-throw swaps; failures preserve working mode/profile. Encode OOM leaves data/output/counters unchanged. Tests cover invalid oversized input, known singular systems, five injected initialization allocation points, injected recovery allocation failure, success after failure, facade mode preservation, and a real 64 MiB RLIMIT subprocess returning Wirehair_OOM. ASan/UBSan passed.
Independent final review made the real 64 MiB precode OOM CTest sanitizer-aware: ASan's shadow mapping prevents process startup under that limit, so Release retains the real RLIMIT E2E while sanitizer builds run deterministic injected allocation failures. Release and ASan/UBSan V2 suites both pass end to end.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with focused unit/property/oracle tests, Release E2E/subprocess CTests, constrained-memory repros, strict warning build, and ASan/UBSan.

title: Value-initialize all V2 facade and precode encoder state
description:
## Problem
Documented pre-initialization accessors expose indeterminate POD fields, and reset assignments copy uninitialized values. This is undefined behavior and produces nonsensical public profiles/system state after construction or failure.

## Evidence
codec/WirehairV2Codec.h leaves Codec::CurrentProfile uninitialized while its constructor is empty. codec/WirehairV2PrecodeEncode.h:196-197 leaves PrecodeEncoder::SystemValue and CodecValue uninitialized; Initialize resets them from default temporaries. A pattern-filled placement-new repro returned 0xfefefefe integers, invalid bool representations, and nonsensical doubles/seeds. GCC Release also warned that these members are used uninitialized at WirehairV2PrecodeEncode.cpp:747 and 799.

## Steps to Reproduce
Placement-new Codec and PrecodeEncoder into storage prefilled with a nonzero byte pattern, call Profile()/System() before initialization and after a failed initialize, and inspect the returned fields or compile at -O3 -Wuninitialized.

## Scope
Value-initialize every public/default lifecycle state, define safe accessor semantics for each mode, and avoid reset operations that read indeterminate temporaries.
acceptance_criteria: Add unit tests using pattern-filled placement storage that inspect every accessor before initialization, after each invalid initialization path, after successful initialization, and after failed/successful reinitialization. Assert valid enum/bool/range defaults and no stale prior state contrary to the documented contract. Require warning-clean optimized GCC/Clang builds and run UBSan plus MemorySanitizer where available. Exercise facade mode transitions end to end and complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Value-initialized all V2 profile/policy/precode state and made precode reinitialization transactional. Pattern-filled placement-new tests inspect Codec, PrecodeEncoder, and MessagePrecodeEncoder accessors; invalid/OOM reinit preserves last good state and successful transitions remain usable. GCC Release and standalone -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Werror compile passed; UBSan passed. Final acceptance added a Clang 18 MemorySanitizer PIE build: wirehair_v2_noinit_test, wirehair_v2_precode_test, and wirehair_v2_precode_encode_test 1 all pass with MSAN_OPTIONS=halt_on_error=1:exit_code=86, including message encoder lifecycle and typed failure/OOM containment. The broader policy benchmark reaches double formatting then reports an interceptor warning in uninstrumented system libstdc++ std::ctype::_M_widen_init, not a codec-state read; focused state coverage is clean.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with focused unit/property/oracle tests, Release E2E/subprocess CTests, constrained-memory repros, strict warning build, and ASan/UBSan.

title: Reject malformed and overflowing V2 PrecodeSystem inputs before writes
description:
## Problem
Low-level V2 precode helpers accept internally inconsistent systems, perform unchecked 32-bit domain arithmetic, and can return success while generated parity values do not satisfy the supplied constraints.

## Evidence
codec/WirehairV2PrecodeEncode.cpp computes K+S+D2 before complete validation, does not require a staircase row's own parity column, and assumes canonical dense rows. Repros: K=2,S=1,D2=H=0 with staircase row {0} returned true and parity 7 although the represented constraint stayed nonzero; K=0 returned true; K=UINT32_MAX,S=2,D2=1 wrapped dense_base and DenseCornerInvertible returned true.

## Steps to Reproduce
Construct the three malformed PrecodeSystem values above and call ComputePrecodeValues/DenseCornerInvertible. Also pass unsorted, duplicate, missing-own-column, and out-of-domain rows under ASan/UBSan.

## Scope
Create one shared no-write validator using widened arithmetic. Validate supported K/S/D2/H and total domain, exact staircase own/link rules, column bounds, row counts, ordering/canonicality, and all assumptions required by set operations and pointer-offset calculations.
acceptance_criteria: Add table-driven negative unit tests for K=0/out-of-range, wrapped totals, missing/duplicate own columns, invalid links, duplicate/unsorted/out-of-span dense columns, mismatched vector counts, and invalid heavy domains. For malformed/invalid systems, assert false, parity unchanged, stats zeroed, and no partial writes. Keep the documented singular-but-structurally-valid dense-corner behavior, including partial cost stats after staircase/dense-known work. Add property tests that every builder-generated system at boundary and randomized K/seeds validates and that feasible computed source+parity values satisfy every binary and heavy constraint. Fuzz malformed systems under ASan/UBSan, run encoder end-to-end cases, and complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes:
Added shared widened ValidatePrecodeSystem enforcing K/S/D2/H domains, row counts, strict ordering/uniqueness, staircase own/link/source-hit rules, dense span/canonical transitions, identity corners, and full symbol bounds before writes. Added table-driven malformed cases, 256 mutation fuzz cases, unchanged parity/zero stats assertions, singular partial-cost behavior, 96 randomized builder systems, and 32 feasible value/constraint properties. Release and ASan/UBSan passed.
Independent final review found that BuildPrecodeSystem still deferred DenseRows/HeavyRows/full-domain/identity-deck rejection until after modifying its output. Added a shared widened parameter validator used before all builder allocation/writes and by structural validation, plus no-write regressions for D2=65, H=129, binary/full-span overflow, and undersized identity decks. Release and ASan/UBSan V2 suites pass after the fix.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with focused unit/property/oracle tests, Release E2E/subprocess CTests, constrained-memory repros, strict warning build, and ASan/UBSan.

title: Add cross-platform CI with sanitizer and end-to-end lanes
description:
## Motivation
There is no automated gate protecting the public C ABI, platform-specific SIMD paths, install behavior, or the registered correctness tests from regression.

## Evidence
The tracked repository has no CI workflow/configuration. The local GCC Release and ASan/UBSan audit suites passed five registered CTests, but compiler/platform diversity, installed consumers, fuzzing, and high-N coverage depend on manual runs.

## Scope
Add a maintainable CI matrix for supported compilers/platforms and separate fast per-change gates from scheduled expensive correctness/performance jobs. Keep deterministic seeds and upload enough diagnostics to reproduce failures.
acceptance_criteria: Per-change CI must build and run CTest on Linux GCC and Clang plus Windows MSVC, cover static and shared libraries, compile the public header from C, and run an out-of-tree installed consumer round trip. A sanitizer lane must run ASan/UBSan with leak detection; add TSan or document a concrete unsupported reason. A scheduled lane must run deterministic random-loss/fuzz coverage and representative small, medium, maximum-N and large-block end-to-end cases without treating terminal decode failure as success. CI commands must also run locally, and repeated review passes must find no remaining workflow defects.
notes:
Reopened during final acceptance audit because native Windows execution was still missing. Implementation is now locally complete: actionlint and ShellCheck pass; ci/test_run_ci.py is 11/11; strict GCC static and Clang shared per-change commands each pass full CTest 21/21, quick exact success/failure large-message profiles, install, and package consumers 3/3; shared also passes installed native Python E2E. ASan+UBSan+LSan passes 20/20 and TSan normal/cold-start passes 2/2. Strict MinGW static/shared package and exact export/import gates pass. Close only after the first pushed hosted matrix, including native MSVC static/shared CTest, is green.
Final pre-push CI runner coverage is 17/17 after adding native dumpbin discovery/parser/validator and strict-shared hook tests; actionlint remains clean. The complete repository Python aggregate is 66/66.
Hosted run 29064885216 at cab00638dc1e9a487846aac55ef94225cadf236b isolated a native MSVC configuration/build defect after both Python suites passed (runner 17/17, wrapper 14/14) and CMake configured. Both windows-2022 lanes failed the first full build: strict_cmake_args replaced CMake's baseline C/CXX flags with /W4 /WX, dropping /EHsc and causing C4530 in gen_tables; /WX also exposed 65 unique WirehairCodec.cpp conversion/constant-condition sites (C4267/C4244/C4127), first at line 949. No tool smoke, profiles, CTest, install, real dumpbin, package, or native Python phase ran. Fix target-scoped strict flags without clobbering toolchain defaults, resolve each source warning by its validated size invariant, rerun local gates, and push a new full matrix. Raw jobs: https://nightly.link/catid/wirehair/runs/86274217417.txt and https://nightly.link/catid/wirehair/runs/86274217442.txt.
Native MSVC remediation is locally complete after hosted run 29064885216. The runner now passes only -DWIREHAIR_STRICT_WARNINGS=ON; CMake applies warning-as-error target-wise and propagates it through nested installed package producer/consumer builds, preserving Visual Studio defaults including /GR and /EHsc. WirehairCodec keeps size_t offset arithmetic but centralizes the checked/lossless <=INT_MAX conversion for all 59 GF256 block-length call sites, uses explicit bounded unsigned conversions, and compile-time-selects the 32-bit SIZE_MAX guard. Two independent reviews found all 69 first-run MSVC diagnostics addressed and no defect. Fresh verification on the final source: repository Python aggregate 66/66; strict GCC 13 static and Clang 18 shared matrices each passed quick exact success/failure profiles, CTest 21/21, install, strict package 3/3, with Clang installed native Python E2E; independent tools+bench builds passed CTest 22/22 for both compilers; ASan+UBSan+LSan passed 20/20; strict MinGW static/shared package and exact PE ABI gate passed. Awaiting the next pushed 9-job hosted matrix only.
Hosted run 29065564699 at 809677c434bc78f26b1b37f2afb043d39bb10f69 confirmed the first remediation: every prior WirehairCodec C4267/C4244/C4127 and missing-/EHsc C4530 diagnostic is gone; MSVC builds wirehair.dll, all generators, unit_test, large_message_test, C/C++ consumers, and test support. Both lanes then exposed later full-build blockers: WirehairV2PrecodeEncode.cpp uses std::back_inserter without directly including <iterator>; LegacyCoreTest passes size_t vector capacities to uint32_t API parameters at 11 sites and instantiates std::fill<vector<uint8_t>, int>. Fix direct include hygiene and use the known uint32_t block_bytes/typed uint8_t fill values, then preflight the remaining V2 graph and push again. Raw jobs: https://nightly.link/catid/wirehair/runs/86276205656.txt and https://nightly.link/catid/wirehair/runs/86276205634.txt.
Second native remediation is locally complete. Added direct <iterator>/<utility> ownership for every affected V2 algorithm symbol; LegacyCore now uses exact uint32_t block_bytes capacities and typed uint8_t fills; all byte-vector fills in the not-yet-built V2 encoder test are typed. Proactive later-phase fixes make both 53-bit RNG-to-double conversions explicit, replace the Windows plugin host's compile-time sizeof runtime branch with a C-compatible typedef assertion, and keep package traversal size_t while API ids/capacities are uint32_t. Three independent reviews found no remaining x64 MSVC /W4 blocker. Final local evidence: fresh strict GCC static and Clang shared full tools/benchmark graphs built 56/57 steps warning-clean and passed 22/22 CTests, strict package 3/3, all six exact bounded smokes, and Clang installed native Python E2E; strict MinGW static/shared package+exact ABI passed; Clang cl-compatible /W4 /WX cross syntax probes passed all changed V2/Legacy/package sources; focused ASan/UBSan passed all 8 affected Legacy/V2 tests (including 96s precode encode). Awaiting third pushed hosted matrix.
Hosted run 29066341405 completed all 9/9 jobs successfully for exact SHA 337cc0d73e79f6b1ad64e0e4dfa7449250e33c90: GCC/Clang/MSVC static/shared, ASan+UBSan+LSan, TSan, and MinGW package ABI. Matrix lanes completed strict build, exact quick success/failure E2Es, CTest, install, strict package consumers, and shared installed native Python E2E. Native MSVC static/shared passed the entire full graph and Windows CTest 19/19. Run: https://github.com/catid/wirehair/actions/runs/29066341405.
close_reason: Implemented, independently reviewed, locally verified, and accepted by the all-green 9-job hosted run 29066341405 at exact SHA 337cc0d73e79f6b1ad64e0e4dfa7449250e33c90, including native MSVC static/shared end-to-end coverage.

title: Honor BUILD_SHARED_LIBS and avoid duplicate core compilation
description:
## Problem
A normal Unix BUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF build still builds and installs both static and shared libraries, compiling every core source twice. This violates standard CMake option expectations and increases clean-build time and CI work.

## Evidence
CMakeLists.txt:129 creates wirehair using BUILD_SHARED_LIBS semantics, then lines 135-143 create wirehair_shared whenever BUILD_SHARED_LIBS is false on non-Windows. The audit build compiled wirehair.cpp, gf256.cpp, WirehairCodec.cpp, and WirehairTools.cpp once per library.

## Steps to Reproduce
Configure with -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF -DBUILD_TESTS=OFF, build the default all target, and observe both libwirehair.a and libwirehair.so plus duplicate object compilation.

## Scope
Make the standard option select one library kind. If building both remains useful, expose an explicit orthogonal option and share safe object compilation only where compile definitions/PIC/ABI permit it.
acceptance_criteria: Add CMake tests for OFF, ON, and any explicit build-both mode, checking target and install artifacts. For each supported mode, build an external C consumer and complete an encode/drop/decode/recover round trip; on shared builds inspect runtime loading/SONAME and on static builds ensure no shared artifact is required. Record clean-build time/object-count before and after, run full CTest, and complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes: BUILD_SHARED_LIBS now selects exactly canonical wirehair static or shared; WIREHAIR_BUILD_BOTH is explicit. Unix dual mode shares one PIC object library (verified one WirehairCodec object) and both primary orientations install/consume successfully. Automated package tests verify separate static/shared artifacts. Old HEAD static-only request built 8 core objects, 2 libs in 1.89s; new builds 4 objects, 1 lib in 0.60s on same host. Shared consumer resolves SONAME libwirehair.so.2 from relocated prefix; static ldd has no wirehair DSO. Full strict shared CTest passes.
close_reason: Implemented, independently reviewed, and verified by strict GCC/Clang/MinGW/AArch64 builds, full CTest/sanitizer suites, installed consumer/plugin E2Es, build-policy regressions, and repeated clean bug-review pass.

title: Export a consumable CMake package for downstream projects
description:
## Motivation
The install tree contains headers and libraries but no package discovery metadata, so downstream CMake projects cannot use find_package(wirehair CONFIG REQUIRED) or a stable imported target.

## Evidence
CMakeLists.txt:189-201 installs archives/shared objects, headers, and the Python file only. A clean audit install contained libwirehair, include/wirehair/wirehair.h, and python/whirehair.py but no WirehairConfig.cmake, version file, pkg metadata, or targets export.

## Scope
Export a stable namespaced target with correct BUILD_INTERFACE/INSTALL_INTERFACE include paths, package configuration and version files, and documented static/shared linkage behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Add automated external C and C++ consumer projects that install Wirehair to a custom prefix and custom libdir, discover it only with find_package(wirehair CONFIG REQUIRED), link the documented namespaced target in static and shared configurations, and complete an encode/drop/decode/recover round trip outside the source/build tree. Verify relocation of the install prefix and Debug/Release compatibility. Run the project CTest suite and repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Installed relocatable wirehairConfig/Version/Targets export with canonical wirehair::wirehair, build/install include interfaces, Threads dependency, and custom CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR support. Automated external C and C++ consumers install to a custom prefix/lib/wirehair-e2e, rename the prefix, use only find_package(wirehair CONFIG)+wirehair_DIR, and pass loss/reorder/recover/block-recover/decoder-conversion in static Debug and shared Release. Plugin E2E also passes. GCC, Clang, AArch64 cross/QEMU, and strict MinGW static/shared consumers configure/link. Install now ships only runtime whirehair.py, not tests.
close_reason: Implemented, independently reviewed, and verified by strict GCC/Clang/MinGW/AArch64 builds, full CTest/sanitizer suites, installed consumer/plugin E2Es, build-policy regressions, and repeated clean bug-review pass.

title: Fourth full bug audit pass
description: Run another repository-wide bug audit with subworkers, confirm suspected bugs with executable repros or tests, implement fixes for confirmed issues, and validate thoroughly.
close_reason: Completed fourth audit pass: confirmed and fixed parser, script, simulator, and v2 precode/bench bugs; validated with focused repros, CTest, standalone harness builds, and ASan/UBSan.

title: Third full bug audit pass
description: Run another repository-wide bug audit with subworkers, confirm suspected bugs with executable repros or tests, implement fixes for confirmed issues, and validate thoroughly.
close_reason: Completed third repository-wide audit pass with confirmed repros, fixes, and validation.

title: Second full bug audit pass
description: Run another repository-wide bug audit with subworkers, confirm any suspected issues with executable repros/tests, implement fixes, and validate thoroughly.
close_reason: Completed repository-wide bug audit pass with confirmed repros, fixes, and validation.

title: Audit and fix confirmed code bugs
description: Search the Wirehair codebase for real bugs, confirm suspected issues with runnable tests or repro cases, implement fixes, and validate with focused and broader test runs.
close_reason: Confirmed bugs were reproduced, fixed, and validated with unit/CTest, ASan/UBSan focused runs, non-native compile smoke, install/Python smokes including custom libdir, and targeted CLI repro commands.

title: Resolve Phase B K=10000 raw inact gate
description: Phase B certification completed in experiments/precode/results/phaseb_cert_20260626.csv. The primary H12 rule clears dense-relative failure at every K/OH and clears sparse-solve reduction for K>=10000. At K=10000, raw inact_mu reduction is only about 6.7%, while rank_mu/non-heavy residual width reduction is about 10.1% because the candidate uses H=12 instead of dense H=6. Decide whether the ship gate should use residual width for H-changing schemes or retune the K=10000 table point to clear literal raw inact_mu >=10%.
notes: Decision (2026-07-02): ship gate for H-changing schemes uses residual width (rank_mu, non-heavy residual), not literal raw inact_mu; K=10000 table point NOT retuned. Full rationale + guard rails recorded in experiments/precode/results/SUMMARY.md 'Ship Gate Decision' section. Key points: raw inact_mu mechanically counts the +6 heavy columns whose cost is already priced via heavy_muladds in rank_total.py; rank_mu -10.1% at K=10000 clears; failure clears ~40x and sparse-solve -27.9% there; retune would burn 200k-trial recert to fix a metric artifact and risks undersized-dense def tail. Guard: always read the gate next to rank_total total-op score; if wirehair-axd end-to-end shows real K=10000 regression, revisit as cost optimization.
close_reason: Resolved: residual-width (rank_mu) gate adopted for H-changing schemes; primary H12 table certified as-is, no K=10000 retune. Documented in SUMMARY.md.

title: Spike V2 LDPC-staircase precode scaffolding behind wrapper
description: Prototype the LDPC-staircase precode plumbing behind the existing WirehairV2 wrapper without touching V1 production paths. This is a pre-certification spike only: use the current Phase-A handoff rule as provisional input, keep it isolated from the blocked certified implementation issue, and validate with lightweight unit/policy checks rather than high-trial Phase-B simulation.
close_reason: Closed as not planned: user wants V2 work to wait until current V1 validation and table work are done.

title: V2 codec prototype: LDPC-staircase + Shuffle-2 D2 + Cauchy H=12 precode behind the v2 wrapper
description: Implement the certified precode (from the Phase B issue) in the codec: staircase parity columns S=GetDenseCount(N) with n1 source hits per column (n1 per the handoff verdict), D2=12 Shuffle-2-generated dense rows (bit-exact rule documented in experiments/precode/README.md — deck via ShuffleDeck16-style Sattolo shuffle, first row = first ceil(span/2) deck entries, subsequent rows = previous XOR 2 deck-driven flips, reshuffle cadence as documented), and explicit Cauchy heavy rows H=12 with band width >= 20 columns (W99 spec from wirehair-wuw notes; full-width preferred, cost is ~H*inact muladds at muladd:xor 1.83/1.10/1.00 for 1280B/100KiB/1MiB). Wire into codec/ (WirehairV2*) behind the existing wrapper so production files stay untouched; validate with wirehair_v2_bench compare against production across N x block-byte grid and with real decode-failure tracking (not just peel proxy). Encoder parity generation must use the n1/staircase structure (measured 54% precode-gen XOR cut at K=3200 for n12+s2; mind the K=10000 combined-leak caveat). Blocked by Phase B certification.
acceptance_criteria: Default full-span Shuffle-2/Cauchy V2 profile initializes across K=2..64000 without identity-corner opt-in. One versioned packet/profile contract is shared by encoder and decoder and binds every matrix-affecting field. Exact sparse peeling plus GF(256) residual solve handles heavy coefficients and block RHS, resumes after NeedMore/OOM, rejects conflicting duplicates/profile mismatches, and recovers byte-exact messages. Tests cover golden rows, tiny exact oracles, constraints, duplicate/loss/reorder/corruption/OOM/no-write, partial tails, source/repair mixes, facade transitions, and K=64000. Real V2 failure/throughput grids run beside production, followed by strict full build/CTest, ASan+UBSan, and CI portability validation; V1 behavior remains unchanged.
notes:

Promote bar: rule fail <= dense + 0.15pp at every K and oh in {0,1}, >=10% inact and >=20% sparse-XOR reduction at K>=10000.
