title: TuneSeedProfile tunes seeds in the v2 synthetic row model but injects them as production p_seed (model-transfer gap)
description: Confirmed by the June 2026 v2-glue region review (2-skeptic verified): TuneSeedProfile (codec/WirehairV2Seeds.cpp:139-226) scores candidate peel seeds via BuildPeelSolvePlan, where the seed only enters a Mix64 hash feeding the v2 experimental row generator (WirehairV2Peel.cpp GeneratePeelMatrixRows). The winner is then injected into the PRODUCTION solver via Impl.OverrideSeeds as _p_seed, which drives a completely different per-row PCG mapping (PeelRowParameters::Initialize). 'Model matrix for seed s peels well' is statistically independent of 'production matrix for p_seed s decodes well', so the bench 'tuned' arm replaces the per-N tuned production seed with an effectively random 8-bit seed (~15/16 tunings). Compounding: candidates are scored at salts no consumer replays. The earlier qn0 adjudication refuted only the RANKING-metric lead, not this transfer gap; 'auto' mode mitigates by A/B-validating tuned-vs-base with real trials before use. Fix options: (a) evaluate candidate p_seeds with real wirehair::Codec encode/decode overhead trials (as densetune/densecheck do for dense seeds); (b) pin the deployment salt and only use tuned profiles with a future v2 row generator that actually consumes the v2 matrix seed; (c) retire the tuned arm. Recorded conclusions from 'tuned' sweeps ('improved some bands, worsened others') are consistent with sampling noise and should not drive decisions. Relates to wirehair-jwg (full table regeneration would subsume per-N seed tuning).
close_reason: Retired the misleading direct compare --v2-profile tuned mode so synthetic TuneSeedProfile output can no longer be injected into production p_seed trials without real validation. compare now accepts only base or auto, rejects tuned with an explicit model-transfer message, rejects unknown profile names, and docs point to auto for real-trial A/B validation or seedtable for offline diagnostics. Validation: cmake --build build --target wirehair_v2_bench wirehair_v2_policy_test; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test; tuned profile rejection smoke; unknown profile rejection smoke; auto profile smoke; git diff --check.

title: Add row-first inactivation peeling methods
description: Add Burshtein-Miller-style row-first inactivation, stochastic degree-weighted row choices, a random inactivation baseline, and a bounded symbolic/lookahead reference mode to the standalone peeling experiment harness. Keep the main codec untouched and report/verify behavior through focused experiments.
notes: Interim analysis caveat: do not mix production Wirehair structure with random structures for schedule claims. Current 100-trial sweep uses method_id in trial seed, so algorithms see independent matrices/loss samples from the same distribution rather than paired identical matrices. Final report should separate: (1) production wirehair+schedule combos, (2) random-only structure+algorithm combos, (3) within-structure method comparisons. Follow-up paired-seed design should use matrix_seed = base^structure^N^trial and schedule_seed = matrix_seed^method_id so every method sees same random matrix samples.
close_reason: Row-first inactivation methods are implemented in the standalone peeling harness and covered by full-method self-tests/sweep

title: Sweep peeling statistic and greedy heuristic combinations
description: Enumerate matrix statistic choices, scoring ablations, and greedy peeling/inactivation selection heuristics in the standalone experiments/peeling harness. Run small/medium/large N sweeps and report residual unpeeled rows/cols plus runtime to identify faster global minima without touching WirehairCodec.cpp.
notes: Implemented methods 21-64 in experiments/peeling/peel_sweep.cpp to sweep candidate pools (all columns, top-K default, all degree-2 endpoints, largest degree-2 component, first min-live row, all min-live rows) against scoring statistics (cached/exact D2, look/fill, ratio, min-fill, duplicate partners, live refs, low refs, max row, random, D3/D4). Reran small/medium/large N sweeps for loss/systematic/repair sources. Best focused-loss residual at N=32000 is rqcc_ratio at 192.01 unpeeled rows/cols; rqcc_lowref is close at 192.94 and simpler. Broader min-row, all-min-row, all-D2, top-K, global, and Markowitz-style variants did not beat the largest degree-2 component family. Bug-fix passes fixed combo look/fill scoring, stale all-D2 candidate collection, combo fallback semantics, strict decimal CSV parsing, and largest-component empty-pool semantics; final read-only pass found no remaining bugs.
close_reason: Completed expanded standalone peeling heuristic/statistic sweep and documented final results.

title: Implement standalone peeling sweep harness under experiments/peeling
description: Build the efficient C++ peel-only experiment harness under experiments/peeling instead of WirehairCodec.cpp. It should enumerate matrix statistics, greedy heuristic ablations, Raptor/RaptorQ-style schedules, and report residual unpeeled rows/cols plus peel-side runtime for small/medium/large N and multiple block sizes.
notes: Implemented standalone peel-only C++ harness under experiments/peeling; it reuses WirehairTools row generation and leaves WirehairCodec.cpp/mainline codec untouched. Enumerates methods 0-20: default, exact/lookahead/stat variants, top-k look/fill, min-row variants, and Raptor/RaptorQ degree-2 component schedules. CSV output reports residual unpeeled cols/rows, component stats, initial todo/d2 rows, choices, build/greedy/total usec, and per-method cost labels. Regenerated loss/systematic/repair sweeps for N=128,2048,32000; peel graph is independent of block_bytes, and README/SUMMARY note that payload bytes are N*block_bytes. Best practical result: rqd2_default/raptorq_d2cc family; min-fill tie-break inconsistent for extra cost; topk8/topk16 are cheaper smaller wins. Validation: diff --check, build.sh, Werror compile of peel_sweep.cpp, ASan sweep, invalid source/loss guards, deterministic 1-vs-4 thread stats check, repeated source-read bug passes.
close_reason: Standalone peeling harness, results, literature cross-check, and repeated validation passes completed.

title: Move peel-heuristic experiments out of mainline codec
description: Remove the WH_PEELOPT experimental peeling code from WirehairCodec.cpp and keep future peeling heuristic sweeps in a separate experiment subfolder/harness. Validate the codec with repeated bug-fix passes after cleanup.
notes: Removed WH_PEELOPT/WH_PEEL_MODE experimental branch from WirehairCodec.cpp, kept only the normal greedy peeling path, and added experiments/peeling/ as the future home for standalone peel sweeps. Validation: cmake build, bench/whx direct build, compile-only WH_SEED_KNOBS/WH_COUNT/ignored-WH_PEELOPT, fuzz/scan passes, ASan-only fuzz all passed. Full unit_test was bounded to 120s and timed out in the known long random-loss loop with only overhead warnings before timeout.
close_reason: Removed experimental peeling code from mainline codec, added experiments/peeling area marker, and completed repeated validation passes.

title: Optimize bounded path-lookahead peeling cost
description: Mode10 bounded path/beam lookahead produced the best residual graph in ablation testing (N=32000 bb=64 cols/rows mu 195.60 vs default 215.38), but the side-band simulator is far too slow for create/decode. Prototype cheaper candidate scoring/state updates, reuse row/column metrics, and retest small/medium/large N plus block_bytes to see whether the residual gain can survive at practical runtime.
notes: Completed 2026-06-06 in the standalone peeling harness. Added local avalanche-estimate methods av_top16/av_top64/av_rqcc16/av_rqcc64, exact one-step controls sim1_top16/sim1_rqcc16, and tail hybrids hyb_rqav5/hyb_rqav10. Added a self-test that compares local avalanche removed-column counts against exact copied one-step simulation during candidate checks and a short walk. Main sweep: 10 frontier/prior structures x methods 18/40/99/115/116/117/118/119/120/121/122/125/126 x N=1000/6400/32000 x overhead 0/1/2, 50 paired trials, N-jitter=10, PDFs. Exact-copy control: N=1000/6400, 20 paired trials. Results saved in experiments/peeling/results/lookahead_cost_N1000_6400_32000_j10.csv, lookahead_exact_controls_N1000_6400_j10.csv, and SUMMARY.md. Result: av_top64 improves residual quality by about 2 cols at N=1000, 4-5 at N=6400, and 6-7 at N=32000, but median greedy time is about 9.9x rqd2_default; av_top16 is about 5.3x. Exact one-step copies did not buy meaningful extra quality and were often 4x-220x slower than local avalanche estimates. Cost frontiers still favor ks_bmax_top16/top64 or rqd2_default/rqcc_lowref; hyb_rqav5 only barely won one 1MiB row at N=32000/OH=2. Conclusion: not a shipping scheduler improvement in this form; future work would need truly incremental avalanche/bucket scoring or a large-block tail-only trigger.
close_reason: Prototype, validation, and small/medium/large retest completed; local avalanche lookahead is documented as not cost-effective enough to ship in this form.

title: Ablation: bounded path lookahead
description: Add a bounded path/beam lookahead mode that simulates a few deferred choices in side-band state and applies the first choice from the best path. Sweep K/beam/depth on small, medium, and large N.
notes: Implemented mode10 bounded path/beam lookahead with WH_PEEL_LOOKAHEAD_K/B/D. 30-40 trial peelstat at bb=64: k8/b2/d2 was best residual graph, N=128 7.03, N=2048 36.63, N=32000 195.60 cols/rows mu, versus default 7.56/38.33/215.38 and mode3 7.47/37.81/211.82. It is expensive: k4/b2/d2 large-N bb=64 create/decode 5.4/4.6 MBPS vs default 203.5/171.3. Good research signal, not a shippable default.
close_reason: Implemented and tested ablation; results recorded in notes.

title: Ablation: hybrid mode by deferral progress
description: Add a knob to use lookahead only for the first X percent of deferrals, then fall back to default greedy. Sweep X.
notes: Implemented WH_PEEL_HYBRID_DEFER_PCT. Tested 10/25/50 percent with mode3 on N=128,2048,32000 bb=64; all matched mode3 because final defer counts are far below those percentages of N (e.g. ~212/32000 = 0.66%). For this graph size, meaningful thresholds would need to be around 1 percent or lower.
close_reason: Implemented and tested ablation; results recorded in notes.

title: Ablation: mode 5 seed sweep
description: Run deterministic WH_PEEL_SEED sweeps for randomized tie-breaking mode 5 and report whether any seed improves residual graph stats.
notes: Ran mode5 seed sweep for seeds 0..63 at N=32000 bb=64 trials=40. Best seed was 2 at cols/rows mu 212.43; worst was 55 at 213.35. Seeded random tie-breaking is better than default in this sample but worse than mode3/mode9 and much worse than mode10.
close_reason: Implemented and tested ablation; results recorded in notes.

title: Ablation: hybrid mode by block-count threshold
description: Add a knob to use lookahead only below an N threshold and default greedy at or above the threshold. Sweep thresholds.
notes: Implemented WH_PEEL_HYBRID_N. With mode3 and WH_PEEL_HYBRID_N=4096, N=128 and 2048 use mode3 behavior (7.47 and 37.81), while N=32000 falls back to default (215.38). This is useful only as a runtime guard; it intentionally gives up the large-N residual improvement.
close_reason: Implemented and tested ablation; results recorded in notes.

title: Ablation: cap second-order lookahead rows
description: Add a cap for the number of degree-2 rows contributing second-order lookahead per column. Sweep caps across small, medium, and large N.
notes: Implemented WH_PEEL_MAX_D2 to cap partner lookahead scoring while still counting exact degree-2 refs. 80-trial peelstat: cap8/16/32 matched full mode3 on this sample (N=32000 mu 211.82); cap4 regressed slightly to 212.59. Cap is safe for runtime trimming, but quality only holds once cap is at least 8 here.
close_reason: Implemented and tested ablation; results recorded in notes.

title: Ablation: exact live degree-2 only
description: Add a pure exact live degree-2 selector that excludes stale Weight2Refs except optional fallback ties. Compare against mode 3 and default.
notes: Implemented mode 8 pure exact live degree-2 count. 80-trial peelstat at N=128,2048,32000 bb=64 matched default exactly in this sample: 7.56/38.33/215.38 cols/rows mu. This suggests the existing Weight2Refs key is already equivalent for this residual metric, so exact-only is not an improvement.
close_reason: Implemented and tested ablation; results recorded in notes.

title: Ablation: weighted lookahead score
description: Add a weighted lookahead scoring mode/knobs for exact_w2, partner lookahead, and fill cost. Sweep weights across small, medium, and large N.
notes: Implemented weighted lookahead mode 9 plus WH_PEEL_WEIGHTED for modes 3/11. 80-trial peelstat at bb=64: W1/W4/W16 with WC=0 all landed near N=32000 cols/rows mu 211.80, essentially same as mode3 211.82 and better than default 215.38. Fill penalty WC=1 was neutral/slightly worse: W16C1 211.86, W4C1 213.20. No weighted setting beat bounded lookahead.
close_reason: Implemented and tested ablation; results recorded in notes.

title: Ablation: mode 3 top-K lookahead
description: Add WH_PEELOPT knobs to restrict expensive lookahead scoring to the top K default candidates. Sweep K on small, medium, and large N.
notes: Implemented WH_PEEL_TOPK for mode 3/9/11 candidate restriction. 80-trial peelstat at N=128,2048,32000 bb=64: full mode3 cols/rows mu 7.47/37.81/211.82 vs topK4 7.49/37.89/213.20, topK8 7.47/37.89/212.70, topK16 7.47/37.81/211.99, topK32 7.47/37.81/212.00. TopK8 is the useful speed/quality point: large-N create/decode 127.7/113.3 MBPS at bb=64 vs full mode3 50.5/43.7, but still slower than default 203.5/171.3.
close_reason: Implemented and tested ablation; results recorded in notes.

title: Report statistical unpeeled rows and columns by peel mode
description: Add or use WH_COUNT instrumentation to aggregate remaining unpeeled/deferred rows and columns for each WH_PEELOPT mode across repeated trials, then report mode-by-mode statistics for small, medium, and large N.
notes: Added WH_COUNT peelstat harness command. Ran WH_PEELOPT modes 0-7 with 200 successful decode trials per N at N=128,2048,32000, bb=64, loss=0.10, startMode=0. Remaining unpeeled rows and columns were identical for every sample. Means by mode/N: mode0 7.55/38.41/213.69; mode1 11.23/46.95/285.48; mode2 10.98/46.90/285.48; mode3 7.46/38.09/210.17; mode4 7.51/38.38/213.54; mode5 7.53/38.44/213.54; mode6 7.56/38.48/213.66; mode7 7.55/38.84/213.94. Mode3 has the best residual graph; modes1/2 are substantially worse.
close_reason: Added peelstat and reported mode-by-mode residual peel graph statistics

title: Future: make lookahead peeling scores incremental
description: Mode 3 lookahead reduced deferred graph size and decode-feed traffic in the N/block-byte matrix, but exact rescoring inside GreedyPeeling erased the win. Follow up by maintaining exact degree-2 buckets/lookahead scores incrementally during PeelAvalancheOnSolve(), then retest small/medium/large N with varied block bytes.
notes: 2026-06-10 implemented experiment-only av_lazy16/av_lazy64 in experiments/peeling/peel_sweep.cpp as dependency-invalidated cache-backed equivalents of av_top16/av_top64. Added paired self-test over wirehair_rand, lt_m2_c1024, lt_m2_c256_fold and N anchors 320/1280/3200 asserting identical selected-column traces and residual metrics. Validation: g++ -O3 /tmp build --self-test PASS; ASan/UBSan /tmp build --self-test PASS; git diff --check PASS. Smoke CSV experiments/peeling/results/n34_8_lazy_avalanche_smoke.csv (N=1000,6400,32000; 6 paired trials; one thread) shows exact/lazy residual metrics and sparse-XOR estimates match for every row, but lazy is slower in all 18 pair comparisons: av_lazy16 1.01x-1.36x slower than av_top16, av_lazy64 1.04x-1.50x slower than av_top64. Verdict: dependency-cache shape killed; correctness preserved but bookkeeping outweighs avoided recomputation.
close_reason: Closed as completed negative experiment: cache-backed avalanche lookahead is exact but slower, so do not promote.

title: Peeling heuristic: weight-2 overlap clustering
description: Prototype a selector that favors columns whose degree-2 rows overlap with repeated partner columns. Test whether clustered weight-2 choices reduce defers or XOR traffic across small, medium, and large N.
notes: Implemented as WH_PEELOPT mode 7 repeated degree-2 partner overlap scoring. Short fuzz passed. It did not improve the large case (N=32000 bb=1300 defers 214 vs default 213, traffic 629.97MB vs 629.52MB), and throughput dropped to 458 MB/s vs mode0 673 MB/s. Not adopting.
close_reason: Prototype tested; result negative versus default

title: Peeling heuristic: randomized multi-start or seeded tie-breaking
description: Prototype randomized seeded tie-breaking or multi-start approximation for deferred-column selection. Fuzz and benchmark across small, medium, and large N to check correctness and stability.
notes: Implemented as WH_PEELOPT mode 5 seeded deterministic tie-breaking around the default key, controlled by WH_PEEL_SEED. Short fuzz passed. Count results were effectively default-like (N=32000 bb=1300 traffic 629.36MB vs 629.52MB), but throughput dropped to 438 MB/s vs mode0 673 MB/s. Not adopting.
close_reason: Prototype tested; no meaningful improvement

title: Peeling heuristic: RaptorQ-style inactivation
description: Prototype a RaptorQ-style lowest-degree-row inactivation selector compatible with the current greedy peel pipeline. Compare graph size, traffic, and throughput across small, medium, and large N.
notes: Implemented as WH_PEELOPT mode 6 lowest-live-degree-row/RaptorQ-style compatible selector. Short fuzz passed. Counts stayed close to default (N=32000 bb=1300 defers 211 vs default 213, traffic 629.57MB vs 629.52MB), and throughput was slower at 580 MB/s vs mode0 673 MB/s. A true RaptorQ-style inactivator would need side-band degree queues/replay, not just selector replacement.
close_reason: Prototype tested; no improvement in compatible selector form

title: Peeling heuristic: lookahead deferral
description: Prototype a one-step lookahead selector that estimates immediate and second-order peel avalanche value. Test correctness and performance across small, medium, and large N with varied block bytes.
notes: Implemented as WH_PEELOPT mode 3 one-step lookahead. Short fuzz passed. It was the only consistent graph-quality win: N=32000 bb=1300 decoder defers dropped to 197 from default 213 and decode-feed traffic to 626.99MB from 629.52MB. However selector overhead dominated: large decode throughput was 485 MB/s vs mode0 673 MB/s. Keep as research signal, not production yet.
close_reason: Prototype tested; promising graph metric but negative wall-clock without incremental maintenance

title: Peeling heuristic: degree-bucket priority queues
description: Evaluate whether bucketed/prioritized candidate selection can reduce GreedyPeeling scan overhead or improve tie choices. Measure against default across small, medium, and large N.
notes: Implemented as WH_PEELOPT mode 4 exact-degree-2 bucket-compatible scoring, not a full dynamic priority queue. Short fuzz passed. Counts were near default with no robust traffic win (N=32000 bb=1300 629.41MB vs default 629.52MB), but throughput dropped to 442 MB/s from mode0 673 MB/s due rescoring overhead. True bucket queues need incremental updates.
close_reason: Prototype tested; no wall-clock improvement

title: Peeling heuristic: component-aware inactivation
description: Prototype a component-aware/local-connectivity deferred-column selector behind an experiment flag. Measure defer count, deferred graph components, traffic, correctness, and throughput on small, medium, and large N with varied block bytes.
notes: Implemented as WH_PEELOPT mode 2 component/local-fanout proxy. Short fuzz passed. Systematic count/bench on N=128,2048,32000 and bb=64,1300 matched mode 1's negative pattern: large decoder defers 255 vs default 213, decode-feed traffic 641.88MB vs 629.52MB, throughput 204 MB/s vs mode0 673 MB/s. Not adopting.
close_reason: Prototype tested; result negative versus default

title: Peeling heuristic: Markowitz/min-fill inactivation
description: Prototype a Markowitz/min-fill deferred-column selector behind an experiment flag. Compare against default on small, medium, and large N, including block-size variation and WH_COUNT graph/traffic counters.
notes: Implemented as WH_PEELOPT mode 1. Short fuzz passed. Systematic count/bench on N=128,2048,32000 and bb=64,1300 showed it is worse than default: large decoder defers increased (N=32000 bb=1300: 255 vs default 213), decode-feed traffic increased (641.88MB vs 629.52MB), and throughput dropped sharply (201 MB/s vs mode0 673 MB/s in this run). Not adopting.
close_reason: Prototype tested; result negative versus default

title: Benchmark matrix: sweep block count N and block_bytes
description: Create a reusable benchmark matrix for optimization experiments. N is the number of blocks/symbols; total memory traffic and working-set bytes scale as N * block_bytes, so memory, cache, TLB, SIMD, and algorithmic experiments must vary block_bytes as well as N. Capture baseline and changed throughput for create/encode/decode/recover.
acceptance_criteria: Harness supports repeated sweeps over N and block_bytes; reports comparable before/after metrics for normal and SIMD builds.
notes: Implemented bench --bb-list matrix support and WH_COUNT count mode list support. Existing --bb single-value output remains compatible; multi-bb output includes N, bb, msg_MiB, create/encode/decode/recover MBPS, overhead.
close_reason: Implemented/measured in optimization experiment pass; kept useful knobs gated where results were mixed.

This is the remaining reactive fixup work; wirehair-jwg tracks the broader long-term full offline table regeneration replacement.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: 2026-07-02 resume details: rebuilt seed-knob harness bench/whx.0yk2 from HEAD 2f3b253 (-DWH_SEED_KNOBS=1). Partial 10k promotion file has 183 data rows, 107 strict (def>0.05, b10<=0.05, b30<=0.05); 22 strict Ns uncovered. Launched (a) independent seedmean audit of the 107 strict rows, t=10000, loss 0.10+0.30, disjoint streams base 0xA0D17+N*1013 / 0xA0D30+N*2027 -> bench/results/seedmean_audit_32000_64000_t10000_2026-07-02.txt (50 threads); (b) resumed 10k promotion seedsearch over the 22 missing Ns -> bench/results/seedsearch_joint_severe_promote_32000_64000_t120_v10000_resume_2026-07-02.txt (30 threads). Verified gen_fixups2 baseline-synthesis roundtrip reproduces HEAD tables exactly (630 peel / 99 dense).

title: tools: GeneratePeelRowWeight distribution lookup
description: Replace linear scan tail with precomputed; tiny.
close_reason: Added top-byte starting lookup for GeneratePeelRowWeight tail scan; verified exact equivalence against old distribution.

title: codec: branch hints (likely/unlikely) + __restrict in hot loops
description: PeelDiagonal/Substitute/Triangle hot loops; help the compiler. Small, low-risk.
close_reason: Added scoped likely/unlikely hints to hot codec rare/common branches; default end-to-end mostly neutral to positive, largest gain at N=8192 in normal build.

title: tools: Weyl IterateNextColumn + NextPrime16 micro-opt
description: Hot per-column generator; reduce modulo/branches. Small.
close_reason: Removed variable modulo from IterateNextColumn, added small-prime shortcut, and verified helper equivalence against old formulas.

title: gf256: AVX-512 widen memswap + scalar 8B tails
description: memswap still 128-bit; widen to ZMM. Tail loops could be wider. Small.
close_reason: Implemented AVX2/AVX-512 gf256_memswap widening and added memswap micro selfcheck/throughput coverage.

title: gf256: branchless y<=1 in mul/muladd (rare-path)
description: cmov/inline vs branch; tiny.
close_reason: Kept the y<=1 special-case as one unlikely branch so the common multiply path avoids an extra branch; measured with micro and end-to-end bench.

title: Memory: huge pages (2MB) for recovery_blocks + workspace
description: Scattered access over multi-MB working set -> TLB misses. madvise(MADV_HUGEPAGE)/mmap in AllocateWorkspace. Check perf dTLB-load-misses first.
notes:
Experiment scope correction: N is block count, not bytes. Huge-page/TLB measurements must sweep block_bytes too because total working-set bytes scale as N * block_bytes.
Measured existing WH_HUGEPAGE path with new N x block_bytes matrix. Huge pages are regional/mixed: some larger block_bytes cells improved (e.g. N=8192 bb=8192 decode/recover modestly), while other cells regressed or were noise. WH_HUGEPAGE and WH_ALIGN64+WH_HUGEPAGE remain opt-in, not default.
close_reason: Implemented/measured in optimization experiment pass; kept useful knobs gated where results were mixed.

title: Memory: 64B-align recovery_blocks & GE rows for AVX-512
description: Avoid cache-line splits in bulk ops; ensure alignment in AllocateWorkspace.
close_reason: Implemented WH_ALIGN64 opt-in for 64-byte workspace bases and GE row pitch; measured mixed results, so left it gated rather than default.

title: Build: PGO (profile-guided opt)
description: Train on a representative encode/decode workload, rebuild with profile. 5-15% potential across all parts. More setup.
notes:
Next-experiment scope: train PGO on representative create/encode/decode/recover workloads across both block count N and block_bytes, including normal and SIMD/GFNI variants.
Implemented PGO plumbing in bench/build.sh and CMake (WH_PGO_MODE=OFF/GENERATE/USE, WH_PGO_DIR; bench PGO=off/gen/use). Trained on mixed N and block_bytes workload plus fuzz. PGO-use built cleanly but was mostly negative/mixed in 4-thread and single-thread matrices, especially larger byte-footprint cases. Keep as build option, not default.
close_reason: Implemented/measured in optimization experiment pass; kept useful knobs gated where results were mixed.

title: Build: -funroll-loops / -fno-semantic-interposition
description: Cheap codegen flags; A/B measure.
close_reason: Measured -funroll-loops/-fno-semantic-interposition variants; not kept as defaults because encode/recover were mixed to negative.

title: EPIC: Small-wins sweep across ALL parts
description: Per user: explore optimizations for every part, even small wins. Build-level (LTO/PGO/unroll), gf256 tails/memswap/branch, codec hot loops & alignment, allocation (huge pages/TLB), tools (Weyl/prime/distribution), API. Each item: measure (count harness + single-thread median timing), keep if it helps, behind flag if risky.
notes: Small-wins sweep update: default kept changes are Weyl modulo removal, small-prime shortcut, exact peel-weight top-byte lookup, scoped branch hints, gf256 y<=1 unlikely branch, AVX2/AVX-512 memswap, and memswap harness coverage. WH_ALIGN64 implemented as opt-in due mixed N=2048 vs N=8192 results. -funroll-loops/-fno-semantic-interposition measured and not kept. PGO/LTO/hugepages remain separate open workflow items where appropriate.
close_reason: All small-wins child experiments are implemented/measured and closed; remaining work belongs to larger algorithm/offline-search issues.

title: Build: LTO (-flto) across all TUs
description: Link-time cross-file inlining; touches every part. Add to CMake + build.sh. Measure create/decode timing.
notes:
Next-experiment scope: include full LTO and ThinLTO where supported; benchmark normal and SIMD/GFNI builds.
Implemented LTO plumbing in bench/build.sh and CMake (WH_LTO=OFF/ON/AUTO/THIN; bench LTO=off/on/auto/thin). Measured default, -flto, and -flto=auto across N=512,2048,8192 and bb=64,1300,8192. Results mixed: -flto=auto helped some single-thread medium cases and 4-thread N=8192/bb=8192, but hurt other N=8192/bb=1300 and large footprint cells. Keep as build option, not default.
close_reason: Implemented/measured in optimization experiment pass; kept useful knobs gated where results were mixed.

title: GPU offload (evaluate; likely poor fit)
description: Workflow lit: GPU helps GE for N>1000 but PCIe overhead + small dense matrices (defer+dense 50-600) usually lose to CPU SIMD. Plus the bottleneck is scattered memory access, not compute. Evaluate only for very large N batch encode; document feasibility. Low priority.
notes: 2026-06-10 GPU feasibility decision: do not prototype GPU offload for the codec hot path. Existing profiling/counters show the bottleneck is scattered data-block XOR traffic, not arithmetic: bench/results/codex_la_prompt.md records ~56% runtime in gf256_add/add2/addset_mem over multi-MB recovery blocks, GF(256) multiply <2%, and bit-matrix GE <1%; GFNI/AVX-512 sped kernels 2-2.8x with ~0 end-to-end gain. WH_COUNT at N=32000, bb=1300 shows encoder_create ~597 MB XOR vs only ~0.14 MB GFmul; precode K32000 dense partial shows backsub (~4.0M block XORs) + sparse (~0.46M) dwarf GE/heavy terms. GPU would have to transfer the same scattered symbol data over PCIe and would mostly run bandwidth-bound XORs, so single-stream decode/encode is expected to lose. Batch/offline table search can use CPU parallelism already; revisit GPU only for a separate many-message batched service with resident device memory and measured transfer amortization.
close_reason: Closed as no-go after feasibility evaluation: GPU offload targets the wrong bottleneck; prioritize symbol-XOR reduction/locality tasks instead.

title: Full offline regeneration of ALL seed/dense-count tables with 128-core search
description: The peel-seed fix (228 entries) patches defects reactively. The root cause is under-optimized original tables (insufficient search at gen time). Re-run tables/Generate*.cpp generators with far more Monte Carlo (128 cores) + the new scan/seedsearch validators to regenerate kPeelSeeds/kDenseSeeds/kDensePoints/heavy seed cleanly across [2,64000]. Supersedes the reactive fixup table.
notes:
2026-06-11 preflight: existing table generators are available behind the gen_tables CMake target (GenerateDenseCount, GeneratePeelSeeds, GenerateMostDenseSeeds, GenerateSmallDenseSeeds, TableGenerator/HeavyRowGenerator), but they are historical one-off programs with hardcoded trial counts/OpenMP loops. A full regeneration should not start while the active precode queue is using ~116 cores and full unit_test another 8. When capacity is free, build a dedicated run plan around the newer bench/whx scan/seedsearch validators and loss=0.10/loss=0.30 verification, then use the old generators/table code only where they are still the source of truth for dense-count and seed-table shape. This remains open as the long-term replacement for reactive fixup tables; wirehair-0yk is the nearer-term reactive [28033,64000] work.
2026-07-07 dense-count generator bug pass: fixed GenerateDenseCount.cpp to advance the trial-stream seed once per N instead of once per dense-count candidate, matching the paired-trial/winner's-curse methodology already used by the sibling dense seed generators. Validation: cmake --build build --target gen_dcounts -j; git diff --check -- tables/GenerateDenseCount.cpp; deterministic bounded old-vs-new comparison with kTrials=40, seed=123456789, N=2..128 built from HEAD and working tree, showing intentional output changes from paired candidate trials (234 changed output rows).
2026-07-08 generator ergonomics slice: GenerateDenseCount now accepts deterministic/range-bounded CLI knobs (--seed, --trials, --nlo, --nhi, --max-failures, --low-count-run) while preserving old defaults. This makes full-regeneration slices reproducible and avoids all-or-nothing 2..64000 runs during validation. For mid-range bounded runs it initializes lastBest from GetDenseCount(nlo) to avoid scanning from count=1. Validation: cmake --build build --target gen_dcounts; two identical --seed 123456789 --trials 8 --nlo 2 --nhi 16 runs compared byte-for-byte on stdout/stderr; single-N --seed 1 --trials 1 --nlo 1000 --nhi 1000 smoke; invalid --nlo 1 range rejects; git diff --check.
2026-07-08 generator ergonomics slice 2: GeneratePeelSeeds now accepts bounded/reproducible run controls (--trials, --sublo, --subhi, --nlo, --nhi, --max-tries, --skip-tuning) while preserving full-table defaults. Bounded runs tune only requested subdivisions and N ranges; subdivisions with no N in range keep the existing seed. This makes peel-seed regeneration divisible into noninteractive slices instead of forcing the full 2048-subdivision campaign. Validation: cmake --build build --target gen_peel_seeds; --sublo 0 --subhi 0 --nlo 2048 --nhi 2048 --trials 1 --max-tries 1 --skip-tuning smoke; same command without --skip-tuning smoke; repeated one-try tuning run compared byte-for-byte on stdout/stderr; invalid --sublo 2048 rejects; git diff --check.
2026-07-08 generator ergonomics slice 3: GenerateMostDenseSeeds now accepts --seed, --trials, --dense-index-lo, and --dense-index-hi. Defaults preserve the historical adaptive full configured dense-index range; --trials enables deterministic fixed-trial validation and skips wall-clock calibration. Also guarded the adaptive calibration scale against a zero-duration timing sample. Validation: cmake --build build --target gen_most_dseeds; two identical --seed 123456789 --trials 1 --dense-index-lo 13 --dense-index-hi 13 runs compared byte-for-byte on stdout/stderr; invalid dense-index range rejects; git diff --check.
2026-07-08 generator ergonomics slice 4: GenerateSmallDenseSeeds now accepts --seed, --trials, --nlo, and --nhi. Defaults preserve the current configured search (N_List in normal builds, full range when ENABLE_FULL_SEARCH is compiled), while fixed seed/trials and range filtering make small-table refinement reproducible. Validation: cmake --build build --target gen_small_dseeds; two identical --seed 123456789 --trials 1 --nlo 17 --nhi 17 runs compared byte-for-byte on stdout/stderr; invalid --nlo 1 rejects; --help smoke; git diff --check.
2026-07-08 generator ergonomics slice 5: gen_tables/TableGenerator now accepts --no-benchmarks/--no-bench and --heavy-trials N, while preserving default benchmark-on and 1M heavy-row perturbation behavior. HeavyRowGenerator keeps the shipped-byte regression gate and lets quick runs skip or downsample only the informational perturbation measurement. Validation: cmake --build build --target gen_tables; build/gen_tables --no-benchmarks --heavy-trials 0 completed and reported shipped Cauchy reproduction + skipped measurement; build/gen_tables --help; invalid --heavy-trials rejects; two sequential --no-benchmarks --heavy-trials 16 runs compared byte-for-byte on stdout/stderr; git diff --check. Bug pass found and fixed one default status-text drift; second pass found no further issues.
2026-07-08 regeneration harness slice: added tables/run_regeneration_slice.sh and ignored tables/results/. The harness builds all generator targets, runs bounded deterministic slices for dense count, peel seeds, most-dense seeds, small-dense seeds, and gen_tables, writes command files plus a manifest with git commit/threads/checksums, and can repeat each step with diff checks (REPEAT=1 default). Validation: default smoke to /tmp/wirehair-tablegen-slice-smoke with REPEAT=1 produced matching repeat checksums and zero-byte diffs for all steps; path-normalization smoke from /tmp with BUILD_DIR=build OUT_DIR=/tmp/wirehair-tablegen-slice-pathcheck REPEAT=0 completed and recorded build_dir=/home/catid/wirehair/build; git diff --check. shellcheck is not installed in this environment. Bug pass fixed relative BUILD_DIR/OUT_DIR normalization; final pass found no further issues.
2026-07-08 regeneration harness toggle slice: extended tables/run_regeneration_slice.sh with BUILD_GENERATORS and per-step RUN_* toggles so full offline regeneration can run one table family/shard at a time while preserving the same manifest/checksum/repeat-diff format. Validation: bash -n tables/run_regeneration_slice.sh; dense-count-only run with BUILD_GENERATORS=0 RUN_PEEL_SEEDS=0 RUN_MOST_DENSE_SEEDS=0 RUN_SMALL_DENSE_SEEDS=0 RUN_GEN_TABLES=0 REPEAT=1 produced only dense_count artifacts and zero-byte repeat diffs; git diff --check. Bug pass checked default behavior remains opt-in per step default 1 and selective manifests record run flags; no further issues found.
2026-07-08 actual dense-count shard: ran tables/run_regeneration_slice.sh with OUT_DIR=tables/results/dense_count_N2_256_t500_20260708, BUILD_GENERATORS=0, only RUN_DENSE_COUNT=1, DCOUNT_TRIALS=500, DCOUNT_NLO=2, DCOUNT_NHI=256, REPEAT=0 on 128 OpenMP threads. Completed quickly; manifest checksum for dense_count.out is 6f1a9305878085bb348c21f8e98554acc1a1c87ccc54a3c7a9442155df46ef3f. Output has 180 rows plus header because GenerateDenseCount's low-count-run compression skips some N after stable runs. Artifact is under ignored tables/results/ and is an operational shard, not a committed replacement table.
2026-07-08 dense-count full-range shard set: using tables/run_regeneration_slice.sh on 128 OpenMP threads, generated ignored operational dense-count artifacts covering N=2..64000 at DCOUNT_TRIALS=500, seed=123456789, max_failures=10, low_count_run=4, REPEAT=0. Shards/checksums: tables/results/dense_count_N2_256_t500_20260708 dense_count.out sha256=6f1a9305878085bb348c21f8e98554acc1a1c87ccc54a3c7a9442155df46ef3f; dense_count_N257_4096_t500_20260708 sha256=92b7ca311c2d8b505f6cb28b7911a44f6f4a2e3a7444ac8580b7930383cb5f91; dense_count_N4097_16384_t500_20260708 sha256=744abe80ade5c6583452b16f1b2784a80cdc219127509526a3a3238fb2120644; dense_count_N16385_64000_t500_20260708 sha256=66c73e0a9f69158e83893aa3416855dc8048de1673f17b1fa4a19a34da0ef313. These are not committed replacement tables; next step is compare/merge policy and repeat or increase trials before changing shipped dense counts.
2026-07-08 dense-count shard comparison vs current GetDenseCount(): compiled a temporary /tmp comparator against WirehairTools.cpp and compared the four ignored dense-count shards. Across 545 sampled rows, 529 differ from shipped GetDenseCount(); 506 are lower and 23 higher, min_delta=-39, max_delta=+15, mean_delta=-9.78. Compare artifact /tmp/wirehair_dense_count_compare.tsv sha256=78d427a0bdc2e9d8b82b5386bde35076612120734f6c702e7a5d4516b020fd98. Interpretation: a single 500-trial run should NOT replace the shipped dense table, which historically used max-over-runs; this validates the harness and exposes expected policy work (repeat/increase trials, aggregate conservatively, then decide merge).
2026-07-08 peel-seed operational shard: ran tables/run_regeneration_slice.sh with OUT_DIR=tables/results/peel_seed_sub0_t10_try16_20260708, only RUN_PEEL_SEEDS=1, PEEL_SUBLO=0, PEEL_SUBHI=0, PEEL_NLO=2048, PEEL_NHI=64000, PEEL_TRIALS=10, PEEL_MAX_TRIES=16, REPEAT=0 on 128 OpenMP threads. Candidate results: seed 0 -> 5 failures, seed 13 -> 1 failure (best), several candidates hit 10000+ penalties; final bounded pick was subdivision 0 seed 13 failures 1. peel_seeds.out sha256=a051e8c7baac871299acc7a6402efb5be4dcae0afcf1c6ac8d406ee728cf215a. Artifact is ignored and not a replacement table; full peel regeneration still needs all 2048 subdivisions and/or stronger trial policy.
2026-07-08 dense-count comparison tooling slice: added tables/compare_dense_count_shards.sh to compare one or more dense_count.out shards (or shard dirs, defaulting to tables/results discovery) against current wirehair::GetDenseCount() by compiling a temporary comparator against WirehairTools.cpp. Validation: bash -n; default discovery over the four dense-count shards reproduced total=545 changed=529 lower=506 higher=23 min_delta=-39 max_delta=15 mean_changed_delta=-9.78; explicit shard-dir check for N2..256 produced total=180 changed=168; copied shard under a path containing a space compared correctly; missing path rejects; git diff --check. Bug pass fixed path-with-spaces parsing by using tab-delimited internal records.
2026-07-08 dense-count aggregation tooling slice: added tables/aggregate_dense_count_shards.sh to merge one or more dense_count.out shards (or shard dirs, defaulting to tables/results discovery) into a conservative per-N report with observation count, min/max generated dense count, lowest failure rate observed at the max dense count, and sources contributing the max. Validation: bash -n; default discovery over the four current ignored dense-count shards produced 546 TSV lines (545 data rows + header), sha256=477f53b08d114ab24c73a0d765eab71f9a1f8989e93f303e7e7e3b276421a32c; explicit shard-dir path containing spaces aggregated correctly; missing path rejects; empty/header-only input rejects with no stdout; malformed numeric row rejects with no stdout; git diff --check; final bug-fix pass found no further issues.
2026-07-08 dense-count comparison hardening slice: tightened tables/compare_dense_count_shards.sh to validate dense_count.out rows the same way as the aggregation helper, reject malformed numeric rows instead of silently skipping them, parse into a temporary records file before comparison, and defer header output until at least one valid row exists. Validation: bash -n; default discovery still reports total=545 changed=529 lower=506 higher=23 min_delta=-39 max_delta=15 mean_changed_delta=-9.78 with output sha256=5badbe647592fd58f1929d5afd9485b25b38a9c78c7c02b7865e1a0c538aa551; explicit shard-dir path containing spaces works; malformed input rejects with zero stdout and one row diagnostic; empty/header-only input rejects with zero stdout; git diff --check; final bug-fix pass found no further issues.
