2026-07-10 handoff audit: reviewed origin/codex/precode-handoff-20260710 (90fed75/c7479c6). The mixed 9,788-line commit combined valuable axd solver work with unfinished regeneration infrastructure, would resurrect this closed Bead via passive JSONL, conflicted with nine active codec files, and its full tools configuration registered heavy_matrix_generator_test twice. Per the retirement verdict, no JWG/table paths or Beads JSON were imported; only axd-specific solver work was reconciled manually.
close_reason: Retired after regeneration evidence invalidated the task premise. Operational shards were intentionally ignored and are unavailable in a fresh clone; the harness does not chain candidate tables, its seed generators remain conditioned on shipped tables/exact fixups, and gen_tables validates a stale private dense-point copy. Production has 768 peel and 163 dense exact-N corrections, including 115 peel residue classes with conflicting required seeds that a single shared table entry cannot replace. Four fair dense-count repeats still lacked a validated non-regressing candidate, and no finite statistical acceptance rule exists. Current shipped tables/fixups remain authoritative; any future retuning must be a newly scoped research campaign with durable artifacts, explicit candidate inputs, single-source validation, and held-out non-regression gates.

title: Skip reconstructing already-received systematic source blocks
description: codex P10. If a received ID is an original block and the output path permits direct copy, skip final recompute in Substitute. Large win for systematic-heavy loss patterns; zero for repair-only. Audit intermediate-symbol/output-column mapping carefully.
close_reason: Explored and implemented the remaining per-block fast path. Full-output recovery already used CAT_COPY_FIRST_N to copy received systematic blocks; ReconstructBlock now also directly copies a received original block from input staging instead of recomputing it. Verified with focused recover_block API test, CMake build, harness build, and 35,712-trial fuzz with 0 failures.

title: Shuffle-2 MST row-difference transform
description: codex P8. Build MST over dense rows weighted by pairwise XOR weight; transform child rows by row ^= parent to sparsify dense rows before GE/value-gen. Could cut MultiplyDenseValues(2.5x). Validate dense-stage weight histogram first; medium-high risk.
notes: 2026-06-10 no-go review: production Shuffle-2 dense generation already gives the row-difference optimum. MultiplyDenseRows/MultiplyDenseValues generate one root row of weight ceil(D/2), then every subsequent dense row is derived by exactly two bit flips before it is stored to the shuffled destination row. Since all distinct same-weight binary rows have even Hamming distance and minimum nonzero row XOR weight 2, the existing generation path is already a Hamiltonian path at the MST lower bound 2*(D-1) inside each dense window. Independent simulation over D={12,38,57,114,190,370} found adj_min=adj_max=2; MST weight matched 2*(D-1) for D<=190 samples (D=370 MST skipped as unnecessary after the invariant check). Therefore an MST row-difference transform cannot cut MultiplyDenseValues below the current root + two-flip stream without duplicate-row edge cases, and would add GE/value bookkeeping risk for no material win. No code change.
close_reason: No-go after code-read and dense-row lower-bound validation: Shuffle-2 already emits dense rows in a two-flip path that attains the MST lower bound, so an MST row-difference transform has no useful optimization headroom and would only add bookkeeping risk.

title: Explicit Cauchy heavy-row solve
description: codex P9. 6x18 heavy matrix is Cauchy-like; use explicit/precomputed 6x6 Cauchy inverses instead of generic GF(256) GE. Low payoff (<1% end-to-end, heavy GF work already tiny) but deterministic. Low-medium risk.
notes: 2026-06-11 code audit: not implementing explicit/precomputed Cauchy inverse in production. The fixed 6x18 table is Cauchy-like at initialization, but Triangle() does not solve a pristine fixed 6x6 submatrix: remaining extra binary rows are converted into heavy rows, binary pivots can be chosen through the heavy band, and those pivots mutate the GF heavy rows before the last-pivot solve. A precomputed inverse of kHeavyMatrix would only cover the uncommon pure-heavy tail and would need substantial new bookkeeping to preserve the current row-operation replay used by InitializeColumnValues/AddSubdiagonalValues/BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal. Existing code already limits GF work to <=6x18 and uses CAT_HEAVY_WIN_MULT for binary-to-heavy elimination. Measurements from the 2026-06-10 census showed heavy_muladds/divs are tiny versus sparse/GE block-XOR cost, so expected end-to-end gain is <1% with nontrivial correctness risk. Close as no-go; future heavy work should target H/band/table regeneration only if reliability goals require it.
close_reason: No-go after code audit: fixed Cauchy inverse does not match mutated production heavy-band state, and measured heavy GF cost is negligible.

title: M4RI / PLE Gray-code tables applied to RHS symbols
description: codex P6. Precompute 2^k Gray-code combinations and clear k columns at once -- applied to the symbol RHS (not just matrix bits). Only touches the GE/back-sub (1.3x of traffic) so modest; relevant if dense back-sub windowing misses opportunities. k=50..600.
notes:
Starting bounded M4RI/PLE/windowing experiment. Existing codec already uses RHS window tables in AddSubdiagonalValues and BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal; first check whether larger/adaptive windows are viable.
Implemented gated WH_WINDOW8 prototype. Fuzz passed: 59,808 trials, 0 fails. Count at N=32000 showed slightly higher XOR traffic than default (encoder_create 14.4x -> 14.4x but +0.6%; AddSubdiagonal/BackSub bytes increased). Throughput mixed: hurts N=8192/32000 bb=1300, helps large-footprint bb=8192 cases (e.g. N=32000 bb=8192 decode 726 -> 823 MB/s, recover 5300 -> 6342 MB/s). Keep opt-in, not default.
close_reason: Implemented/measured in optimization experiment pass; kept useful knobs gated where results were mixed.

title: Schur-complement / nested-dissection partitioning of deferred system
description: codex P5. Decompose residual sparse deferred-column graph into components; eliminate each locally, accumulate Schur complement onto mix/heavy separator. Cost sum(c_i^2)+sep^2 vs k^2 -- high win IF graph splits, ~0 if one giant component. PREREQ: measure residual component-size histogram before implementing.
close_reason: Prerequisite residual-component measurement completed under WH_COUNT. Deferred-column graph is already almost fully decomposed (e.g. N=2048: 46 cols/46 components; N=8192: 75/75; N=16000: 123/123; repair-only N=8192: 89 cols/87 comps max=3). Nested-dissection/Schur rewrite is not justified: sparse deferred part is already split and remaining coupling is dense/mix separator; GE/back-sub traffic is small.

title: Cache-aware recovery_blocks layout reorder
description: Substitute/PeelDiagonal read source blocks at scattered column indices across the multi-MB recovery array -> L3/DRAM misses (latency bound). Reorder block storage so peeling-graph-adjacent columns are memory-adjacent (reduce reuse distance). High risk: touches all _recovery_blocks indexing. Pairs with 6e prefetch.
notes: 2026-06-11 code audit: close as no-go for a physical recovery_blocks layout reorder. The workspace is one contiguous logical recovery-block array followed immediately by peel rows/cols/refs; recovery block identity is assumed by direct pointer arithmetic throughout value generation, encode(), ReconstructBlock(), ReconstructOutput(), WH_OPLOG pointer-to-block inversion, and the temporary window-table reuse in AddSubdiagonalValues/BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal. Static search found dozens of direct _recovery_blocks + _block_bytes * logical_index uses across these paths, so introducing a cache-aware physical order would require a new logical-to-physical indirection and inverse mapping in nearly every row-op and API path. That is high correctness risk for an optimization that overlaps with wirehair-2sc row-op tiling and existing WH_PREFETCH work, both of which preserve the logical storage contract. Future locality work should continue via schedule tiling/prefetch and only revisit physical layout if op-log measurements show tiling cannot recover the missed locality.
close_reason: No-go after storage-contract audit: physical recovery_blocks reorder is invasive across solve/encode/reconstruct/logging, while row-op tiling and prefetch address the locality target without remapping block identity.

title: EPIC: Larger algorithm explorations (speculative/research)
description: Discovered via codex GPT-5.5 LA research (bench/results/codex_la_ideas.md), the 8-agent codebase+literature workflow, and the 6a symbol-XOR measurement. Higher-risk/higher-effort directions for the memory-bound codec. Reprioritized: the bottleneck is PEELING+SUBSTITUTION traffic (11.7x of 13x), not the GE (1.3x).
close_reason: Closed broad exploration umbrella: the concrete follow-up work is already split into specific beads (precode replacement/certification/prototype, recovery layout, degree-retune, heavy rows, pivot selection, NT/prefetch, and seed-table regeneration). Keeping this generic epic open duplicates the actionable queue.

title: Degree-distribution retune to cut symbol-XOR traffic (overhead-aware)
description: 6a shows Substitute(5.5x)+PeelDiagonal(3.7x) traffic is set by peel-row weights. Lighter degree distribution -> fewer XORs -> less traffic & faster, but raises overhead/defer_count. Offline search degree table + seeds with objective: decode_failure + lambda*symbol_bytes (codex P7). Versioned/opt-in. Validate overhead, defer_count, symbol-bytes, decode cycles.
notes:
Starting quick degree-distribution/traffic experiment. Use WH_PEELCAP/WH_COUNT as an opt-in prototype, and sweep block_bytes because wall-time/memory pressure scales as N * block_bytes.
Quick WH_PEELCAP prototype result: compiling count+seed knobs and setting WH_PEELCAP=4 or 6 reduced encoder_create symbol XOR traffic at N=32000 from ~14.4x message to ~1.7x/2.1x, but it massively increased deferred columns (207 -> 2438/1625) and broke invertibility. Fuzz with cap produced many encoder_create-null failures. Do not ship cap-only degree retune; any real retune must co-optimize dense/heavy precode and overhead tables.
Handoff: cap-only retune was rejected. Keep issue open only for a real co-optimized distribution+dense/heavy/table search; no direct WH_PEELCAP-style change should ship.
Experiment-only cost-frontier pass completed 2026-06-06. Broad screen: 191 pure random-row structures, methods 10/18/40/115/116/117/118, N=1000 and 6400 complete at 50 trials; N=32000 stopped after low-degree/d1mix/uniform-mix/robust-LT families. Confirmation: 45 frontier/prior-winner structures, same 7 methods, N=1000/6400/32000, overhead 0/1/2, N-jitter=10, 100 paired trials with PDFs. Results saved in experiments/peeling/results/degree_retune_frontier_N1000_6400_32000_j10.csv and SUMMARY.md. Takeaway: cost-optimal random row distribution scales with N and block size: cap16 around N=1000, cap64/d1mix around N=6400, robust-soliton-ish cap128 around N=32000 for 100KiB/1MiB. For 1280-byte pieces scheduler CPU favors rqcc_lowref on low-degree robust LT rows despite larger residuals. This is not a shipping retune; real codec failure-rate, dense/heavy precode, and table validation remain open.
2026-06-08 optimization loop: added cached degree-CDF sampling to codec-side v2 peel evaluator and standalone experiments/peeling harness. This preserves deterministic graph results while cutting weighted-structure build time: lt_m2_c320 build_us 1193.2 -> 381.6 (-68.0%), rs_c001_d50_c128 1143.2 -> 275.6 (-75.9%) on N=1000/t5/ks_bmax_top16. v2 seedtable N=32000,candidates=128,trials=4 stayed byte-identical and improved 121.65s -> 116.83s (-4.0%). Validation: policy test, peel_sweep self-test, ASan/UBSan peel_sweep self-test. Issue remains open for real degree+dense/heavy co-optimization.
2026-06-08 second optimization loop: optimized codec-side v2 peel scorer. CountLiveRowsForColumn for todo columns now uses stored incidence count, and top-K boundary scoring reuses counted live degree-2 endpoints instead of rescanning incident rows. Seedtable N=32000,candidates=128,trials=4,bb=1280 stayed byte-identical and improved 116.83s -> 51.35s after the degree-CDF cache; compared with the original 121.65s baseline, the combined tuning-path speedup is -57.8%. Validation: policy test and broader seedtable smoke over N=1000,6400,32000 and bb=1280,102400.
2026-06-10 peelcost pass: added an opt-in wirehair_v2_bench peelcost mode for degree-distribution retune screening. It evaluates policy or named LT/robust candidates with paired peel matrices and reports a block-XOR proxy combining matrix_xors, precode generation, N*solve_width, GE block-xors, and heavy-row muladd estimate. The mode explicitly does not model LDPC rank/constraint-row effects; precode_sim remains required for reliability validation. Saved smoke CSV: experiments/peeling/results/v2_peelcost_N320_3200_32000_t6.csv. Validation: cmake --build build --target wirehair_v2_bench wirehair_v2_policy_test; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test; peelcost default-path sweep; ldpc precode parser smoke; rqcc_lowref parser smoke; git diff --check. Small t=6 readout under this proxy: N=320 OH0 dense favored lt_m2_c96 (~21.8k block-xors); N=3200 OH0 favored lt_m2_c512 (~443k dense, ~430k ldpcdense); N=32000 favored lt_m2_c1024 (~12.76M dense at OH0). Treat as screening only, not a shipping retune.
2026-06-10 paused after peelcost screening checkpoint. Next degree-retune work should either extend peelcost candidate coverage or validate a shortlisted degree+precode candidate in precode_sim/end-to-end codec trials before any policy change.
2026-06-11 named peelcost expansion checkpoint: regenerated build/codec/wirehair_v2_bench and wrote experiments/peeling/results/v2_peelcost_named_N320_3200_32000_t8.csv. Shape validated: 702 rows = N {320,3200,32000} x block bytes {1280,102400,1048576} x overhead {0,1,2} x 13 named structures x dense/ldpcdense, all 8 paired trials, ks_bmax_top16. Summary appended to experiments/peeling/results/SUMMARY.md. Readout under the block-XOR proxy: small N favors smaller caps (N=320 OH0 lt_m2_c96, OH2 lt_m1_c64), N=3200 shifts to lt_m2_c512/lt_m2_c1024, and N=32000 is dominated by lt_m2_c1024 across block size/overhead/precode cells. Robust low-degree mixtures are not a large-N path in this named-candidate screen. This remains a proxy only; promotion still requires precode_sim rank/failure validation with the selected dense/LDPC-heavy rule.
2026-07-07 triage checkpoint: keep open. Existing degree-retune evidence is still peelcost/proxy-level; it is not a shipping retune. Closeable next slice is bounded precode_sim validation of shortlisted row distributions (wirehair, lt_m2_c1024, rs_c001_d50_c128, lt_m1_c16, lt_m1_c64) against dense/codecport/codecport_n13 over K=10000,32000,64000 and OH0..2, followed by rank_total.py total-block-op accounting and a promote/kill verdict in the experiment summary.
2026-07-08 bounded rowdist validation completed. Generated paired precode_sim screen at seed 0x5eed0006: t500 for K=10000,32000 over rowdists wirehair, lt_m2_c1024, rs_c001_d50_c128, lt_m1_c16, lt_m1_c64 with schemes dense, codecport, codecport_n13 and OH0..2; t100 K=64000 smoke for wirehair and lt_m2_c1024; rank_total report over all generated CSVs. Verdict in experiments/precode/results/SUMMARY.md: do not promote any shortlist rowdist. lt_m1_c16 is 100% failure, lt_m1_c64 is ~97-100% failure, rs_c001_d50_c128 fails 53-67% at K=32000, and lt_m2_c1024 is reliability-viable with H12 codecport but total-block-op higher than production wirehair under codecport at K=32000 and K=64000. Validation: rank_total.py --self-test; git diff --check; generated CSV row-count/header pass.
close_reason: Named degree-distribution retune shortlist validated and rejected; no rowdist promotion. Future degree work needs a new co-optimized family, not the existing controls.

title: 6e: Software-prefetch scattered source blocks in Substitute/PeelDiagonal/MultiplyDenseValues
description: 6a measurement: 13x symbol-XOR amplification dominated by Substitute(5.5x)+PeelDiagonal(3.7x)+MultiplyDenseValues(2.5x); memory-LATENCY bound (IPC<1, scattered block reads across 2.66MB+ recovery array). Iterator column sequence is deterministic (Weyl) -> look ahead 1-2 and _mm_prefetch the next source block to hide L3 latency. No algebra change -> low risk. Measure single-thread create/recover timing (count harness confirms bytes unchanged). THE highest value/risk lever for MTU.

title: Task5: Dense-seed co-optimization for residual hard N (~80)
description: After Task4-style peel fix, ~80 N remain weak (60 severe): catastrophic@loss0.30 even with best peel seed, or no good peel seed (noGood@0.10). These need JOINT (peel_seed,dense_seed[,dense_count]) search. Extend seedsearch to also vary d_seed via the existing OverrideSeeds/WH_DENSE/WH_DSEED knobs. Verify @0.10 AND @0.30 + fuzz.
close_reason: Delivered joint peel+dense correction-table support and root fixup tables. Packaged 605 peel correction rows and 91 dense correction rows; clean CMake and harness builds; focused fuzz 52,064 trials with 0 correctness failures. Remaining high-N reactive work is tracked by wirehair-0yk.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Task5 DELIVERED: joint (peel,dense) search built+validated, fixes peel-UNfixable defects via dense seed. Ran on 294 hard N, cut at 153 ([4935,25211], covers all validated catastrophic). Result: WirehairPeelFixups.inc 601 entries + NEW WirehairDenseFixups.inc 59 entries (GetDenseSeed correction). Build clean, fuzz 0 fails/36k. Spot-check @0.30: N=7533 17.7->0.018, N=11446 8.5->0.016 (FIXED via dense). Gap: GOOD30=0.15 filter accepts mediocre peel fixes (e.g. N=24146 5.4->0.133) instead of the better dense fix -> tighten goodthr + re-run for full quality. Remaining: hard N in [25211,31912] (same method).

title: Task4: Extend peel-seed fix to N=[17500,64000]
description: Same scan->seedsearch->kPeelSeedFixups pipeline that fixed [2048,17500] (weak 306->80, severe 221->60, 0 regress, 0 fuzz fails). Stopped at 17500 only because the large-N seedsearch tail was slow. Run scan [17500,64000] (one campaign, <=96 thr, bb=64) -> seedsearch -> append to WirehairPeelFixups.inc -> re-scan+fuzz verify. Bug-safe (table data only).
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Task4 PARTIAL DELIVERED: extended peel-seed correction table 228 -> 361 entries, range [2373,24384] (was [2373,17444]). Scan [17500,32000] found 629 weak N (4.3%, severe -- N=24405 mean 59, max 8861); seedsearch covered [17500,24350] (226 N, ~41% peel-fixable) before cut for time. New table: build clean, correctness fuzz 0 fails/30976+17312 trials, p99=p999=1. RESIDUAL: (a) N in [24384,32000] not yet searched (incl N=24405, peel-fixable, just past cut), (b) ~59% of large-N defects need dense-seed co-tuning (Task5). Method scales; remaining is compute time + Task5.

title: 6d: RaptorQ-style inactivation replacing greedy deferral
description: Replace greedy column deferral with min-degree/lookahead inactivation -> lower defer_count -> smaller dense solve -> fewer back-sub symbol ops. Codex P2. Medium-high risk; optimize total symbol-rowops not just defer_count. Guard behind flag.
notes: DEPRIORITIZED by 6a: GE/dense-solve is 1.3x of 13x. Inactivation reduces defer_count -> smaller GE, but GE data-traffic already tiny. Reconsider only if it also cuts Substitute/peel traffic.
close_reason: Closed

title: 6b: Cache-tiled substitution (algebra-preserving locality)
description: Tile byte-level row-ops in AddSubdiagonalValues/BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal/Substitute over ~16-32KB stripes so pivot/source blocks stay hot across many dest updates. No algebra change -> low correctness risk. Targets 22% L1-miss bottleneck; helps mainly large block_bytes. Codex P4.
close_reason: Closed

title: 6c: Markowitz/min-fill pivoting in deferred GE
description: Replace first-row pivot in Triangle with min-fill (row_nnz-1)*(col_nnz-1) selection over deferred+dense GF(2) matrix -> fewer triangular nonzeros -> fewer symbol row-ops in back-sub. Needs column-permutation plumbing through _ge_col_map. Schedule-only, wire-compatible. Codex P1. Medium-high risk -> guard behind flag, verify overhead+correctness unchanged.
notes: DEPRIORITIZED by 6a measurement: GE data-traffic (AddSubdiag+BackSub) is only 1.3x of the 13x; Markowitz pivoting can at best shave that. Lower ROI than 6e (prefetch) / degree-dist.
close_reason: Closed

title: 6a: Instrumentation counters for symbol-XOR bytes per stage + GE stats
description: PREREQ. Macro-gated (WH_COUNT) counters in WirehairCodec for: bytes XORed per stage (PeelDiagonal/MultiplyDenseValues/InitializeColumnValues/AddSubdiagonalValues/BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal/Substitute), gf256_add/add2/addset/muladd call+byte counts, defer_count/dense_count/pivot_count, GE matrix nonzeros. Expose a getter; harness prints per-encode_create/decode/recover. Lets us measure the bottleneck and validate 6b-6d. Low risk.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: catid
comments.text: 6a DONE. Per-stage symbol-XOR (N=2048 bb=1300, encoder_create=13.0x msg): Substitute 5.5x, PeelDiagonal 3.7x, MultiplyDenseValues 2.5x, BackSub 0.7x, AddSubdiag 0.6x, Init 0.1x; Triangle/MultiplyDenseRows=0 (GE bit-matrix only). KEY: GE data-traffic is just 1.3x of 13x -> codex's Markowitz(6c)/inactivation(6d) have LIMITED impact. Real bottleneck is PEELING+SUBSTITUTION (11.7x). Levers: access-locality (prefetch/tile the scattered block XORs in Substitute/PeelDiagonal/MultiplyDenseValues) + degree distribution. GFmul=0.4% (GFNI irrelevant, reconfirmed).

title: EPIC: Task6 reduce symbol-XOR memory traffic (the real speed lever)
description: Codec is memory-bound; the only speed lever is fewer/local symbol XORs, not faster GF (S1/S2 proved ~0 end-to-end). Sub-tasks 6a-6d below. Validate every change with 6a counters + correctness fuzz; bench only after a saturating campaign frees the CPU.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Task6 finding: create/decode is memory-bound at ~13x symbol-XOR amplification (Substitute 5.5x + PeelDiagonal 3.7x + MultiplyDenseValues 2.5x dominate; GE only 1.3x). Memory MICRO-opts all NULL: prefetch (cross-row in Substitute), huge-pages (madvise), LTO -- all ~0% (Zen5 OoO+prefetchers+L3 already handle the scattered access near-optimally; bound by data misses not TLB/codegen). => The ONLY real speed lever is REDUCING the 13x: degree-distribution retune (lighter peel rows = fewer XORs, trades overhead) or a different code structure -- both need full offline table regeneration (wirehair-31h / degree-dist exploration). Honest conclusion: wirehair is near its memory-bound structural limit; no free speed wins. Instrumentation (6a) + null results are the deliverable; 6b/6c/6d/6e gated-off, documented.

title: Future: reduce symbol-XOR traffic in the solve (memory-bound lever)
description: codex gpt-5.5 LA research + our profiling agree: codec is memory-bound; speed lever is fewer/local symbol XORs, not faster GF. Top candidates (compat-preserving, schedule-only): (1) Markowitz/min-fill pivoting in Triangle GE -> 15-35% less dense-solve/back-sub symbol traffic; (2) RaptorQ-style inactivation replacing greedy deferral -> lower defer_count -> superlinear dense reduction; (3) cache-tiled RHS execution in AddSubdiagonalValues/BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal/Substitute -> 5-20% miss-traffic. Higher risk: Schur-complement separator, M4RI-on-symbols, degree-dist retune. Full notes: bench/results/codex_la_ideas.md. High value, high risk on intricate codec -> prototype behind flags w/ counters first.
notes: 2026-06-10 calibration (cold 4GiB pool): block XOR costs 0.157us @1280B (15.2 GiB/s), 5.66us @100KiB (33.7), 55.7us @1MiB (35.1). The old xor_calibration 1280B figure (0.0226us) was cache-resident and ~7x optimistic in the fully-cold limit; N-dependent footprint interpolation needed for honest conversions. muladd:xor = 1.83/1.10/1.00 across the three sizes — GF(256) heavy ops are nearly free at >=100KiB.
close_reason: Closed parent research bucket: all listed child ablations are complete, calibration findings are recorded, and remaining concrete work is tracked separately by active/specific issues (row-op tiling in wirehair-2sc, degree/precode retune in wirehair-vbk/wirehair-edj, recovery layout in wirehair-g4c, and heavy-row work in wirehair-wuw).

title: S4: Multi-destination GFNI muladd at heavy-solve / back-sub call sites
description: When one source block feeds several dest rows with different coeffs, load src once, compute several affine products. Targets heavy-row elimination & back-substitution. (codex P2)
notes: 2026-06-10 decision: close multi-destination GFNI muladd prototype as no-go for current codec priorities. Existing WH_COUNT at N=32000 shows encoder_create bb=1300 does ~597 MB XOR traffic but only ~0.14 MB GFmul/muladd traffic; decode(feed) is ~629 MB XOR vs ~0.14 MB GF, and recover has no GFmul. bench/results/codex_la_prompt.md/FINDINGS.md already recorded GF(256) multiply <2%, GE bit-matrix <1%, and 2-2.8x GFNI kernel speedups giving ~0 end-to-end gain. New muladd calibration makes GF(256) muladd near XOR cost at large blocks (muladd:xor 1.10 at 100 KiB, 1.00 at 1 MiB), but precode heavy work remains tiny: K32000 dense OH0 heavy_muladds ~2802 block ops versus sparse ~459k and backsub ~4.0M block XORs. Even H=12 variants would not move the bottleneck. Do not prototype call-site batching unless a future WH_OPLOG capture shows a real muladd-heavy stage after a major algorithm change.
close_reason: Closed as no-go: multi-destination GFNI targets a negligible GFmul/muladd slice; prioritize symbol-XOR reduction/locality.

title: G1: SIMD-vectorize GE bit-matrix row XOR loops
description: Replace scalar uint64 ^= loops in PeelDiagonal/MultiplyDenseRows/TriangleNonHeavy/Triangle with AVX2/AVX-512 row-tail XOR. Pad _ge_pitch alignment, audit tail masking. Speeds encoder_create/decode for large N. (codex P5)

comments.author: catid
comments.text: CLOSED-WONTDO: profiling N=16000 shows GE bit-matrix ops (MultiplyDenseRows 0.68%, Triangle <0.6%) are negligible; 56% of time is data-block XOR (memory-bound). SIMD-ing GE bit XOR can't move end-to-end. Confirms memory-bound conclusion [[wirehair-memory-bound]].

title: S3: GFNI muladd multiple accumulators / deeper unroll
description: After S1, unroll GFNI muladd to 4x ZMM with independent accumulators to hide affine+xor latency and saturate ports.
notes:
Starting GFNI deeper-unroll/multi-accumulator experiment. Must measure end-to-end because previous count data shows GF multiply traffic is tiny versus XOR/memory traffic.
Implemented gated WH_GFNI_UNROLL4 prototype for gf256_mul_mem and gf256_muladd_mem. Micro selfcheck passed. Micro: mul_mem improved at 1024/16384/1MiB, muladd mixed. Fuzz passed: 57,472 trials, 0 fails. End-to-end GFNI matrix mixed: some create/decode improvements (e.g. N=2048 bb=1300 decode 2653->2777 MB/s, N=32000 bb=8192 decode 737->756), but recover regressed in several cells. Keep opt-in, not default.
close_reason: Implemented/measured in optimization experiment pass; kept useful knobs gated where results were mixed.

title: Ignore generated Python bytecode caches
description: Repository Python tests create untracked __pycache__ directories and .pyc files because .gitignore has no Python bytecode rules, leaving recurring generated noise and making safe staging harder.
acceptance_criteria: Add standard repository-wide ignores for __pycache__ directories and Python bytecode files, prove tracked Python sources remain visible, remove only generated cache directories, and verify a Python test run recreates caches without changing git status.
notes: Added repository-wide __pycache__/ and *.py[cod] ignores. Removed only generated cache directories, reran CI runner tests (11/11), confirmed recreated bytecode is ignored while ci/run_ci.py remains unignored, and verified no cache paths appear in git status.
close_reason: Python bytecode artifacts are ignored and verified without hiding sources

title: Report logical work and estimated memory traffic separately in experiment tools
description:
## Problem
XOR calibration and row-op replay label normalized byte counts as bandwidth even when actual read/write traffic differs by operation, making absolute GiB/s comparisons across tools misleading.

## Evidence
experiments/peeling/xor_bench.cpp:516-518,630-643,847-850 counts dst^=src as 2*bytes though it reads src+dst and writes dst; fanin uses k+1 and omits destination reads. experiments/tiling/rowop_tiling.cpp:834-837 hardcodes three streams for mixed real operations even though zero/memcpy/div/add2 have different traffic. Summaries label these normalized values simply GiB/s.

## Scope
Define a per-operation byte ledger and emit clearly named logical-work bytes plus estimated read/write traffic, documenting write-allocate/cache assumptions. Preserve timing/checksum/speedup semantics and version persisted schemas.
acceptance_criteria: Unit-test byte accounting for zero, memcpy, xor/add, add2, mul/div, muladd, fanin K, and mixed trace aggregation against hand-calculated ledgers. End to end, run a small synthetic calibration and replay a persisted real trace while preserving operation schedules/checksums and deriving old normalized plus new logical/traffic metrics from the same timing samples. Compare timing/speedup behavior statistically rather than requiring byte-identical noisy measurements, and require accounting columns to agree across peeling and tiling tools. Update summaries/schema readers, validate legacy-file handling, and complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Added shared experiments/ByteLedger.h with hand-checked logical/read/write ledgers for zero, memcpy, XOR/add, addset, add2, mul, div, muladd, fused fan-in K, chained fan-in K, and mixed aggregation. xor_bench and rowop_tiling emit schema v2 while preserving legacy normalized columns; v2 separates logical destination work, estimated reads/writes, and total estimated traffic. Assumptions explicitly exclude RFO/write-allocate, cache-line rounding, prefetch, and cache effects. Added strict legacy/v2 validator and unit tests; all six persisted v1 calibration/replay artifacts validate. End-to-end script exercises default/cold/muladd/fanin, rejects mixed output schemas, replays synthetic XOR and persisted real trace, checks cross-tool identities and unchanged checksum 0x8c08d158d46a7cbe; 3-sample median speed ratio 0.574 vs historical 0.597 (no noisy-time equality). GCC -Werror and ASan/UBSan calibration/trace replay pass. Multiple bug passes fixed low-precision v2 totals and invalid multi-header mode combinations; final pass found no further defect.
close_reason: Logical work and estimated read/write traffic are separately versioned, documented, validated, and verified across both experiment tools.

title: Remove disproven perfect-heavy-matrix guarantees from generator docs
description:
## Problem
Several table generators still claim the shipped heavy matrix is perfectly invertible under binary perturbation, a guarantee disproven after fixing the generator's elimination check. These comments can drive invalid reliability assumptions.

## Evidence
tables/GenerateDenseCount.cpp:35-43, GeneratePeelSeeds.cpp:35-43, GenerateMostDenseSeeds.cpp:48-56, GenerateSmallDenseSeeds.cpp:48-56, and HeavyRowGenerator.h:31-74 repeat perfect/invariant claims. HeavyRowGenerator.cpp:161-170 and project memory establish an empirical roughly 1/256 singular floor and a shipped-byte reproduction gate.

## Scope
Consolidate one authoritative explanation of the shipped matrix, empirical perturbation behavior, and what the generator does/does not certify; link all callers to it and remove contradictory copies.
acceptance_criteria: Add a source/documentation grep/lint test that rejects the known false guarantee phrases outside an explicitly historical explanation. Review every heavy-matrix comment and relevant README/SUMMARY for consistent empirical language. End to end, run gen_tables --no-benchmarks --heavy-trials with a fixed deterministic seed/trial fixture and assert its recorded count exactly, or validate the observed rate against a documented statistical interval that permits zero for small samples. Shipped-byte reproduction must still pass, and output must describe the perturbation rate as empirical rather than a guarantee. Complete repeated review passes.
notes: Added tables/HEAVY_MATRIX.md as the authoritative wire-format and empirical reliability contract; replaced false guarantee copies in all four seed/count generators, legacy codec commentary, precode README/SUMMARY, header docs, and benchmark prompt. Generator output now labels perturbation results empirical/informational. Added repository documentation lint for known false formulations and required references, plus deterministic gen_tables regression gate. Verified docs lint; gen_tables --no-benchmarks --heavy-trials 4096 twice byte-identically; shipped seed 2318331135281 reproduced exact bytes; exact empirical singular count 14/4096 (expected 16); no generator failures. Final repository grep and repeated review found no remaining known false guarantee phrase.
close_reason: Heavy-matrix documentation now consistently states the empirical limit, with lint and deterministic shipped-byte/count gates.

title: Add reliability-gated and Pareto precode ranking
description:
## Motivation
rank_total.py sorts only modeled operation cost, so unreliable low-degree schemes appear as winners and users must manually apply the reliability caveat documented in experiments/precode/results/SUMMARY.md.

## Evidence
rank_total.py:266-295 orders total ops only. SUMMARY.md:314-327 explicitly warns that cost-only ranking selects unreliable schemes and applies an external reliability gate.

## Scope
Add explicit reliability controls such as maximum fail rate/confidence bound, minimum trials, modeled versus alternative heavy-row count H selection, and cost-vs-failure Pareto output. When reliability is evaluated at H different from the cost model, recompute cost for that H or label the modeled-H assumption explicitly. Never label a row a winner when it fails the selected gate; record assumptions in output.
acceptance_criteria: Unit-test synthetic fronts with dominated/nondominated rows, cost/reliability ties, zero failures with small samples, confidence-bound edge cases, fail@H' alternatives, cost recomputation or explicit modeled-H holding, insufficient trials, and no feasible scheme. End to end, reproduce the tracked degree-rowdist conclusion automatically from repository data, compare selected/filtered schemes with the documented decision, and generate deterministic machine-readable plus human reports. Validate against an independent confidence/Pareto oracle and complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Implemented reliability-gated and Pareto ranking in experiments/precode/rank_total.py. Defaults require >=100 trials, observed failure <=0.005, and a 95% one-sided Wilson upper bound <=0.05. Reliability H is selectable as modeled or 0..128; cost defaults to the complete modeled-H ledger, while --cost-h reliability recomputes H*inact_mu+H^2 using modeled inact and explicitly labels the modeled base assumption. Reports include rejected reasons, no-feasible state, cost/failure Pareto front, reliability-tiebroken winners, alternative-H failure counts, modeled-H crossovers, deterministic wirehair.precode-ranking.v1 JSON, and human output. Unit/property tests cover independent Wilson and brute-force Pareto oracles, dominated/nondominated fronts, exact ties, reliability-dominated cost ties, zero failures/small samples, insufficient trials, alternate H, both cost modes, runaway rows, and no feasible scheme. Repository E2E automatically reproduces the rowdist conclusion: at K32000/OH0/bb1280 production codecport_n13 is 5,059,750.2 ops vs retuned codecport 5,101,302.1 (41,551.9 higher); at K64000 production is 16,290,996.0 vs retuned 16,371,540.4 (80,544.4 higher). Fresh seeded precode_sim -> parser -> calibration -> TXT/JSON E2E passed. Three manual bug-review passes completed; final pass found no remaining issue.
close_reason: Acceptance criteria implemented and verified by unit, property, oracle, tracked-corpus, and fresh simulator end-to-end tests.

title: Provide complete context-managed Python Encoder and Decoder wrappers
description:
## Motivation
The installed Python file is an import-time ctypes example rather than a safe binding: callers manually manage raw pointers and it does not expose result strings, recover_block, decoder_becomes_encoder, structured errors, or deterministic ownership.

## Evidence
python/whirehair.py declares only part of include/wirehair/wirehair.h and exposes a global CDLL plus _run_example. The library is loaded during import and all lifecycle management is branch-local.

## Scope
Add testable Encoder/Decoder wrappers with context-manager/finalizer ownership, typed result handling, complete public C API coverage, injectable loading, bytes-like validation, and clear borrowed/owned source lifetime behavior. Coordinate installation/package discovery with wirehair-9vb.
acceptance_criteria: Unit-test every wrapper method with a fake CDLL, including pointer-width prototypes, result-string conversion, exactly-once close/finalization, double close, exceptions, recover_block capacity, decoder conversion, buffer lifetime, and import without an available library where supported. Build/install static/shared artifacts to a custom prefix and run a real Python end-to-end loss/reorder round trip, per-block recovery, decoder-to-encoder retransmission, and negative cases on Linux and Windows CI. Preserve the simple script example on top of wrappers and complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes:
Follow-up ownership review now retains native cleanup responsibility until decoder-to-encoder replacement wrapper construction succeeds; injected wrapper-construction failure frees exactly once. Installed discovery now searches standard prefix/bin for Windows DLLs, with a platform-mocked unit. Wrapper suite is 11/11; strict Clang installed shared native Python E2E covers structured creation, capacity, pre-completion, oversized-input and closed-source negatives plus loss/reorder/recovery/conversion success. Awaiting pushed Windows lane only.
Final ownership review replaced per-wrapper cleanup guessing with one idempotent _NativeCodecOwner shared through construction and decoder conversion. The 14/14 wrapper suite now covers pre/post-base-init, close-then-raise, detached/replaced-finalizer and __new__ failures plus native conversion failure and normal lifecycle; exact fake-native free counts remain one. The complete Python aggregate is 60/60, and a fresh installed Clang shared module passed ci/python_native_test.py against the installed native library. Follow-up wirehair-jqo is locally complete; this parent feature still awaits pushed Windows native E2E.
After the native export CI tests landed, the final repository Python aggregate is 66/66; the wrapper-specific suite remains 14/14. Hosted Windows shared native E2E is the only remaining xoc gate.
Hosted shared MSVC job 86278561264 in run 29066341405 passed wrapper units 14/14 and the installed Windows native Python E2E for exact SHA 337cc0d73e79f6b1ad64e0e4dfa7449250e33c90. The lane installed bin/wirehair.dll plus whirehair.py, validated the exact 15-symbol DLL ABI, passed strict package consumers 3/3, then completed structured negatives, owned input, loss/reorder, full/per-block recovery, decoder conversion, retransmission, and closed-source lifecycle coverage (bytes=4099, blocks=37, delivered=37). Run: https://github.com/catid/wirehair/actions/runs/29066341405.
close_reason: Implemented, independently reviewed, locally verified, and accepted by the all-green 9-job hosted run 29066341405 at exact SHA 337cc0d73e79f6b1ad64e0e4dfa7449250e33c90, including native MSVC static/shared end-to-end coverage.

title: Index received originals for near-O(1) recover_block lookup
description:
## Problem
Every direct-original recover_block call scans all received rows to find its staging position, making recovery of many individually requested blocks roughly O(N squared).

## Evidence
WirehairCodec.cpp:4107-4131 linearly scans row IDs on each call. Recovering all 64,000 directly received one-byte blocks measured 444 ms versus 122 microseconds for full wirehair_recover. This is a follow-on to the closed direct-copy optimization, not a duplicate.

## Scope
Maintain or lazily build an original-ID-to-staging-row index with bounded memory, correct sentinel handling, and reset/rebuild behavior across decoder reuse, failed decode, and decoder-to-encoder conversion.
acceptance_criteria: Add unit tests for ordered/out-of-order originals, duplicates per API policy, mixed repair rows, missing originals requiring regeneration, partial final blocks, terminal failure, reuse, and conversion. Add a benchmark at N=64,000 recovering all blocks individually with a gate demonstrating near-linear total lookup work and material improvement over the 444 ms baseline without regressing full recover. End to end, reassemble a lossy/reordered message exclusively through recover_block and byte-compare it under ASan/UBSan, then complete repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Added bounded uint16 original-id to staging-row index in Codec workspace, maintained across initial/extra/reused rows and reset on decoder reuse/conversion. Tests cover reverse ordered N=64000 originals, mixed repair/lost originals, duplicates, partial final, terminal failure, reuse, conversion, and exclusive recover_block reassembly. Three full N=64000 recover_block passes complete below millisecond timer resolution versus 444 ms prior baseline; ASan/UBSan pass.
close_reason: Implemented, covered by focused unit/E2E tests and strict installed consumers, and verified in Release plus ASan/UBSan after repeated bug-review passes.

title: Keep offline generators and benchmarks out of default builds
description:
## Problem
BUILD_TESTS currently adds several offline search/generation programs and a benchmark to the default all target even though they are not CTest tests, inflating routine developer and CI builds.

## Evidence
CMakeLists.txt:163-182 adds gen_small_dseeds, gen_peel_seeds, gen_most_dseeds, gen_dcounts, and gen_tables normally; codec/CMakeLists.txt adds wirehair_v2_bench normally. The clean audit build compiled all six, while CTest registered only unit and V2 correctness executables.

## Scope
Separate correctness tests from offline generators/benchmarks with EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL or explicit BUILD_TOOLS/BUILD_BENCHMARKS options while retaining named targets and documented reproducible invocation.
acceptance_criteria: Add build-system tests proving a default library+tests build omits offline executables, explicit tool targets/options still build, and BUILD_TESTS=OFF does not pull test-only support code. Smoke-run each explicitly built generator with its bounded deterministic arguments and run the V2 benchmark on a small case. Record clean-build wall time/object count before and after, run all CTests and an end-to-end codec round trip, then perform repeated bug-review passes.
notes: Generators are always named explicit targets but EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL unless WIREHAIR_BUILD_TOOLS=ON; V2 benchmark similarly uses WIREHAIR_BUILD_BENCHMARKS. BUILD_TESTS=OFF does not build unit/test support. Automated build_policy_e2e proves default omission, explicitly builds all 5 generators+benchmark, and smoke-runs selection selftest, bounded one-trial peel/most-dense/dense-count, table generator with no benchmarks/heavy trials, and one-trial V2 compare. Old default: 6.19s/34 objects/5 generators+benchmark; new (despite added correctness tests): 3.60s/25 objects/none, same host. Full E2Es pass.
close_reason: Implemented, independently reviewed, and verified by strict GCC/Clang/MinGW/AArch64 builds, full CTest/sanitizer suites, installed consumer/plugin E2Es, build-policy regressions, and repeated clean bug-review pass.

title: Sweep remaining weak-not-in-table overhead Ns
description: After wirehair-0yk exhausted the severe and severe-not-in-table [32000,64000] reactive fixup campaign, 999 entries remain in bench/results/weak_not_in_table_32000_64000_t80_2026-06-11.txt after excluding installed rows and all 2026-07-07/2026-07-08 seedsearch/gate artifacts. This task is the lower-priority broad weak-list campaign: decide whether to process them with cheaper triage, split by severity/mean, or leave them to the full offline table regeneration path in wirehair-jwg. Acceptance: remaining weak list is either swept with the same override/gate discipline or explicitly retired/superseded with documented evidence and a committed bead note.
notes: 2026-07-08 triage/retire decision: bench/results/weak_not_in_table_32000_64000_t80_2026-06-11.txt is a broad residual list, 1342 unique N in [32020,64000] (346 in 32000-39999, 485 in 40000-49999, 511 in 50000-64000). The July severe top5 artifacts cover the severe/gated campaign; a quick file-pattern count covers 263 of the raw weak list and still leaves ~1079 raw weak Ns, consistent with the bead's 999-after-all-exclusions order of magnitude. Current generated fixup tables are exact-N lookups and top out at N=31968 for peel and N=27984 for dense in bench/results/Wirehair{Peel,Dense}Fixups.inc, while the residual weak list is mostly above that range. Starting another broad reactive sweep would duplicate wirehair-jwg's full table-regeneration goal and generate a large exact-N patch set without a root-cause cleanup. Decision: do not process this P3 weak list reactively; supersede it into wirehair-jwg full offline regeneration.

title: Harden experiment result pipelines against silent failures
description: Third review round (2026-07-02) found results-integrity gaps in the experiment infrastructure. Tracked-script bugs (verify_fix.sh build gate, gen_fixups.sh count, drive_seedsearch.sh wait race, invalid 0xF1XED seed literal) are fixed, but the following remain: (1) .gitignore ignores experiments/precode/results/*.err unconditionally, so a crashed precode_sim run's diagnostics never appear in git status while its truncated CSV does - a partial result can be committed with no error record; consider un-ignoring non-empty .err or asserting emptiness in a preflight. (2) experiments/precode/run_bigk_queue_2026-06-10.sh truncates tracked result CSVs BEFORE running each stage (missing binary destroys committed data), writes unconditional OK/done markers even when every stage failed, and never rebuilds/freshness-checks the git-ignored precode_sim binary; future queue scripts should write to temp and mv on success, gate markers on exit codes, and rebuild first. (3) bench/results/*.sh drive scripts that generated the committed fixup tables and FINDINGS.md numbers are git-ignored (gitignore line ~73), so the provenance pipelines exist only on this machine; also the global *.log pattern hides any log repo-wide. One historical untracked script (bench/results/drive_ext.sh) filtered fuzz output to successful-trials-only lines, making failed fuzz runs indistinguishable from clean ones in ext.log - audit conclusions drawn from ext.log if they ever matter again.
notes: 2026-07-03 audit fixed part of the queue-script failure handling: stale markers are cleared and done markers are gated on /tmp/queue_fail, and focused parser/script failures were hardened in whx/precode_sim/peel_sweep. Remaining scope: result pipelines should still write CSVs to temp files and move on success, rebuild/freshness-check ignored experiment binaries before long runs, and decide how to surface ignored logs/scripts/err files so partial failed runs cannot look clean.
close_reason: Hardened result pipelines: queue script rebuilds precode_sim, writes CSVs through temp files, preserves failure stderr beside results, gates markers on failures, added precode result integrity preflight, and surfaced bench provenance scripts/logs plus precode err files through .gitignore.

title: Re-scoped: k-ary gather-XOR kernels pay only at <=100KiB blocks (E6 verdict)
description: Quiet-window fan-in microbenchmark (experiments/peeling/results/fanin_gather.csv, 4GiB cold pool, taskset, 32GiB target, 5-repeat median): fused add8 vs chained-pairwise logical throughput ratio = 2.22x @1280B, 1.34x @100KiB, 1.13x @1MiB. The session plan's E6 kill-gate (add8 >= 1.15x at 1MiB) FAILED, so the production gather-kernel change (gf256_add4/add8_mem + Substitute/Encode source gathering) was killed for the 1MiB regime. However the 100KiB bar (>=1.25x) passes with margin, and MTU blocks gain 2.2x. If pursued, target bb<=128KiB only, gate at runtime by block_bytes, and re-run the composed cell with tiling (wirehair-2sc) before claiming additive gains. Muladd:xor ratios from the same window (muladd_calibration.csv): 1.83 @1280B, 1.10 @100KiB, 1.00 @1MiB.
close_reason: Implemented and validated an opt-in -DWH_GATHER=1 prototype only. Added gf256_add_multi_mem with self-test coverage and fused recovery-source XORs in Substitute/Encode behind a block_bytes <= 128KiB gate; default builds remain on the old path. Validation: default and WH_GATHER builds, targeted repros incl partial final block and 1MiB fallback, gather fuzz 1376 trials 0 fails plus ASan/UBSan repro/fuzz 288 trials 0 fails, default CTest 5/5, WH_GATHER CMake unit_test, WH_GATHER+WH_OPLOG replay verify, count comparisons showing 128KiB enabled and 131073/1MiB identical to base. Timing smoke was mixed, so this is not promoted to default production.

title: Fix gf256 unaligned uint64 scalar bulk/tail accesses (strict-alignment targets)
description: CONFIRMED low severity. The mobile non-NEON bulk paths and every function's 8/4-byte tail in gf256.cpp cast raw uint8_t pointers to uint64_t*/uint32_t* and dereference (e.g. gf256_add_mem ~785-795, add2_mem ~970-982, addset_mem ~1132-1144, memswap ~1727-1740, tails at ~914-925, 1057-1070, 1236-1249, 1400, 1429, 1667, 1696, 1837-1854; line numbers pre-date June 2026 fixes, re-grep for '(uint64_t \*)' casts). Wirehair passes base + i*block_bytes with arbitrary block_bytes, so pointers are routinely misaligned: UB under strict aliasing and real faults on strict-alignment targets (ARMv5/v6 non-NEON — exactly the configs that take these paths). Fix: use memcpy-based loads/stores (compilers emit identical code on x86/aarch64) or byte loops on GF256_TARGET_MOBILE. Keep x86 SIMD paths untouched. Validate: gf256 self-test + whx micro on x86, cross-compile -fsanitize=alignment spot check.
close_reason: Replaced scalar gf256 uint64_t/uint32_t dereferences in mobile non-NEON bulk loops and all 8/4-byte tails with memcpy-based unaligned load/store helpers, leaving x86 SIMD loadu/storeu paths untouched. Also annotated intentional byte-tail switch fallthroughs so rebuilding gf256.cpp is warning-clean. Validation: cmake --build build --target wirehair wirehair_v2_policy_test; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test; bash bench/build.sh; ./bench/whx micro --threads 1 --secs 1; ./bench/whx fuzz --threads 1 --secs 3 --nmax 512 --seed 0xA11A6E; temporary -DGF256_TARGET_MOBILE -fsanitize=alignment,undefined harness over misaligned gf256 add/add2/addset/mul/muladd/memswap calls; git diff --check; rg confirmed no remaining uint64_t*/uint32_t* scalar casts in gf256.cpp/h.

title: P5: Schur-complement separator experiment
description: Evaluate the Schur-complement / mix-heavy separator idea from the solver next-step list. First measure residual graph component sizes and separator coupling after compression; only prototype local-component elimination if histograms show useful separation. Validation: keep wire format unchanged, compare dense/deferred component histograms, symbol-rowop estimates, correctness fuzz, and wall time across block sizes 1280 bytes, 100 KiB, and 1 MiB before considering any production change.
notes: 2026-06-10 decision: close Schur-complement separator experiment as measured no-go based on existing WH_COUNT component instrumentation recorded in bench/results/FINDINGS.md section 11. The first requested measurement is already done: deferred residual graph samples are almost fully split before the mix/dense separator (N=2048 systematic 46 deferred cols / 46 components; N=8192 75/75; N=16000 123/123; repair-only N=8192 89 cols / 87 components, max component 3). That means there is no large sparse residual component for nested-dissection/local elimination to exploit. Remaining coupling is the dense/mix separator, and GE/backsub symbol traffic is a small fraction of total traffic compared with PeelDiagonal/Substitute. Do not prototype a high-risk Schur rewrite unless future instrumentation shows large connected residual components after a major row/precode change.
close_reason: Closed as no-go: component census shows the sparse residual is already decomposed; separator rewrite has little target.

title: O2: Heavy row count/width sweep (kHeavyRows/kHeavyCols)
description: Sweep kHeavyRows in {4,5,6,8} and heavy cols around 18. GFNI lowers GF256 cost so larger heavy tail may cut overhead. Changes code matrix => versioned. Measure mean & p99/p999 overhead, NeedMore rate, decode cycles. (codex P8)
notes:
Starting compile-time heavy matrix sweep support. Add WH_HEAVY_ROWS/WH_HEAVY_COLS overrides so variants can be measured without editing source constants.
Added WH_HEAVY_ROWS/WH_HEAVY_COLS compile-time overrides for safe subset sweeps (rows 1..6, cols rows..18); kHeavyMatrix remains the generated 6x18 table. Fuzz results: WH_HEAVY_ROWS=4 failed (4004 failures/73440 trials), WH_HEAVY_ROWS=5 failed (3730 failures/71424 trials), default 6x18 passed (68576 trials, 0 fails). Conclusion: current seed/dense/heavy tables are coupled to 6 rows; fewer rows are invalid without table regeneration, and >6 requires regenerating kHeavyMatrix and all seeds.
close_reason: Added sweep support and measured 4/5-row variants invalid without regeneration; default unchanged.

title: G4: Block-data row-op tiling for large block_bytes
description: Log block-data row-op schedule during solve, replay in 32-256KiB tiles to keep pivot/dest slices hot. Wins for large block_bytes, neutral/negative for MTU. (codex P4)
notes:
Started 2026-06-06 with a standalone row-op tiling benchmark under experiments/tiling to avoid mixing with existing dirty codec changes. The harness verifies that replaying the full row-op schedule independently for each byte tile matches untiled output, then benchmarks synthetic dst^=src schedules with source fanout 8 at block sizes 1280, 100 KiB, and 1 MiB. Results saved in experiments/tiling/results/rowop_tiling_1280_100k_1m.csv and SUMMARY.md. Best observed speedups: 1280-byte symbols 0.994x (neutral/slightly negative), 100 KiB 1.285x at 16 KiB tiles, 1 MiB 2.090x at 16 KiB tiles. Normal verify and ASan/UBSan verify passed. Keep issue open: this does not yet log/replay actual AddSubdiagonalValues/BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal/Substitute schedules from the codec, and production final-block padding still needs validation.

2026-06-10 WH_OPLOG phase: added opt-in codec schedule capture behind -DWH_OPLOG=1 and WH_OPLOG_PATH. It logs InitializeColumnValues, MultiplyDenseValues, AddSubdiagonalValues, BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal, and Substitute as CSV tuples with op_type zero/memcpy/xor/addset/add2/muladd/div, dst/src block kinds, offsets, scalar, and bytes; offsets cover partial final-block padding. Documented build/run/schema in experiments/tiling/README.md. Validation: normal WirehairCodec.cpp object compile clean; WH_OPLOG object compile clean; traced bench/whx build with EXTRA='-DWH_OPLOG=1 -DWH_COUNT' recovered correctly for count --N 64 --bb 128 --loss 0.10 and produced a 1929-row CSV with all expected stages/op types and no invalid real block indices; normal WH_COUNT whx smoke also recovered correctly. Keep issue open for Phase 2 schedule-file loader/replay in experiments/tiling/rowop_tiling.cpp and actual large-block quiet-machine measurements.

2026-06-10 replay-loader phase: extended experiments/tiling/rowop_tiling.cpp with --schedule WH_OPLOG CSV loading, --block-bytes, --tiles, and --repeats. The loader replays zero/memcpy/xor/addset/add2/muladd/div using gf256.cpp, validates source kinds and block/offset bounds, infers block_bytes from max op bytes when omitted, and verifies tiled byte-range replay matches untiled output before timing. Build script now links gf256.cpp; README documents capture plus replay. Validation: bash experiments/tiling/build.sh; ./experiments/tiling/rowop_tiling --verify-only; ./experiments/tiling/rowop_tiling --schedule /tmp/wh_oplog_smoke.csv --tiles 16,32,64 --verify-only; smoke timing showed identical checksum 0x2bd2d1cf8f3ab488 for untiled/tiled; ASan/UBSan build with -march=native passed both verify modes. Keep issue open for quiet-machine captures at N/block_bytes matrix (especially 100KiB/1MiB) and decision-quality throughput numbers.
close_reason: Completed actual WH_OPLOG capture/replay phase: captured full-block N=128 schedules at 1280/102400/1048576 bytes plus a partial-final-block repro at N=64, saved schedules and aggregate timing CSV under experiments/tiling/results, verified persisted schedules normal and ASan/UBSan. Real schedule readout: MTU tiling loses (0.71x), 100KiB modestly helps (1.23x full, 1.11x partial), 1MiB strongly helps (1.90x). Production use should be block-size gated and measured end-to-end.

title: G5: Heavy-row muladd mul==1 fast path / reduce GF256 op frequency
description: Detect identity multipliers in Triangle/BackSubstitute heavy elimination and use XOR path; measure fraction of identity entries.
notes:
Starting small heavy-row identity-multiplier fast-path experiment: replace gf256_muladd_mem(..., code_value=1, ...) with gf256_add_mem at heavy-row call sites where safe.
Implemented payload heavy-row code_value==1 fast paths in AddSubdiagonalValues and BackSubstituteAboveDiagonal, replacing gf256_muladd_mem with gf256_add_mem. Fuzz passed: 57,984 trials, 0 fails. WH_COUNT at N=32000 bb=64/1300 is effectively unchanged because GFmul traffic is already tiny (~0.01/0.14 MB vs hundreds MB XOR). End-to-end movement is mixed/noise; keep direct path as a small safe cleanup, not a major speed win.
close_reason: Implemented safe identity-multiplier fast path and measured neutral/mixed impact.

title: O1: Dense count re-optimization (speed-weighted objective)
description: Re-run GenerateDenseCount with objective mean_extra + lambda*decode_cycles, p999 constraint. Try D +/- 4 around current. Millions of trials at table knots + interpolation gaps. (codex P9)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: catid
comments.text: O1 measured: dense_count is a WEAK speed lever -- N=4096 reducing 74->50 (-32%) cuts create XOR amplification only 14.1x->13.8x (~2%), and raises overhead. MultiplyDenseValues is only 2.5x of 14x. The dominant 9.2x (Substitute 5.5x + PeelDiagonal 3.7x) is the peeling, set by the degree distribution -> needs full offline table regen (tradeoff). Closing O1 as low-value.

title: G2: M4RI / four-Russians for dense binary GE
description: Process k=4..8 pivot cols at once; build 2^k XOR-combination table, eliminate each row's k-bit pattern with one table XOR. Gate on _pivot_count/_ge_pitch thresholds. Expect 2-4x GE for large deferred systems. (codex P7)
notes: 2026-06-10 decision: close the bit-matrix-only M4RI/four-Russians GE task as no-go. Existing findings show GE bit-matrix ops (MultiplyDenseRows/Triangle) are <1% of runtime while symbol XOR traffic dominates. Current precode numbers reinforce the ceiling: at K32000 dense OH0 ge_block_xors_mu is ~106k versus backsub_xors_mu ~4.0M (2.7%); the promising ldpcdense_s152_d38 point is ~59k GE block-xors vs ~4.0M backsub (1.5%). A 2-4x speedup of only the bit-matrix GE would not move end-to-end time, and the precode replacement is already reducing GE width structurally. If M4RI/PLE is revisited, it should be a separate RHS-symbol table/schedule experiment that reduces data-block operations, not this bit-matrix-only task.
close_reason: Closed as no-go: dense bit-matrix GE is too small a runtime/cost slice; structural precode and symbol-schedule work dominate.

title: G3: vpopcntdq-guided pivot selection (min tail weight)
description: Bounded-window pivot search choosing row with min remaining tail popcount via VPOPCNTDQ to reduce fill-in XORs and downstream heavy ops. (codex P6)
notes: 2026-06-11 simulator pass: added replay-only --ge-pivot-window N to experiments/precode/precode_sim and documented it. It scans the next N remaining GE replay rows and chooses the candidate pivot with the lowest remaining tail popcount, falling back to the first later candidate if the window has none. Validation: bash experiments/precode/build.sh; ./experiments/precode/precode_sim --self-test; generated experiments/precode/results/ht0_ge_pivot_window.csv over K={1000,3200,10000}, schemes={dense,ldpc}, OH=0, 300 paired trials, windows {0,16,64,256}. Result: fill_in_mu drops 80-98%, but ge_real_bitops_mu and ge_real_rowops_mu stay flat or drift slightly upward (-0.1% to +0.3% in the screen). Since the replayed GE work does not improve, do not port this pivot policy to production on current evidence; revisit only if a future RHS/value-schedule metric shows block-XOR savings.
close_reason: No-go after replay experiment: bounded min-tail pivot windows collapse fill-in but do not reduce measured GE word-XORs or row eliminations; production change is not justified.

title: S5: Nontemporal stores + prefetch thresholds (mul_mem/addset_mem/output)
description: NT stores only where dest fully overwritten & not reread soon; prefetch large streams. Size-thresholded. (codex P12)
notes: 2026-06-11 audit: no production NT-store/prefetch patch. gf256_addset_mem/gf256_mul_mem are generic APIs: mul_mem explicitly supports in-place operation, and addset/output reconstruction call sites usually reread the destination immediately through subsequent add/add2 folds. Window-table and solve paths also reuse generated blocks soon. A safe NT-store path would need a separate call-site-specific API for aligned, full-cacheline, no-immediate-reuse writes; the available codec output sites do not provide that contract. Software prefetch was already measured in bench/results/FINDINGS.md as ~0% on the dominant Substitute stream, and the codec already has a narrow T0 prefetch near the peel path. Closing this implementation-level speed idea as not-worth-it; structural traffic reduction remains the active speed lever.
close_reason: No-go after call-site audit: NT stores are not safe for the generic GF APIs or current output folds because destinations are often reread immediately; software prefetch was already measured as ~0% on the dominant stream.

title: O4: Seed search upgrade (peel/dense, sequential-ID aware)
description: Expand seed search; optimize for sequential repair IDs as well as random. Better per-N/bucket seeds. A/B with equal Monte Carlo budget. (codex P11)
close_reason: Delivered reactive seed-search tooling and default-on peel/dense fixup path. Current validated result: [2048,32000] fuzz 0 failures, mean overhead near documented floor with known rare-tail/high-N residuals split to wirehair-0yk; long-term full table regeneration remains wirehair-jwg.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: FOUND concrete table defect: N=3061 default peel seed gives mean overhead 0.575 (p999=63, max=492) vs typical 0.02. Almost ANY other p_seed fixes it (pseed=5 -> 0.0155), a ~30x reduction. Peel-seed siblings (N%2048==1013: 1013,5109,7157,9205,11253) are fine, so it's a specific (pseed,dense_count,dseed) interaction at N=3061. Scanning full N range for all such weak spots, then exception-table fix.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: RESUMED post-reboot under safe limits (<=96 threads, single campaign, bb=64 low-bandwidth). Fixed a harness bug: scan/seedsearch/repro ignored --threads (dispatched before arg parse) -> ran at default ~102; stacking two = ~204 threads, which contributed to the hardware reset (marginal DIMM P0MH0). Added hard thread cap (hw-16, max 104). Validated seedsearch: weak N fixed (2962:1.55->0.02, 3430:0.77->0.02), good N unchanged (no overfit). Running full scan [2048,24000] thresh 0.10 to enumerate severe weak N, then seedsearch -> per-N peel-seed correction table in GetPeelSeed.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Scan [2048,24000] complete: 581 weak-spot N (~2.6% of range), 301 with mean overhead >1.0, 426 >0.3 (vs documented 0.02). Defect confirmed real & block-size-independent (N=3430: 0.527 at bb=64 == 0.547 at bb=1300). Nature: bimodal -- most decodes fine (p999<=1) but ~0.1-0.5% of erasure patterns catastrophic (up to 816 extra blocks). Each is a peel-seed defect: any non-default seed fixes it (verified at loss 0.10 AND 0.30). Full parallel seedsearch campaign running to build kPeelSeedFixups[] table. Harness bug fixed mid-stream: driver wait-loop pgrep matched my own status commands (string 'whx.base scan' in echo) -> never fired; now running seedsearch directly.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: FINAL overhead fix (peel 574 + dense 62 corrections, [2373,25787]): HEADLINE before/after fuzz [2048,32000] same seed: mean overhead 0.0893->0.0340 (-62%), max 2907->258 (-89%), 0 correctness fails. Refinement (joint peel+dense, goodthr=0.04, best-per-N generator) cut post-Task5 fuzz mean 0.077->0.025, max 1633->134. Residual: unfixed [25787,32000] hard N + 1 stubborn marginal (N=24146, best seeds have rare tails). Tables default-on, bug-safe (GetPeelSeed+GetDenseSeed binary-searched corrections).

comments.author: catid
comments.text: FINAL (extended to [2373,28033]): 606 peel + 92 dense corrections. Before/after fuzz [2048,32000] same seed (0 fails both): mean overhead 0.0893->0.0230 (-74%), max 2907->125 (-96%). Near the documented ~0.02. Default-on, bug-safe (GetPeelSeed+GetDenseSeed binary-searched). Remaining: [28033,32000] partial (cut for time) + [32000,64000] (same scan->joint->regen method). Pipeline: bench/whx scan+seedsearch[joint dense]+gen_fixups2.

title: O3: Peel degree distribution retune (weight-1 prob, soliton)
description: Optimize sparse degree distribution for lower deferred count + overhead, not just overhead. Adaptive P(weight=1) by N. Requires seed retune. (codex P10)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: catid
comments.text: O3/task6 QUANTIFIED the degree-distribution speed lever via a WH_PEELCAP knob + 6a counters. Capping peel-row weight at ~26-30 cuts create XOR amplification 14.1x->13.0x (~8%) and gives MEASURED ~5% faster create AND decode wall-time at N=4096, for +0.005 overhead (0.016->0.021, untuned seeds). BUT the naive global cap BREAKS encoder invertibility for some (N>=~7000, seed) combos (fuzz cap=30: 6999/267k encoder_create=null) -- conflicts with wirehair's seed-tuned invertibility guarantee. A safe cap (no failures to N=64000) is high (~56, ~2% speed). => Real ~5-8% speed lever but REQUIRES full offline table regeneration (re-tune all seeds/dense-counts/heavy for the capped distribution). This is THE concrete speed path; tracked as wirehair-31h (full regen). Knob gated behind WH_SEED_KNOBS, off by default.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: DEFINITIVE: peel-cap speed lever is NOT viable. Feasibility test (seedsearch under cap=30): large N (16000,32000) have NO working peel seed (best=1e9, encoder fails for all seeds); N=7301/10000 best stays 4.3/10.5. Lighter peeling overwhelms the fixed dense+heavy precode -> must GROW precode to invert -> adds back the traffic the cap saved. Degree distribution is deeply coupled to precode; the 13x is near-optimal. NO table-level speed win exists; a faster codec needs a different construction. Closing speed exploration.
